news.Title: Tesla sales rebound in Europe after massive slump
news.Content:
There’s a bright spot in Tesla’s ( TSLA ) core auto sales, with a slight reversal of fortune in greater Europe.

According to the European Automobile Manufacturers' Association (ACEA), Tesla electric vehicle registrations (a proxy for sales) in Europe rose to 17,664 units in February, an 11.8% gain compared to a year ago. In January, sales dipped 17%, the last month of a losing streak that had been ongoing since December 2024.

While this February saw a sales jump, it came against a weak February in 2025. Last year, Tesla’s European sales tumbled 27%.

Tesla’s recent losing streak came against the backdrop of rising EV sales in the greater Europe region. Total EV registrations in the region, which includes the UK and the European Free Trade Association, rose 15.8% in February, with overall registrations regardless of powertrain up only 1.7%.

More from Yahoo Scout

What drove Tesla's February recovery in European sales?

What challenges does Tesla face in Europe?

Why are Chinese EVs gaining market share?

How is BYD competing with Tesla in Europe?

Tesla's main Chinese competitor, BYD ( BYDDY ), which sells a mix of pure EVs and hybrids, reported another strong sales month, with February totals jumping 162% to 17,954 units sold in Europe.

BYD’s sales in Europe have soared every month since ACEA began including the company in its data last summer.

While EV sales have slumped somewhat in the US, cheap EVs and hybrids are flourishing in the EU, especially from Chinese automakers like BYD and Li Auto, as buyers across the pond embrace Asian imports.

As for Tesla, CEO Elon Musk’s deep unpopularity in Europe doesn’t help the company’s image, and Tesla’s lack of new products, such as a cheaper EV, is also hurting sales, leaving it with no competition against inexpensive Chinese imports.

In the US, domestic automakers like Tesla have been insulated from Chinese competition, at least thus far. Automakers like Ford are reportedly discussing ways to build Chinese vehicles in the US via joint ventures. Stellantis is already working with China’s Leapmotor on co-developed vehicles as well.

Tesla, which just killed off its more expensive Model S and Model X EVs, is betting on a future of full self-driving, robotaxis, and Optimus robots to fuel growth. The company’s overall global sales dropped 9% last year after slipping 1% in 2024.

Pras Subramanian is Lead Auto Reporter for Yahoo Finance. You can follow him on X and on Instagram .

news.Title: Meta Just Signed a $27 Billion Artificial Intelligence (AI) Deal. Here's the Under-the-Radar Stock That Won.
news.Content:
Over the last few years, Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) has quietly solidified its position as a leader in the artificial intelligence (AI) realm through a combination of open-source models and integrating generative AI across its social media platforms.

On one side of the equation, Meta is democratizing AI for developers and enterprises through its Llama model series. On the other side, the company is embedding machine learning into core advertising tools across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp -- enhancing user engagement and improving returns for advertising partners.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

This dual-approach is fueling Meta's aggressive AI infrastructure spending, which is projected to reach up to $135 billion this year. Without heavy investment, the company risks falling behind its big tech counterparts in the AI boom.

Let's explore Meta's recent headline-grabbing $27 billion capacity deal. Smart investors should know why this move is justified for Meta, and on a deeper level, understand the data center stock benefiting from such agreements behind the scenes.

Why did Meta just spend $27 billion on artificial intelligence?

In mid-March, Meta announced a five-year capacity deal with neocloud specialist Nebius Group (NASDAQ: NBIS) .

The deal commits Meta to $12 billion of dedicated capacity across multiple data center sites powered by large-scale deployment of Nvidia 's Vera Rubin graphics processing unit (GPU) architecture. In addition, Meta has the option to purchase additional capacity featuring Nebius' upcoming GPU clusters for another $15 billion.

Partnering with Nebius brings significant advantages to Meta. Namely, Meta now has immediate access to scarce GPUs all while avoiding the full capital and time burdens of building every data center design internally.

Working with Nebius allows Meta to diversify its supply chains, navigate around power and chip shortages, and accelerate timelines for training new models and bringing next-generation services into production.

How Nebius is winning in the neocloud era

Neoclouds are specialized AI infrastructure providers that build their own data centers and rent access to high-performance GPUs to developers. In an age where data center power and capacity are becoming as hot a commodity as oil, Nebius has swiftly become a critical supplier to some of AI's most important players.

Story Continues

The company's rise hails from a trifecta of hyperscaler partnerships that provide Nebius with both validation and operational leverage. The new Meta deal joins an existing multi-year, $19.4 billion agreement with Microsoft as well as a $2 billion capital infusion from Nvidia.

These relationships underscore Nebius' strength in building vertically integrated data centers across the U.S., Europe, and the Middle East. The company's ability to rapidly deploy the most in-demand AI accelerators in the world allows big tech to move full steam ahead as these developers push the frontiers of ever-growing inference workloads.

Investors should care about these partnerships because Nebius' ties to blue-chip AI stocks ultimately creates a virtuous cycle of hyperscale demand, funding, and priority within chip stacks. In essence, Nebius is far more sophisticated than a company leasing GPU capacity. Rather, the company is evolving into an indispensable partner to the very companies redefining the future of technology.

Is Nebius stock a buy?

As of this writing (March 20), Nebius boasts a market capitalization of $28.7 billion. Let's take a look at the company's valuation profile relative to its run-rate annualized recurring revenue (ARR).

Prior to the Meta deal, Nebius' management was guiding for annualized ARR between $7 billion and $9 billion for 2026. This represents 540% growth year over year at the midpoint ARR figure.

Clearly, Meta's new $27 billion contract now renders management's guidance conservative. The new commitment should materially lift the company's revenue trajectory beginning in 2027 -- when the Meta deal is expected to come online.

At today's market value, Nebius trades for roughly 3.6 times its forecasted ARR. However, this figure is actually even lower when accounting for the uplift of future deals such as the Meta contract.

For a hypergrowth AI infrastructure stock with contracted, multi-year revenue visibility from big tech, I think Nebius trades at an attractive valuation. The biggest risks I see with the company are its ability to remain on track constructing new data centers and allocating capital efficiently, as AI infrastructure is largely a capital-intensive industry.

With that said, I would not encourage waiting for a pullback on any potential "bad news." Investors with a long time horizon should consider dollar-cost averaging into Nebius stock over time. This approach allows investors to enjoy upside from the secular tailwinds of accelerating AI infrastructure investment while simultaneously smoothing out any volatility the neocloud space may witness.

In today's era where AI compute remains undersupplied, Nebius' platform and blue-chip relationships make it a compelling buy-and-hold opportunity for growth investors.

news.Title: Should You Buy Rivian Stock Right Now?
news.Content:
Not long ago, I named Rivian (NASDAQ: RIVN) my top growth stock to buy in 2026 . My thesis was two-fold.

First, Rivian is about to begin deliveries of its R2 SUV. As I'll highlight below, this should prove a historic milestone for the company's growth journey. Second, Rivian's AI exposure is undervalued. After the recent market correction , growth and value investors alike should consider Rivian shares.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

1. Don't underestimate Rivian's R2 launch

Tesla 's (NASDAQ: TSLA) current market cap of $1.2 trillion is now largely driven by speculative business ventures like robotics manufacturing and robotaxis. But what really put Tesla on the map financially was the launch of its first two affordable models: the Model 3 and Model Y.

To put into perspective how important these two models were for Tesla's growth, consider that out of 418,227 Tesla vehicles delivered to customers last year, 406,585 units were either a Model 3 or a Model Y. The rest of Tesla's lineup accounted for just 11,642 units sold.

Right now, Rivian has just two models on the market: the R1T and R1S. Both can easily cost upward of $100,000 when all options, fees, and taxes are included. That price point effectively prices out the vast majority of consumers. Its R2 SUV, however, will have a starting price of just $45,000. That's below the key $50,000 threshold that nearly 70% of prospective car buyers in the U.S. want to stay below, according to a recent study.

Following the R2 launch will be two additional Rivian models priced under $50,000: the R3 and R3X. Will these vehicles help scale Rivian's sales as much as the Model 3 and Model Y helped Tesla? That remains to be seen. But Tesla already set a precedent for Rivian's growth potential, at a time when electric vehicles (EVs) accounted for a far smaller percentage of U.S. auto sales than today.

2. Rivian's bets on AI are already paying off

After the latest market sell-off, Rivian shares trade at just 3.2 times sales. That's far below what most artificial intelligence (AI) stocks trade at. And it's reasonable for the market to lag in valuing Rivian as a bona fide AI business. Last December, the company held its first "AI Day," in which it outlined a three-pronged strategy that should guide the company over the coming years.

Story Continues

First, Rivian wants to add more AI into its factory design and production process, improving throughput times and reducing costs. Second, the company wants to expand AI in its in-vehicle entertainment system. Third, Rivian wants to aggressively accelerate how AI guides its approach to self-driving software, with the potential for Rivian to produce its own AI chips down the line.

Last week, we saw the first fruits of these efforts, with Uber Technologies (NYSE: UBER) committing to invest up to $1.25 billion in Rivian in exchange for up to 50,000 Rivian R2 SUVs, which will be used to power that company's robotaxi efforts.

The Uber deal is conditional on Rivian meeting certain autonomy milestones. Whether or not Rivian will hit those milestones remains unclear. But what is clear is that other companies are willing to bet more than $1 billion on Rivian's AI efforts. That should get investors excited.

news.Title: What's Going On With Alibaba Stock Tuesday
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Alibaba Group ( NYSE:BABA ) shares rose about 3% in Hong Kong on Tuesday after the company broadened its in-house chip push with a new processor aimed at agentic AI and inference workloads.

Its Damo Academy introduced the XuanTie C950, a RISC-V-based central processing unit designed for cloud computing. RISC-V is an open-source chip architecture that competes with designs from Arm Holdings ( NASDAQ:ARM ) and Intel ( NASDAQ:INTC ).

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 5 Warning Signs with BABA.

Is BABA fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Alibaba said the C950 can be tuned for specific inference tasks, allowing customers to adjust performance for different uses. Chinese media reported that the chip runs at 3.2 GHz and is more than three times faster than the prior XuanTie C920.

The launch adds to Alibaba's semiconductor efforts through its T-Head unit as it tries to build more of its own artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company is competing with Nvidia (NVDA) and Huawei Technologies in China's AI race, while CEO Eddie Wu said last week that Alibaba's proprietary AI accelerators have already moved into mass production.

news.Title: Should You Buy Apple Stock Now or Wait for a Dip?
news.Content:
Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) has been off to a poor start to 2026 as its shares are down 7% thus far. But that's not a big drop off for one of the most valuable companies in the world. Its market cap remains fairly high at around $3.7 trillion. The company's robust business model, strong brand, and devoted user base have made the stock a hot buy for years.

Today, it's trading at 32 times its trailing earnings, which may be a bit rich for a company that normally doesn't generate double-digit growth. Are you better off waiting for more of a dip in its share price before buying Apple stock, or is it still worth adding it to your portfolio today?

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

The stock is priced at a high premium

Apple's valuation has been elevated for multiple years. And not only is it high when compared to the average S&P 500 stock, which trades at 24 times its trailing earnings, but also compared to what investors have normally paid for Apple's stock in the past. Over the past decade, it has averaged a price-to-earnings multiple of 25.

The stock experienced a boom amid the early stages of the pandemic back in 2020 when investors were bullish on many growth stocks and speculative investments, and it has largely remained at elevated levels since then. This is even as the company's artificial intelligence (AI) strategy has come under fire, as Apple has been slow to roll out iPhones with cutting-edge AI capabilities. Its Siri assistant will likely get an upgrade this year, but whether that will lead to a surge in revenue is by no means a certainty.

Apple's stock still looks too expensive to buy

When Apple last reported earnings in January, it delivered a surprise to investors, as its top line rose by 16% due to strong iPhone demand, with CEO Tim Cook admitting that the quarter even eclipsed management's expectations. However, that's not a typical performance for Apple, whose growth rate is typically in the single digits. And unless it can prove otherwise, and that AI will be a huge growth catalyst for the business, I'd wait for a pullback before considering the stock.

Apple may be a quality business, but that doesn't mean its stock is a buy at any price. Its inflated valuation could make it difficult for investors who buy today to earn a good return. There are better-priced growth stocks out there to choose from than Apple, which is why, at its current valuation, I'd pass on the stock.

Story Continues

news.Title: OpenAI's Biggest Backer May Also Be A Risk
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Microsoft ( NASDAQ:MSFT ) has been OpenAI's biggest supporter for years, but now it's also being flagged as one of the company's biggest risks as IPO talk starts to build.

In a document shared with investors that reads a lot like a prospectus, OpenAI pointed out just how dependent it still is on Microsoft, especially when it comes to funding and computing power. The relationship runs deep. Microsoft has poured about $13 billion into OpenAI since 2019 and owns a 27% stake that was recently valued around $135 billion. On top of that, OpenAI's systems are closely tied to Microsoft's Azure platform, making the partnership hard to unwind quickly.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 1 Warning Sign with MSFT.

Is MSFT fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

At the same time, OpenAI is clearly trying to branch out. It recently raised $110 billion from partners like Amazon, Nvidia ( NASDAQ:NVDA ) and SoftBank ( SFTBY ) , and is looking to bring in another $10 billion from a broader group of investors by the end of March. Still, the company made it clear that if anything changes with Microsoft, whether it's the partnership being scaled back or reworked, it could have a meaningful impact on its business.

View Comments

news.Title: Amazon in Focus After Iran's Drone Hit Bahrain Effects AWS Services
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Amazon ( NASDAQ:AMZN ) said its cloud unit's Bahrain region was hit by drone activity tied to the wider U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran, disrupting services and forcing customers to shift workloads elsewhere, according to a Monday Reuters report.

The company is helping users move to other AWS regions while it works to restore normal operations, a spokesperson said. Amazon did not give details on the scope of the damage or how long the outage may last.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 2 Warning Sign with AMZN.

Is AMZN fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The incident is the second to affect AWS's Bahrain operations in March. Earlier this month, Amazon said facilities in Bahrain and the United Arab Emirates experienced power outages, prompting another round of recovery work.

AWS runs a global network of data centers used by companies to store data and run applications. Interruptions in one region can trigger migration efforts to keep services online and reduce downtime.

The Bahrain disruption comes as regional tensions continue to create risks for infrastructure across the Middle East, where cloud providers and other technology groups have faced renewed operational pressure.

news.Title: Zoox to widen US robotaxi footprint with San Francisco, Vegas expansion
news.Content:
March 24 (Reuters) - Zoox, Amazon's ( AMZN ) self-driving unit, said on Tuesday it will expand its robotaxi ‌service in San Francisco and Las Vegas, ‌and begin testing its purpose-built robotaxis in Austin and Miami, marking ​its broadest push yet into the U.S. autonomous ride-hailing market.

Alphabet's Waymo leads the robotaxi market, while Tesla has the financial resources and manufacturing scale to ‌expand its limited service ⁠in Austin, Texas, and plans to ramp up rapidly across the United States ⁠this year.

More from Yahoo Scout

What operational milestones has Zoox achieved so far?

What competitive advantages does Waymo maintain over Zoox?

How will Tesla's expansion plans affect the robotaxi market?

How is Zoox expanding its robotaxi service footprint?

Meanwhile, Zoox plans to significantly grow its footprint in San Francisco, focusing on dense, high-demand neighborhoods ​such as ​the Marina, Chinatown and ​the Embarcadero, while expanding ‌in Las Vegas to cover more hotels and entertainment destinations along the Strip.

The company will also begin operating its purpose-built robotaxis on public roads in Austin and Miami, starting with rides for employees, ‌their families and friends before ​gradually opening to the public ​later this year.

Zoox ​said it has logged nearly 2 million ‌autonomous miles and carried more ​than 350,000 ​riders, as it introduces new features aimed at reducing wait times and improving ride experience ​in a crowded ‌and fast-evolving sector.

(Reporting by Akash Sriram in ​Bengaluru and Abhirup Roy in San Francisco; Editing ​by Krishna Chandra Eluri)

news.Title: Microsoft's Azure Story May Have Hidden Upside
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Microsoft (MSFT) might have more upside in its cloud business than investors are giving it credit for, at least according to Evercore's latest take on Azure.

The firm believes Azure's AI revenue could reach about $25.7 billion in 2026, ahead of its earlier $21.8 billion estimate, assuming capacity buildouts and chip supply stay on track. If that plays out, total Azure growth could come in around 41% for the year, roughly 4.3 points above expectations, with non-AI Azure still growing at about 16%. The base business is already strong, with Microsoft reporting 15% overall revenue growth last quarter and Azure expanding 38%.

Is MSF fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

That said, the near-term setup is a bit messy. Evercore pointed out that Azure hasn't clearly re-accelerated yet, and rising capex along with ongoing questions around Copilot are keeping investors on the sidelines. A more meaningful pickup in Azure growth likely won't show up until the second half of 2026, mainly due to capacity constraints. On the product side, Microsoft is pushing forward with Copilot Wave 3 and the new Microsoft 365 E7 bundle, which is seeing gradual adoption across enterprises.

news.Title: Rubrik announces new integration with Microsoft Defender at RSAC 2026
news.Content:
Rubrik ( RBRK ) announced a new integration with Microsoft ( MSFT ) Defender at RSAC 2026. The integration connects Microsoft’s real-time identity threat detection with Rubrik’s automated identity rollback and recovery capabilities, helping organizations respond faster to identity-based attacks. “Detection is only half of the battle,” said Anneka Gupta, Chief Product Officer at Rubrik. “Organizations need the ability to quickly and surgically reverse malicious identity changes and completely restore their infrastructure. By combining Microsoft Defender’s threat detection with Rubrik Identity Resilience, we give security and IAM teams the power to move from a detected compromise to a trusted, recovered state in hours, instead of days.”

news.Title: CrowdStrike unveils Falcon Next-Gen support for Microsoft Defender
news.Content:
CrowdStrike ( CRWD ) announced that Falcon Next-Gen SIEM now ingests and correlates Microsoft ( MSFT ) Defender for Endpoint telemetry. “Strategic alignment and disciplined execution between industry leaders is what drives meaningful innovation and stronger security outcomes for customers,” said Daniel Bernard, chief business officer at CrowdStrike. “Our integration with Microsoft accelerates legacy SIEM transformation without the operational burden of deploying additional sensors. By advancing our open, data-agnostic architecture, we are giving organizations the flexibility, performance, and data economics to modernize security operations across any technology stack – meeting customers where they are to unlock the protection outcomes and value from Falcon.”

news.Title: 張忠謀"大掌櫃"何麗梅轉戰宏碁當獨董 張淑芬:近期吃過飯.祝福
news.Content:
財經中心／葉為襄、胡崇恩 台北報導

台積電慈善基金會今天（24）舉行記者會，董事長張淑芬親自到場主持分享，張淑芬近期屢遭詐騙集團冒名，她呼籲大家要小心，而被媒體問到先生張忠謀身體狀況，她說很健康。最近傳出張忠謀倚重的台積電前大掌櫃何麗梅將在四月退休，未來要轉戰宏碁當獨立董事，張淑芬說給予祝福。

緩緩走進會場，成為現場焦點，台積電慈善基金會董座張淑芬穿著一襲亮眼淺藍色套裝，親自到場主持，近來屢被詐騙集團冒名，她本人呼籲大眾要小心。難得現身的張淑芬神采奕奕，貼心提醒小心防詐，但外界更關心她的先生台積電創辦人張忠謀的健康狀況。

張忠謀「大掌櫃」何麗梅轉戰宏碁當獨董 張淑芬：近期吃過飯、祝福。（圖／民視財經網）

透露輝達執行長黃仁勳和張忠謀的好交情，近期張忠謀重用的前大掌櫃財務長、何麗梅，4月將自服務27年的台積電退休，轉戰宏碁獨立董事。

82歲的張淑芬分享去年聚集2萬3053名熱情志工，落地執行870場溫暖行動甚至飛到馬來西亞檳城。(圖/台積電慈善基金會)

證交所的最新公告，何麗梅已獲提名是宏碁新一屆獨董候選人，預計5月29號股東會上，將正式通過，張淑芬也透露何麗梅和張忠謀的好交情。82歲的張淑芬分享去年聚集2萬3053名熱情志工，落地執行870場溫暖行動甚至飛到馬來西亞檳城，喊話未來將繼續在有台積電的地方做公益。

原文出處： 張忠謀「大掌櫃」何麗梅轉戰宏碁當獨董 張淑芬：近期吃過飯、祝福

news.Title: 外資賣超121億元連4賣　重砍力積電逾8萬張居首
news.Content:
（中央社記者吳家豪台北2026年03月24日電）台股今天開高走低，終場下跌110.26點，收32612.24點，外資及陸資賣超新台幣121.87億元，連續4個交易日賣超，以賣超力積電8.08萬張最多，並減碼面板股群創4.24萬張、友達2.51萬張，另外也續砍元大台灣50逾2.18萬張。

台股今天早盤受中東衝突暫緩激勵，反彈至33361.46點、大漲638點，但賣壓出籠，大盤失守33000點，記憶體與載板族群走弱，加權指數尾盤翻黑收32612.24點，下跌110.26點，跌幅0.34%，成交值降至6643.52億元。

三大法人今天合計賣超200.77億元，其中，自營商賣超104.84億元，連4賣，投信買超25.94億元，連3買，外資及陸資賣超121.87億元，連4賣。

根據證交所資料，外資今天賣超個股集中在晶圓代工、面板及權值股，前10名依序為力積電 (6770) 、群創 (3481) 、友達 (2409) 、元大台灣50 (0050) 、元大滬深300正2（00637L）、華邦電 (2344) 、期街口布蘭特正2（00715L）、台聚 (1304) 、群益台灣精選高息（00919）、國泰金 (2882) 。

外資同步加碼記憶體、航運及塑化股，今天買超前10名個股依序為南亞科 (2408) 、大聯大 (3702) 、新興 (2605) 、南亞 (1303) 、旺宏 (2337) 、第一金 (2892) 、長榮航 (2618) 、兆豐金 (2886) 、元大金 (2885) 、瑞軒 (2489) 。

投信買超族群集中在金融股，前10名依序為台新新光金 (2887) 、中信金 (2891) 、國泰金、富邦金 (2881) 、文曄 (3036) 、瑞軒、創見 (2451) 、聯電 (2303) 、致茂 (2360) 、宏碁 (2353) 。

news.Title: 「TACO交易」尋求避險！台股ETF受益人不減反增 00961、00922躍升資金避風港
news.Content: 美伊衝突等地緣政治風險持續牽動全球金融市場，在川普政府針對中東局勢的發言與政策頻繁轉折下，市場陷入「TACO 交易」的保守氛圍 。儘管美股前一日受美伊暫停軍事行動的消息激勵而強彈，但台股今 (24) 日表現卻開高走低，盤中一度衝上 33361.46 點後賣壓出籠，終場下跌 110.26 點，收在 32612.24 點 。在震盪格局中，資金明顯轉向台股 ETF 尋求避險，其中 FT 臺灣永續高息（00961-TW）與國泰台灣領袖 50（00922-TW）受益人數月增率雙雙突破 30%，成為近期投資人抗震的首選標的 。 近期市場受美伊戰事消息面牽動，雖然川普宣布暫停對伊朗能源基礎設施進行軍事打擊五天，暫緩市場的避險情緒，支撐美股道瓊指數週一狂漲 631 點，標普 500 與那斯達克指數也同步創下逾一個半月來最大單日漲幅 。市場人士分析指出，由於政策訊號反覆且地緣政治風險並未完全根除，投資人對於方向判斷趨於保守，導致台股在創下盤中高點後，資金撤出意願轉強，成交值達 6643.52 億元，維持明顯的高檔震盪態勢 。 在市場波動加劇之際，資金並未全面撤離股市，而是轉向以 ETF 作為主要的承接工具。觀察近一個月台股 ETF 受益人數變化，買盤主要集中在市值型、高股息以及特定的科技主題型產品 。 根據最新數據統計，受益人數月增率前十名呈現資金高度集中的趨勢：FT 臺灣永續高息（00961-TW）：月增率 32.47% 奪冠 ；國泰台灣領袖 50（00922-TW）：月增率 31.14% 緊隨其後 ；富邦摩台（0057-TW） 與 保德信市值動能 50（009803-TW）：分別增長 27.07% 與 20.65%；富邦科技（0052-TW）、元大電子（0053-TW）、元大台灣 50（0050-TW）：月增率也維持在 14% 至 17% 之間 。 值得注意的是，這波買盤並非盲目追逐，而是展現出「避風港」的特質。若對比大盤近一個月下跌 3.44% 的表現，多檔熱門 ETF 如 0057 跌 2.81%、009803 跌 1.94%、0053 跌 2.2% 以及 00961 跌 3.04%，其跌幅均小於大盤 。這顯示投資人更偏好兼具抗跌防禦性與中長線成長題材的標的 。 市場法人進一步分析，台股目前基本面與籌碼面仍有支撐，企業獲利與整體電子產業趨勢並未轉弱 。儘管短線受到戰事、國際政策與消息面的干擾，造成盤勢波動劇烈，但在基本面穩固的前提下，資金透過 ETF 進場布局，反映出市場對台股中長期後市仍具信心 。 表：近一個月台股 ETF 與受益人數月增率 股票名稱 收盤價 (元) 近一個月漲幅 (%) 受益人數月增率 00961 FT 臺灣永續高息 9.89 -3.04 32.47%

news.Title: 美伊心理戰!台股洗三溫暖高低震盪近千點 終場收跌110點
news.Content:
財經中心／唐詩晴、賴文軒 台北報導

美伊戰爭疑雲籠罩，兩國心理戰讓消息滿天飛，也讓台股上沖下洗，大玩雲霄飛車，從開高報復性反彈600多點，隨後卻跳水收黑，高低震盪近千點，終場跌110點，收在32,612點，台積電收平盤，但記憶體族群慘遭血洗，金融股相對抗跌，扮演撐盤要角。

川普宣布延後5天打擊伊朗能源設施，被伊朗洗臉，是在玩心理戰，美伊戰火之上，兩方消息滿天飛，也讓國際股市和台股，如同川劇變臉，一變再變。

民視記者唐詩晴：「週二台股大洗三溫暖，開盤報復性反彈600多點後，又翻黑下探，失守3萬3千點大關，盤中高低點震盪926點。」

從最高33,361點，直接下探到最低32,434點，原以為低接買盤挹注，指數翻紅回神，沒想到終場前2分鐘，來個回馬槍，再跳水，收跌110點，來到32,612點，成交值再萎縮到6,643億。

統一投顧總經理 廖婉婷：「昨天台股上市融資減肥了52億，實際上相對3/4和3/9，減肥幅度來看我覺得不夠，所以我建議可以關注幾個點，第一個就是能否成功站上月線，這個就會有機會收復短多，第二個的話就是油價有沒有辦法繼續往下跌，因為如果美伊戰事緩和的話，油價應該要持續的回落，第三個就是看美債殖利率，因為之前的油價引發通膨的擔憂，實際上美債殖利率是反彈到4.4%以上的，那昨天雖然有一點回落，可是回落的幅度也不是太重，所以如果美債跟油價都沒有辦法成功的往下探底的話，那我認為通膨的擔憂還是存在，它就會壓抑指數。」

大型權值股漲跌互見，台積電收平盤1,810元。（圖／民視新聞）

早盤領頭權值股，終場漲跌互見，台積電收盤遭壓摜低，平盤1,810元作收，金融抗跌，記憶體全面狂殺，兩度列處置股的旺宏，也亮綠燈，IC載板、PCB族群，疲軟不振，獲利了結賣壓明顯，航空股起飛，國防部採購，確定無人機商機，但除了長榮航太收紅、為升漲停，其他像中光電、雷虎收黑，隨著布蘭特原油，再度漲破100美元，台塑化、台塑強漲收紅。

統一投顧分析師 陳立委：「現階段還是受到戰事，這種非經濟因素的干擾，那我們投顧是預估，指數可能會在於月線以及季線，這邊做一個區間震盪整理，除非說戰事能夠緩解。」

國際油價仍牽動金融市場變化。（圖／民視新聞）

news.Title: 看準半導體商機　比利時列日機場爭取台灣貨運直飛
news.Content:
（中央社記者江明晏台北2026年03月24日電）歐洲重要貨運樞紐比利時列日機場來台爭取深化合作，列日機場表示，列日機場是純貨運機場，24小時全天候運營，貨運量逐年成長，估2026年達140萬噸，看好歐洲半導體設備大廠與台積電等產業的空運往來需求，將是桃園貨運直飛列日的龐大潛力。

為促進台灣與比利時空運物流產業的交流與合作，比利時台北辦事處瓦隆尼亞商務組今天在台舉辦「台比空運物流合作交流會」，由比利時台北辦事處瓦隆尼亞商務組長鄒宏平（Philippe TZOU）開場，並邀請台灣國際物流與供應鏈協會（TILSCA）理事長秦玉玲及台北市空運承攬商業同業公會（TAFLA）理事長羅文祥致詞，並由列日機場業務總監拉波特（AlexisLapot）進行專題簡報。

拉波特指出，列日機場為歐洲純貨運機場，同時也是歐洲第5大機場，具高度競爭優勢，機場採取24小時全天候運作且無宵禁限制，並有約20萬平方公尺倉儲空間。

他進一步說，列日機場地處歐洲市場中心，能快速連結荷蘭、德國、法國及盧森堡等主要經濟體，連結全球超過200個目的地，吸引Kuehne+Nagel、DHL、DSV及DB Schenker等全球主要貨運代理業者進駐。

他受訪時表示，列日機場的貨運量持續成長，2023年約為100萬噸，2024年約為115萬噸，成長15%，2025年約為130萬噸，2026年前2月的貨量也年成長20%，預估2026年可達140萬噸，在持續擴大倉儲設施與整體物流能量，目標貨運處理能力可提升至250萬噸規模，同時整合空運、陸運、鐵路及內河運輸，打造「陸海空整合」的國際物流樞紐。

針對貨源的部分，他表示，列日機場有約50%的貨運來自亞洲，主要為銷往歐洲的電子商務貨物，這一部分貨運量穩定且具週期性，其餘則有來自非洲和南美洲的生鮮品，並自歐洲出口的藥品，以及香港、荷蘭等地之間的馬匹運輸，每年約處理約1萬2000匹。

拉波特指出，台灣貨運直航列日機場的潛力著眼在半導體領域，例如半導體設備龍頭ASML長期與台積電建立合作關係，歐洲製造的設備需要運往台灣，而台灣製造的零組件則需運往歐洲，列日機場距離ASML約1小時車程，將支撐雙邊空運的需求。

羅文祥表示，台灣空運運量目前位居全球第9，在國際物流體系中扮演重要角色，面對當前美國市場的不確定性，歐洲有望成為台灣未來重要貿易合作夥伴，這次列日機場來台介紹桃園直飛列日的發展機會，是一個很好的契機，有助強化台歐之間的空運連結，未來合作值得期待。

包括長榮航空、中華航空及星宇航空、貨攬業者，以及交通部航政司、民航局與桃園國際機場等單位代表均出席這次交流會。

航空業者表示，近期歐洲空運市場受中東戰事波及，空運運力供給減少，原本在中東轉機前往歐洲的貨機也必須繞道而行，反映供給減少、油價大漲，近期歐洲空運價格確實上揚，而台灣與歐洲的空運貨源早年以電子產品為主，也有自行車相關產品等，近年則有電子零組件、半導體相關與大型機台，以及無人機等，相當看好歐洲空運市場。

業者透露，華航已在盧森堡設立歐洲貨運中心，將持續評估歐洲貨運需求性，長榮空運主力在美國，預計波音777-300ER客機改裝為全貨機完備後，有望加碼投入歐洲空運市場，星宇航空已採購10架A350F貨機，預估2028年有望迎來首架貨機。

news.Title: Apple (AAPL) Rated Sector Weight on Mixed Spending Trends
news.Content:
Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL ) ranks among the best most active stocks to buy right now . On March 12, KeyBanc reaffirmed a Sector Weight rating on Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL), citing a 9% month-over-month fall in indexed spending data, which is lower than the three-year average of 4%. KeyBanc stated that the data is broadly in line with forecasts, with January and February figures having been somewhat mixed and providing little context for the quarter.

The firm stated that its projections are roughly on par with expectations for fiscal year 2026 and just below the consensus for fiscal year 2027 after updating its estimates to account for recent product releases.

KeyBanc stated that it’s less worried about gross margin strain than investors are, adding that it isn’t expecting a catalyst, with the share trading at its historical valuation.

Moreover, on March 5, Rosenblatt boosted its price target for Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) to $268, citing the company’s product releases aimed at lower-end customers, including a Neo device for the Chromebook segment and a new iPhone 17e model.

Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories, and other varieties of related services.

While we acknowledge the potential of AAPL as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. news.Title: 台股開高走低收跌110點！台指期夜盤不退縮　今晚反彈近4百點
news.Content:
中東情勢瞬息萬變，台股也是陰晴不定，甚至是單日上沖下洗，弄得投資人猶如坐雲霄飛車。像是今天早盤，原本反應川普昨天爆料正與伊朗對話，激勵美股反彈1％的利多氣氛，台股一度上漲638點，沒想到行情持續不到5分鐘，迅速下殺，反倒下跌287點，高低相差926點。終場仍下跌110點，收在32612點，反彈行情瞬間消失無蹤。

不過，今天傍晚，伊朗外長釋出最高領袖同意與美國談判的消息，再度激勵台指期夜盤反攻，截至發稿為止，上漲近4百點：美股那斯達克期貨盤前也回到平盤附近震盪。

中東情勢昨晚以來不斷有新消息傳出，先是川普爆料正與伊朗進行建設性對話，雙方都想達成協議，因此他將延後對伊朗發電廠與能源基礎設施等軍事打擊5天；談判對象不是最高領袖，也不知道是不是還活著，但來談的人似乎有在掌權，講的事情確實都有發生：談判順利的話，事情就會落幕，否則美國只好繼續轟炸。

消息一出，雖然伊朗很快否認，指稱是川普要激勵金融市場，不過市場仍解讀川普急於結束這場戰爭，激勵市場演出慶祝行情，像是台指期夜盤一度90度急拉近1500點，收盤仍大漲1059點；美國道瓊工業指數收盤上漲631點，漲幅1.38%；那斯達克指數上漲1.38%，費城半導體指數上漲1.34%。

國際油價則是迅速急跌，布蘭特原油收盤暴跌10.3%，收在每桶95.92美元，收在100美元之下。不過隨著伊朗稍早否認，今天又反彈1.8％，但仍未重回百元之上。

盤勢震盪，但海外ETF仍有強者出現，像是第一金太空衛星(00910)ETF受惠多檔成份股大漲拉抬，今天收盤上漲3.76%，是盤面漲幅最強的海外股票ETF；今年以來漲幅更高達32%。

00910多檔成份股近期持續釋出訂單與營運業績正向消息，例如：火箭實驗室(Rocket Labs)及黑天科技(Blacksky Technology)都與美國國防部取得火箭及發射合約，為營收帶來實質貢獻；行星實驗室(Planet Labs)則掌握即時影像圖資利基，對於戰爭的即時地面監控帶來相當大的幫助，也成為各國爭相合作的高頻影像合作對象；此外，SpaceX的星鏈(Starlink)及亞馬遜的Kuiper計畫，則為歐洲得安技術(Intellian Tech)及台廠啟碁等通訊設備廠商，帶來地面接收站週邊設備訂單的放量成長。

第一金太空衛星ETF經理人曾萬勝指出，輝達執行長黃仁勳宣布推出專門用於軌道數據運算中的晶片，進入太空AI的時代，加上衛星通訊、定位觀測等需求可望持續成長，太空AI將更進一步帶領衛星通訊產業鏈持續高速成長，並推升太空經濟持續擴展。

曾萬勝樂觀預期，今年SpaceX即將IPO募資題材，將帶來市場資金競爭效應與太空衛星個股比價效應，第一金太空衛星ETF鎖定太空衛星產業純度最高的投資標的，是目前台灣投資人參與全球太空通訊、低軌衛星商機最簡便的投資工具。

不過，曾萬勝提醒，太空衛星產業具備高波動、高成長特性，戰爭題材帶來衝刺行情，同時也伴隨籌碼震盪風險，今年太空產業進入到營運轉折年，從燒錢轉向獲利；加上產業龍頭掛牌帶來族群投資價值重估等；加上技術面推進，火箭實驗推動Neutron大型火箭發射試飛等，一旦成功可望驅動大型火箭訂單爆量等，太空衛星具備中長線成長機會，近期太空衛星股價受

news.Title: Broadcom (AVGO) Secures Long-Term Growth with Hyperscaler Deals
news.Content:
Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ: AVGO ) ranks among the best high profit margin stocks to buy . On March 5, Benchmark reaffirmed its Buy rating and $485 price target for Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ:AVGO), highlighting the company’s better-than-expected second-quarter forecasts. Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ:AVGO) exceeded market estimates with earnings per share of $2.05, compared to the projection of $2.02. The company also topped revenue expectations, reporting $19.31 billion versus $19.21 billion.

Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ:AVGO) expects its AI revenue to be significantly greater than $100 billion in the coming year. The company also mentioned supply obligations that span through 2028. That said, the market’s key issue is whether hyperscaler and LLM capital expenditures can be managed absent a near-term return on investment slowdown.

Meanwhile, management emphasized enhanced multi-year visibility, which will be fueled by an increasing number of six key platform users expanding their training and inference capacities. Demand, according to the company, is becoming centered among these hyperscaler partners.

news.Title: Erste Group Upgrades Meta (META) to Buy, Highlights the Company’s AI Investments
news.Content:
Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ: META ) ranks among the best high profit margin stocks to buy . On March 5, Erste Group raised Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ:META) to Buy from Hold, highlighting the company’s AI investments and pricing. Analyst Hans Engel stated that Meta expects a major increase in spending to meet its AI objectives in 2026.

Based on the anticipated 12-month price-to-earnings ratio, Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ:META) is currently trading at the lower end of its valuation history over the past 10 years. news.Title: Prenetics Global Conference: CEO Pivots to IM8, Targets $200M 2026 Sales and $40M Buyback
news.Content:
Key Points

Prenetics has pivoted to focus on its supplement brand IM8 , which launched in December 2024 and is guiding to roughly $180–$200M in revenue for 2026 after reporting $60M for full-year 2025 and $10M in monthly revenue by December 2025.

IM8’s rapid online expansion is driven by celebrity co-founder David Beckham and athlete ambassadors, a content-heavy ad approach (about 2,200 Meta ads and ~50 landing pages), and sales into 31 countries with ~40% of revenue from the U.S.

Prenetics announced a $40M share repurchase plan alongside $2.75M of executive insider buying, reports IM8 unit economics near a 3:1 LTV-to-CAC (CAC ~$220–$230, LTV ~$660–$670), and holds about $160M in liquid assets.

Interested in Prenetics Global Limited? Here are five stocks we like better.

Invest in Gold

American Hartford Gold: #1 Precious Metals Dealer in the Nation

Learn More

Priority Gold: Up to $15k in Free Silver + Zero Account Fees on Qualifying Purchase

Thor Metals Group: Best Overall Gold IRA

Powered by Money.com - Yahoo may earn commission from the links above.

Prenetics Global (NASDAQ:PRE) co-founder and CEO Danny Yeung said the company is now centered on its IM8 supplement business after divesting non-core operations over the past 12 to 18 months, pointing to rapid early growth since IM8’s launch in December 2024.

Positioning IM8 in a competitive market

Yeung described IM8 as entering a “very competitive” supplement category where many advised against launching due to the crowded landscape and limited regulation. He said the company’s approach relied on pairing a “great product” with strong brand awareness and distribution, aided by having David Beckham as a co-founder to “cut through the noise” from a PR and messaging standpoint.

→ Planet Labs: The Satellite Stock That Keeps Shooting to the Moon

Yeung said IM8’s core message is simplifying nutrition. Rather than consumers assembling large supplement stacks—he cited “12–16 different supplements”—IM8 aims to consolidate “16 supplements in one powder sachet” mixed with water.

Product development, testing, and certifications

Yeung said IM8’s formulation took roughly a year and a half to develop and was supported by a scientific advisory board that included Dr. Dawn Mussallem of Mayo Clinic and Suzanne Devkota of Cedars-Sinai, among others. He also emphasized steps taken to support quality and credibility, including:

A 12-week randomized case-control trial conducted with the San Francisco Research Institute prior to launch

NSF Certified for Sport certification

Third-party testing from Eurofins

→ Active ETFs Surge Past Passive, and These Are in the Lead

Yeung said the company makes research results and reports available for download on its website. He also noted that IM8 includes “90+ ingredients” and that each ingredient on the label is third-party tested, saying the testing verifies that stated ingredient quantities match what is delivered.

Story Continues

On athlete adoption, Yeung cited Aryna Sabalenka, the world’s No. 1 women’s tennis player, saying she took the product for three months before contacting the company. He also said IM8 recently signed Ollie Bearman, an F1 driver, and that Bearman’s team is using the product.

Growth milestones and international reach

→ Trash to Treasure: 3 Waste Removal Stocks to Minimize Volatility

Yeung outlined a timeline of growth since launch. He said IM8 began selling online in December 2024 and generated $581,000 in revenue in its first month. In January, the company held a public launch in New York, and Beckham appeared on the TODAY show to promote the product, which Yeung said drove PR and media exposure.

He said the company saw an uplift in June after bringing on Sabalenka, describing the partnership as organic and driven by her performance coach’s experience with the product. In October, IM8 launched its second SKU, a Longevity product, which Yeung said increased average order value from $110 to $150 “overnight.” By December 2025, he said IM8 reached $10 million in monthly revenue.

Yeung said IM8 shipped to 31 countries from the outset, calling it logistically difficult but strategically important. He said the U.S. represented about 40% of overall revenue, with Canada, the U.K., Australia, and Singapore among the next largest markets. Yeung said the business is “100% online,” with 98% of sales on IM8’s own website and about 2% on Amazon, where he said pricing is intentionally higher to steer consumers to the company’s site and customer journey.

Yeung said full-year revenue in 2025 totaled $60 million, including $25 million in the U.S. He added that, in his view, the U.S. “can easily do $150 million–$200 million” at scale.

2026 outlook, marketing engine, and new SKUs

Looking to 2026, Yeung said momentum has continued into the first quarter and that the company is confident in its projection of $180 million to $200 million for 2026. He attributed growth in part to a content-driven social advertising strategy, stating IM8 runs about 2,200 Meta ads at any given time and uses roughly 50 landing pages tailored to different consumer personas.

On product expansion, Yeung said the company is being deliberate about adding SKUs to avoid diluting the brand message and because developing best-in-class products requires significant work. He said two additional SKUs are planned for launch by Q4, aimed at “very large” total addressable markets, but noted they were not included in 2026 guidance.

Retention targets, unit economics, and capital allocation

On customer performance, Yeung said repeat purchases are a majority of revenue and argued that rapid growth to $100 million in annual recurring revenue would not be possible without strong retention. He said IM8 is targeting 12-month retention of roughly 25% to 30%, compared with what he described as typical supplement-category retention “in the teens.”

He also discussed evolving subscription options. Yeung said that for much of 2025, IM8 offered one-time purchases and a monthly subscription, which he described as $112 for a one-time purchase in the U.S. and $89 for monthly renewal. In December, the company introduced a three-month program, lowering the monthly subscription price to $78 with $235 paid upfront. Yeung said the three-month option helps because customers commit for a longer period, shipping is consolidated, and revenue can be recognized when three months’ product is shipped.

On unit economics, Yeung cited an industry benchmark of a 3:1 LTV-to-CAC ratio over 24 months. He said IM8 is currently around that level, with customer acquisition cost of roughly $220 to $230, average order value around $200, and lifetime value around $660 to $670.

Yeung also addressed a recently announced $40 million share repurchase plan and executive open-market buying. He said the leadership team believes the public company has been undervalued and cited what he described as strong brand momentum. Yeung said executives have purchased $2.75 million in total, including $1.25 million of his own funds. He also said the company had $160 million in liquid assets.

In response to a question about China, Yeung said he has spoken with potential partners and sees “significant upside opportunity,” but emphasized the company is not rushing entry and is taking a strategic approach. He said many U.S. companies fail in China by attempting to import products without proper localization. He added that IM8’s ambassadors—including Beckham and Sabalenka—are well-liked in China, which he believes could be advantageous when the company decides the time is right.

Finally, Yeung said the company is using AI tools to scale its marketing operation, arguing that effective use requires “focused attention” and constant testing across creative, landing pages, and conversion metrics. He said that refining this approach is part of a plan to reach profitability by Q4 2027.

About Prenetics Global (NASDAQ:PRE)

Prenetics Global (NASDAQ: PRE) is a molecular diagnostics and genetic testing company that delivers a broad range of laboratory and at-home testing solutions. The company's core offerings include next-generation sequencing (NGS) panels for hereditary health risks, pharmacogenomic reports to guide medication choices, and comprehensive consumer DNA testing services. In addition to genetic insights, Prenetics provides infectious disease diagnostics—most notably real-time PCR testing for pathogens such as SARS-CoV-2—through an integrated platform that combines sample collection, laboratory processing and digital reporting.

Serving both business-to-consumer and business-to-business markets, Prenetics operates a network of laboratories and service centers across Asia Pacific, Europe, the Middle East and North America.

The article " Prenetics Global Conference: CEO Pivots to IM8, Targets $200M 2026 Sales and $40M Buyback " was originally published by MarketBeat.

news.Title: Nvidia Stock Could Become the Next Apple. Here's What It Means for Investors.
news.Content:
I know what you're probably thinking. Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) is the most valuable company in the world -- even bigger than Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) . Why would it need to "become the next Apple"?

Nvidia just had its annual GTC conference. And at that conference, management talked extensively about the boom in artificial intelligence (AI) inferencing and Nvidia's growing ecosystem that expands beyond graphics processing units (GPUs) to capture more than one-time hardware sales.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Just as Apple built an ecosystem of consumer products that have become as essential to many households as laundry detergent and toothpaste, so too is Nvidia building an ecosystem primarily for enterprises to create a recurring revenue stream in the age of AI inferencing.

Here's why this evolving business model could be a game changer for investors by adding balance to Nvidia's investment thesis.

An ecosystem in the making

Nvidia's earnings growth has exploded in recent years as key hyperscaler customers build data centers that rely on Nvidia GPUs . The data center business is so massive that other segments like professional visualization, gaming, automotive, and robotics barely move the needle. In fiscal 2026, the data center segment made up just under 90% of total revenue. And that puts pressure on Nvidia to continue selling GPUs to hyperscalers to maintain its breakneck growth rate.

Nvidia's latest architecture, Rubin, already addresses part of the problem. It includes six chips that work together to improve efficiency at rack scale for data center applications. Many of Rubin's breakthroughs are related to AI inference rather than training.

Think of the AI model as the knowledge base that AI agents and tools use to do real-world work. Applying AI models requires immense compute for inference.

The tokenization of inferencing creates a recurring revenue stream for Nvidia. The idea is that hyperscalers will charge customers based on the number of AI inference tokens used. As AI usage for generative AI, AI agents, and physical AI grows, so will the number of tokens demanded. Nvidia's hardware and software are built to process tokens faster, which will appeal to hyperscalers.

All told, Nvidia's goal is to create an ecosystem that includes its purpose-built AI chips, networking hardware, and inferencing software that will scale in lockstep with token demand -- as inference and physical AI will demand far more tokens than simple chat-based generative AI.

Story Continues

Taking a page out of Apple's playbook

Nvidia's road map for capitalizing on inferencing recurring revenue mirrors Apple's blueprint.

iPhone is the focal point of an ecosystem of Apple products that complement each other -- from iPhone to Mac, iPad, Apple Watch, AirPods, etc. This is similar to Nvidia building on its GPU business with "extreme codesign" to capture revenue from AI data center compute, networking, and storage.

Apple has services that directly support those products, like iCloud, as well as services that can be integrated and used by those products -- like Apple Music, Apple TV, Apple Card, etc. Nvidia's recurring revenue stream from AI inference tokens would follow this same business model.

Apple has evolved into a stable, high-margin, moderate-growth company that generates a ton of free cash flow (FCF) to buy back stock and steadily increase its dividend. Similarly, Nvidia expects to spend 50% of its FCF this year on buybacks and dividends. Nvidia only pays a $0.01 quarterly dividend now, but I could see it announcing a massive dividend raise followed by moderate annual increases in a similar vein to what Apple has done for 14 consecutive years.

Nvidia is a great buy now

A potential slowdown in growth sounds like bad news for Nvidia. But in reality, it could just mark the next stage of the company's maturation.

If Nvidia were able to reduce its dependence on data center hardware through an inferencing-as-a-service revenue stream, it could make the stock more appealing to balanced investors who like a little passive income sprinkled on their long-term holdings. After all, Apple commands a much higher valuation than Nvidia -- with Apple trading at 29.3 times forward earnings compared to just 21.9 for Nvidia -- suggesting that some investors value predictable earnings growth and balance more than the prospect of hypergrowth.

All told, Nvidia's build-out of a recurring revenue stream is a brilliant way to help reduce the pain of an eventual pullback in hyperscale capital expenditures -- making the stock's investment thesis even more appealing to long-term investors.

news.Title: Memory Is Wall Street’s Favorite Tech Trade as Mag 7 Disappoints
news.Content: (Bloomberg) — In the stock market’s volatile and uncertain start to the year, one trade has held strong: go long memory and storage. Companies like Sandisk Corp. ( SNDK ), Western Digital Corp. ( WDC ), and Seagate Technology Holdings Plc ( STX ) are among the leaders in the S&P 500 Index ( ^GSPC ) in 2026, even with their recent declines, as heavy spending on artificial intelligence creates insatiable demand for memory and storage components used in data centers. They were also top performers in 2025. “Infrastructure is the place to be,” said Rob Thummel, a senior portfolio manager at Tortoise Capital, which holds shares of Western Digital, Seagate, Sandisk and Micron Technology Inc. in its Tortoise AI Infrastructure ETF. The strength of these stocks runs counter to the Magnificent Seven technology giants, all of which are down this year, with an index of the group declining 10%. And with so many AI hyperscalers needing memory and storage products, the companies now have tremendous pricing power, suggesting that the old playbook surrounding the cyclical nature of the business may need to be reconsidered. In particular, the stocks fit into the so-called heavy assets, low obsolescence, or HALO, trade that’s caught Wall Street’s attention, Thummel said. “The sell side of that has been megacap tech, those haven’t done as well,” he said. “Obviously, the buy side of that has been infrastructure and memory storage and data storage.” The thesis got some validation last week when memory chipmaker Micron Technology Inc., another one of the year’s best performers, gave a blowout forecast that underlined the ways spending on AI has made this cycle bigger and potentially longer lasting than past booms. This dynamic helps explain the group’s favored status even as investors turn skeptical about the companies spending aggressively on AI, those that could be disrupted by the technology and even long-time winners like Nvidia Corp. “You’re simply seeing a different level of upside in memory, along with massive pricing power, so while safe may not be the right word, these companies seem easier to underwrite than other parts of the AI trade,” said Jamie Zakalik, a senior research analyst who covers semiconductor companies at Neuberger Berman. Sandisk is by far the leading gainer in the S&P 500 this year and last, soaring more than 1,850% since starting trading in February 2025. Western Digital and Seagate are in the top 20. And Micron, which has shed 12% since its earnings last week raised concerns about its own aggressive spending plans, is still up 42% in 2026, putting it among the 25 best performers. Story Continues By comparison, the S&P 500 is down 3.9% this year, the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 Index has lost 4.2% and the Philadelphia Stock Exchange Semiconductor Index has gained just 9.7%. Of course, increases in memory prices are driven by supply shortages. Failures to balance supply and demand have been at the heart of the industry’s cyclical history as booming prices lead to increased production, which can cause prices to plummet when demand softens. “The supply is still trying to play catch up,” said Ann Miletti, head of equity investments at Allspring Global Investments. “Over time that will get resolved. I think it’s more of a timing issue than it is anything else.” Still, investors see further upside for the stocks as hyperscalers keep spending to build new data centers. “There are a lot of reasons to be positive about memory right now,” Zakalik said. “AI is a very different market, with very different memory needs than traditional hardware. There’s an argument to be made that demand will be more structural, so maybe this time is different.” Just ask Nvidia Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang. Last week, he said his company is expecting more than $1 trillion in AI chip sales over the next few years, an indication that escalating spending on AI will continue to create high demand. That’s encouraging for investors buying memory names. “I still do see upside as long as we continue to see this hyperscaler spending and new AI applications,” Tortoise Capital’s Thummel said. Huang “talks about what’s needed, and memory’s one of those things.” Tech Chart of the Day Nintendo Co. is cutting back the production of Switch 2 after demand for the $450 gaming console trailed the company’s expectations during the year-end holiday season, particularly in the US. Top Tech Stories The Federal Communications Commission ordered a ban on the import of new models of foreign-produced consumer wireless routers after an interagency panel determined they threaten national security. Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. is launching a new chip for agentic AI and inference computing, adding to a portfolio of semiconductors designed to drive its artificial intelligence ambitions. SK Hynix Inc. plans to spend 11.9 trillion won ($7.9 billion) on cutting-edge extreme ultraviolet lithography chipmaking tools from ASML Holding NV, deepening a push into next-generation memory to sate quickening AI demand. Apple Inc. is preparing to introduce advertising in its Maps app, part of a broader push to generate more money from services. news.Title: 何麗梅4月自台積電退休　獲宏碁提名獨董候選人
news.Content:
（中央社記者吳家豪台北2026年03月24日電）晶圓代工廠台積電 (2330) 企業策略發展組織及企業永續辦公室主管資深副總經理何麗梅將於4月退休，新動向已曝光。電腦品牌廠宏碁 (2353) 今天公告董事候選人名單，何麗梅獲提名為獨立董事候選人；宏碁預計在5月29日舉行股東會進行董事改選。

被外界稱為「大掌櫃」的何麗梅於1999年加入台積電，自1999年至2003年擔任會計長，2003年至2019年期間，擔任財務長兼發言人。她曾獲得中華財金高階管理人協會頒發「傑出財務長」，並於2007年至2019年間，由金融亞洲及機構投資人評選為台灣及亞洲地區「最佳財務長」。

宏碁將於5月29日召開股東常會改選董監事，董事候選名單包括宏碁董事長暨執行長陳俊聖、宏碁集團創辦人施振榮、施振榮長子施宣輝；獨立董事候選名單包括何麗梅、吳由理、楊泮池、何美玥。

news.Title: 中東戰事疑慮市場震盪！台股收跌110點　台積電平盤報1810
news.Content:
【記者呂承哲／台北報導】隨著美國總統川普喊出暫停對伊朗能源基礎設施軍事打擊五天，為外交周旋爭取時間，帶動國際油價重挫10％，並與伊朗達成具建設性的會談，但伊朗又出面否認消息，週一（23日）美股4大指數仍全面上揚逾1%，輝達上漲1.59%，台積電ADR上漲2.80%。台股週二（24日）開盤上漲超過630點，台積電上漲40元，報1850元。

截至中午12點，台股上漲241.52點或0.74%、暫報32964.02點，早盤高低震盪約926點。台積電股價上漲20元或1.1%、暫報1830元，鴻海股價上漲1元或0.51%、暫報197元，台達電股價上漲30元或2.13%、報1440元，聯發科股價上漲10元或0.62%、暫報1635元。

台股週二收盤，台股終場下跌110.26點或0.34%、報32612.24點，成交金額來到億元。台積電股價收在平盤、報1810元，台達電股價上漲5元或0.35%、報1415元，鴻海股價下跌1元或0.51%、報195元，聯發科股價下跌5元或0.31%、報1620元。

台股週一重挫821.38點或2.45%，終場收在32722.5點，成交金額6770.28億元。從三大法人來看，外資及陸資賣超839.23億元，投信買超104.07億元，自營商賣超311.12億元，合計賣超1046.27億元。

法人表示，央行上修2026年經濟成長率至7.28%，加上外銷訂單前2月創新高，顯示台灣經濟基本面穩健，科技業訂單與獲利動能持續。不過，美伊戰事升溫及主要央行偏鷹立場，短線市場仍需整理消化賣壓，操作建議以低接為主、避免追高。

中長期來看，輝達GTC大會釋出關鍵訊號，AI已進入大規模應用階段，從模型訓練轉向推理與實際任務執行，資料中心將轉型為「AI工廠」，帶動龐大算力需求，預估2027年AI基礎建設規模上看1兆美元。受此趨勢帶動，AI伺服器、散熱、網通及零組件需求可望持續成長。隨Vera Rubin平台獲多家雲端業者採用並預計下半年上市，台廠供應鏈後續動能可期。

news.Title: Musk’s Terafab Fever Dream Exposes Reality of the AI Chip Crunch
news.Content:
(Bloomberg) — When Elon Musk took the stage on Saturday to unveil his plans to get into semiconductor production, he didn’t spare the superlatives.

“I have an important announcement to make, which is the most epic chip-building exercise in history by far,” he said before a small crowd in Austin, Texas. “This is really going to take things to the next level, a level probably people aren’t even contemplating right now. We’re going to adjust the context by a few orders of magnitude here.”

No one has ever proposed anything quite like what Musk calls Terafab. The project, as outlined, would be a massive operation to build cutting-edge semiconductors for artificial intelligence, robotics and space forays. He would not only try to take on the best chip manufacturer in the world — Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. ( TSM ) — he wants to do it at volumes far beyond the industry’s current capacity.

The scale boggles the mind. The project would require something like $5 trillion to $13 trillion in capital spending, according to estimates from Bernstein analysts. That would fund 140 to 360 new factories, each making 50,000 wafers per month in order to reach the 1 terawatt of annual computing capacity he proposed.

Musk, by far the richest person in the world, has accomplished what others believed impossible before — creating a commercially viable rocket business with SpaceX, bringing electric vehicles to the mainstream with Tesla Inc., and delivering internet connectivity from space with Starlink. But some doubt Musk can, or even intends to, build what he sketched out in Austin.

“A true Terafab feels like a stretch to us,” wrote the Bernstein analysts, including Stacy Rasgon. The amount of compute would be “on the order of the entire current global installed semi capacity and would in fact require many multiples of current installed capacity for ‘relevant’ semis.” Patrick Moorhead of Moor Insights & Strategy wrote that it’s unlikely Musk will ultimately build chip fab facilities at all.

Rather, Musk’s goal with his Terafab proclamation may be something different: to highlight the growing shortage of chip production capacity, or motivate chipmakers to step up. Or it may boost the prospects for SpaceX as it heads for an initial public offering later this year.

There are growing concerns in Silicon Valley that the semiconductor industry isn’t ramping up fast enough to produce the chips AI companies will need to meet their ambitious business plans. Amazon.com Inc., Alphabet Inc. and other hyperscalers expect to spend about $650 billion this year alone to build out data center infrastructure. That’s already creating a severe crunch in memory chips, and is beginning to spill over into AI accelerators.

Story Continues

Nvidia Corp.’s ( NVDA ) Jensen Huang has talked about how he’d like more out of TSMC, the Taiwanese firm that makes all of his advanced semiconductors. But the linchpin of global chipmaking has taken a disciplined approach to expansion. “We directly receive very strong signals from our customers’ customers, requesting the capacity to support their business,” CEO C.C. Wei told analysts during an October earnings call. “We will also remain disciplined and thorough in our capacity planning approach to ensure we deliver profitable growth for our shareholders.”

Musk was explicit about how Tesla ( TSLA ), xAI and SpaceX ( SPAX.PVT ) are going to need enormous quantities of silicon in the years ahead. He wants to eventually have one terawatt of computing power for AI services and hundreds of millions of new Optimus humanoid robots every year. The “key missing ingredient” is computing power, he said estimating the current AI output is only about 2% of what his companies need.

He said he has urged suppliers including TSMC, Samsung Electronics Co. ( 005930.KS ) and Micron Technology Inc. ( MU ) to expand as quickly as they can — and promised to take pretty much everything they make.

“We will buy all of their chips; I have said these exact words to them,” he said.

But semiconductor companies have opted to expand at a more measured rate, one far short of what he thinks necessary for Tesla, SpaceX and xAI.

“So we either build the Terafab or we don’t have the chips,” he said. “And we need the chips.”

Analysts are skeptical not just because Musk has never made chips before. He’s proposing to do it in a way that contravenes the economics of the chip sector. Building factories to supply your own needs was the way the industry got its start. But the steep price — a cutting-edge facility costs about $30 billion and can be obsolete in as little as five years - means you have to produce and sell a massive number of chips to justify the fixed costs.

There’s logic to Musk’s recognition of the risks of supply shortages and what that might mean for his ambitions for the vehicle and robot markets, according to Barclays Capital.

“However, as is frequently the case with Tesla’s efforts, this endeavor is unprecedented in a number of ways,” its analysts wrote in a research note. “Tesla lacks experience in manufacturing chips, with significant cost and execution risk. Accordingly, partnership with either Samsung or TSMC, or perhaps even Intel, could be a more likely route.”

Nvidia is just one example of a company that’s never owned a factory. The majority of its peers are in the same boat. They primarily outsource their production to TSMC, and to a lesser extent Samsung.

Those companies are able to make the economics work by essentially pooling the business of most of the industry to get a volume of orders that pays for the capital expenditure and research and design.

Musk proposed that Terafab would use what’s known as the integrated device manufacturer model, which means designing and making chips in-house. That’s the model Intel Corp. used to dominate for decades. But the company is now a shadow of its former self — in part because of mis-steps that highlight the enormous complexities of fabricating chips.

In-house chipmaking has faded since Morris Chang founded TSMC in 1987 and created the pure-foundry business that freed up tech companies to focus on design. Today, the Taiwanese firm serves as the go-to chipmaker for Nvidia, Apple Inc., Advanced Micro Devices Inc., Broadcom Inc. and Google - just to name a few.

In addition to a different business model, Musk also seemed to envision a very different manufacturing setup than ones used by TSMC and Samsung.

“They’re getting clean rooms wrong in these modern fabs. I’m going to make a bet here, that Tesla will have a 2-nanometer fab, and I can eat a cheeseburger and smoke a cigar in the fab,” he said in a podcast in January.

Clean rooms are critical for modern chip production. Individual transistors are many times smaller than a virus. Just one speck of dust can cause havoc and millions of dollars of wasted effort.

During Saturday’s livestream, Musk said the Austin fab will house equipment for logic semiconductors, memory chips, packaging, testing, and lithography mask production in a single building. No existing chip firm has an operation like that. The specialization and differences in processes mean it’s typically not economically feasible.

At the same time, Musk also appeared to be invoking US interests as a reason for his new chip venture. He retweeted a post on X saying that Terafab is vital to national security as the bulk of the world’s chips are still made in Taiwan.

Privacy Policy

Subscribe

To be sure, Musk’s semiconductor ambitions should be viewed through the prism of audacious ideas over the years: helping humankind colonize Mars; shooting people through sealed tunnels from New York to D.C.; reusing space rockets. At least one of those dreams is now reality.

Musk’s vision touched a nerve because it highlights one of the most pressing concerns facing the industry today: a shortage of the high-end semiconductors that companies and governments desperately need to deploy game-changing AI.

Musk “has admittedly done more than one thing that naysayers have called impossible at the outset,” the Bernstein analysts wrote. “Perhaps Elon has something more off-the-wall in mind to improve things (we leave that as an exercise for the reader, as we don’t know what it would be).”

—With assistance from Kara Carlson.

news.Title: 台積電、鴻海急殺反彈！專家揭「3大止跌訊號」 台股避險勝黃金
news.Content: 財經中心／倪譽瑋報導 近日台股持續震盪，摩爾投顧分析師 謝晨彥 在 影片 中說明，外資機構操作時不會一口氣賣掉手中股票，因此會看到盤勢急跌反彈，股票賣完，觀察殖利率壓力是否下降等3訊號後會再慢慢進場；而台股的跌勢相對黃金小，當整個市場穩定下來後，可留意資金是否回流。聚焦到台積電、鴻海，兩者同樣面臨外資賣超，但回歸基本面，仍有AI需求加持，面臨波動倒不必特別思考如何砍掉手上股票。 台股連日修正 分析師看好避險能力 23日台股早盤一度暴跌逾千點，最終收斂至821.38點。謝晨彥對此提出，機構在操作過程中不會一口氣把股票全賣「他一定是逐步，所以你會看到急跌反彈、急跌反彈」慢慢把原本想要賣的股票都賣完以後，待觀察國際局勢，發現股市跌勢已經告一段落，他們才會慢慢進場。 謝晨彥指出，自美伊戰爭以來台股未見跌停「會不會是市場資金在抽離當中，買盤的意願是很強的？」現在原油大漲、股市大跌，資金要往哪裡去「能量守恆定律，他就是移動，你這邊在大跌我敢買嗎？」所以變成台股跌幅較小，當整個市場穩定下來後、當大家發現避險的首選是台股的時候「你想資金是不是就開始大幅回流？」 謝晨彥補充外資注重的訊號，第一是信用利差，公司債與美國公債的殖利率差距、第二是短期與長期利率的利差、第三是10年期公債殖利率。他認為未來要注意的是波動，波動曲線開始正常化、信用利差沒有擴大、殖利率壓力下降「止跌時間就到了」；但也提醒，他們不會一口氣全賣掉「盤一定是急殺反彈」無法保證當前就是穩定了。 台積電、鴻海等科技股面臨賣超 但長期展望較佳 台灣科技股從基本面來看，謝晨彥認為，外銷訂單還是關鍵，如輝達提出新一代AI架構Vera Rubin的願景，資通訊與電子零組件AI需求強勁，這一塊對台股是相當有利的；但目前由於國際天然氣供應吃緊，台灣眾多科技產業需要電力，他認為重啟核電是好事，「如果這塊能夠盡快定案的話，也許對台股電力方面的壓力就會比較小一點」。

news.Title: Meta Will Pay Influencers Up to $3,000 Per Month to Post on Facebook. Is It Enough to Fix Engagement?
news.Content:
Meta (NASDAQ: META) recently announced a new program, called the Creator Fast Track, aimed at persuading social media influencers to post content on its Facebook platform. And for some influencers, it could mean $3,000 per month in guaranteed payments from Meta.

Handing out monetary incentives to influencers with big followings is nothing new for social media companies, but the reasons behind this content push matter. Facebook continues to fall behind video-focused rivals in the amount of time spent on its platform.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Unfortunately, throwing money at the problem probably won't work.

Paying for posts

The Creator Fast Track program will pay Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok influencers with at least 100,000 followers a guaranteed $1,000 per month if they post at least 15 Reels and 10 posts in a month on Facebook. If a person has 1 million or more followers, the pay jumps to $3,000 per month.

Influencers won't get paid for very long, however, with the guaranteed payments lasting just three months. They can still earn money on Facebook's platform through other monetization programs, though.

So, why do this? Facebook is trying to appeal to younger users and boost the time people spend on the platform. Millennials make up the largest segment of Facebook users, about 52%, and the company is trying to appeal to younger users who prefer to spend more time watching videos on TikTok and YouTube.

It's not that younger users are absent from Facebook -- men aged 18-24 are Facebook's second-largest user group. But Facebook falls far behind in how long people spend on its platform. For example, TikTok users spend more than 33 hours per month on the app, YouTube users spend over 27 hours, and Facebook lags with just under 19 hours.

Paying influencers to post Reels is very strategic for Facebook . Meta's management says its users spent 30% more time watching Instagram Reels in the fourth quarter -- and the company is likely trying to replicate that success on Facebook.

Most importantly, Facebook is looking to boost its video engagement and views more organically. Management said on the Q4 call that Facebook videos saw a 7% lift in views due to the company's "optimization" efforts. That suggests video improvements have come more from Facebook making changes behind the scenes to boost engagement, rather than from organic growth from user content.

Story Continues

Meta shareholders should watch this closely

I doubt paying influencers guaranteed payments for three months will make much of a difference for Facebook and how people use its social media platform. The new Creator Fast Track program feels more like one small piece in a broader content strategy for Facebook as it tries to get influencers to cross-post content on its platform.

It's not a bad strategy, but it's an admission that the company continues to face increasing pressure to stay relevant and keep its users engaged. I wouldn't be surprised to see more video-focused incentives or programs from Facebook as it looks to boost the amount of time its users spend on the platform.

news.Title: 她白銀慘虧20%！網嚇瘋：求女神先別碰台股
news.Content:
財經中心／李筱舲報導

國際局勢大洗三溫暖！美國總統川普23日突宣布暫緩攻擊伊朗電力設施，並釋出五天談判緩衝期，激勵全球股市與比特幣強勢反彈。然而，被封為「反指標女神」的財經網紅巴逆逆卻慘淪受害者，她剛抵達日本福岡就發現白銀在停損後即噴發，讓她傻眼表示「白白虧損 20%」。

川普23日突然宣布暫緩攻擊伊朗，讓全球市場與比特幣都往上漲。（圖／翻攝自吃土鋁繩-巴逆逆臉書）

川普變臉救市！巴逆逆停損白銀慘賠20%

巴逆逆昨（23）晚在臉書PO出影片，她表示，她在2月27日、28日回台灣的時候，正好碰到川普說「要打仗」的消息。結果23日她一抵達日本全球市場便因川普宣布暫緩攻擊而瘋狂噴發，全球股市與比特幣都上漲。她說川普對外聲稱「都（與伊朗）談好了」，雖然看起來伊朗方面表示沒有這件事，但巴逆逆無奈表示很多時候要以川普的說法為準。她透露，她的白銀在這波操作中虧了20%左右，最後選擇認賠賣出，巴逆逆直言「我也希望他（川普）可以早點告訴我他的計畫，那我今天就不用停損了」。

這波操作讓巴逆逆停損白銀慘賠20%。（圖／翻攝自吃土鋁繩-巴逆逆臉書）

反指標發威？巴逆逆停損白銀網瘋問：幾號回台灣？

貼文曝光後，也讓大票網友留言笑喊：「原來是女神離開台灣，難怪噴」、「您真的不開廟斂財嗎？右邊龍柱我捐了！」、「妳休息吧，別殘害台股」、「先不要！川普說話反反復復的啊」、「她到底是怎麼做到的？我真的很好奇一個人可以控制整個戰爭跟股市」、「我們可以一起集資斗內你補損，拜託先不要亂動」、「我覺得妳這兩個月都先不要碰台股跟美股，放過台灣跟華爾街」、「重點是你回台灣的時間？」。

《民視新聞網》提醒您：內容僅供參考，投資人於決策時應審慎評估風險，並就投資結果自行負責。

news.Title: 台股新視野! 產業布局報告 "創新板"網站看得到
news.Content:
財經中心／葉為襄、胡崇恩 台北報導

投資熱潮夯！以往股民們要拿到產業最新研究報告，可能得仰賴券商，證交所攜手資策會和台經院，宣布啟動前瞻產業研究合作，首度把國家級深入產業報告，帶給投資人，軍工、AI、量子運算怎麼佈局，創新板的網站都能看到。

證交所總經理李愛玲與智庫代表於近日共同出席啟動儀式，象徵台股朝向「亞洲那斯達克」的目標邁出關鍵一步。此次合作旨在透過國家級研究力量，涵蓋生技醫療、數位雲端、電動機械等三大類別，並鎖定 AI、軍工、量子運算等 14 項關鍵產業情報。

台股新視野！ 量子運算與AI等各產業報告創新板網站免費看。(圖/民視財經網)

證交所總經理李愛玲表示：「我們希望引領國內外創新公司，能在台灣市場成長為獨角獸。未來每個月將提供 3 到 5 則深度報告，透過提高資訊透明度，讓投資人能看見創新板企業的長遠發展性與價值。」

過去智庫的研究報告多用於政府決策幕僚或企業研發參考，此次轉向資本市場是一大突破。資策會產業研究所長 洪春暉指出，未來將針對資本市場的需求與資源配置進行「客製化」調整，讓趨勢分析更貼近投資端的實際應用。台經院副院長 周霞麗則提到，在國家政策支持下，生醫、半導體及軟硬體整合等內容，都將是未來報告的產出重點。

台股新視野！ 量子運算與AI等各產業報告創新板網站免費看。(圖/民視財經網)透過此次證交所與智庫的跨界合作，不僅為投資人提供了獨一無二的佈局參考，也為「創新板」企業大幅提升了市場能見度，建立更健全的投資生態系。

news.Title: 美伊心理戰!台股洗三溫暖高低震盪近千點 終場收跌110點
news.Content:
財經中心／唐詩晴、賴文軒 台北報導

美伊戰爭疑雲籠罩，兩國心理戰讓消息滿天飛，也讓台股上沖下洗，大玩雲霄飛車，從開高報復性反彈600多點，隨後卻跳水收黑，高低震盪近千點，終場跌110點，收在32,612點，台積電收平盤，但記憶體族群慘遭血洗，金融股相對抗跌，扮演撐盤要角。

川普宣布延後5天打擊伊朗能源設施，被伊朗洗臉，是在玩心理戰，美伊戰火之上，兩方消息滿天飛，也讓國際股市和台股，如同川劇變臉，一變再變。

民視記者唐詩晴：「週二台股大洗三溫暖，開盤報復性反彈600多點後，又翻黑下探，失守3萬3千點大關，盤中高低點震盪926點。」

從最高33,361點，直接下探到最低32,434點，原以為低接買盤挹注，指數翻紅回神，沒想到終場前2分鐘，來個回馬槍，再跳水，收跌110點，來到32,612點，成交值再萎縮到6,643億。

統一投顧總經理 廖婉婷：「昨天台股上市融資減肥了52億，實際上相對3/4和3/9，減肥幅度來看我覺得不夠，所以我建議可以關注幾個點，第一個就是能否成功站上月線，這個就會有機會收復短多，第二個的話就是油價有沒有辦法繼續往下跌，因為如果美伊戰事緩和的話，油價應該要持續的回落，第三個就是看美債殖利率，因為之前的油價引發通膨的擔憂，實際上美債殖利率是反彈到4.4%以上的，那昨天雖然有一點回落，可是回落的幅度也不是太重，所以如果美債跟油價都沒有辦法成功的往下探底的話，那我認為通膨的擔憂還是存在，它就會壓抑指數。」

大型權值股漲跌互見，台積電收平盤1,810元。（圖／民視新聞）

早盤領頭權值股，終場漲跌互見，台積電收盤遭壓摜低，平盤1,810元作收，金融抗跌，記憶體全面狂殺，兩度列處置股的旺宏，也亮綠燈，IC載板、PCB族群，疲軟不振，獲利了結賣壓明顯，航空股起飛，國防部採購，確定無人機商機，但除了長榮航太收紅、為升漲停，其他像中光電、雷虎收黑，隨著布蘭特原油，再度漲破100美元，台塑化、台塑強漲收紅。

統一投顧分析師 陳立委：「現階段還是受到戰事，這種非經濟因素的干擾，那我們投顧是預估，指數可能會在於月線以及季線，這邊做一個區間震盪整理，除非說戰事能夠緩解。」

國際油價仍牽動金融市場變化。（圖／民視新聞）

後續台股能

news.Title: 戰事攪亂台股漲勢 投顧點名軍工.能源受惠
news.Content:
[ 非凡新聞 ]記者謝旻蓁,攝影詹明樺

台股近期陷入回檔格局，在美伊戰局持續膠著下，國內投顧業者分析，軍工和能源類股將成為首波受惠族群。另一方面，近期多家外資上修國內高價股目標價，像是花旗環球上調穎崴股價至10000元，高盛也將信驊調升至15000元。專家分析，國內企業獲利基本面不變，待戰事平息後，資金將重回市場。

中東戰火未見明朗，拖累全球股市走勢，尤其科技股表現相對疲弱，市場持續觀望合適投資標的，專家分析，從基本面和產業面來看，軍工和能源是值得留意的族群。

群益投顧專案經理 董嘉青：「這次美國導彈用的比較兇，所以後面他們一定會去補導彈的庫存，美國有一些無人機的公司，也看到有一些商機，能源它生產中斷，它要恢復生產，其實按照第一次波灣跟第二次波灣戰爭經驗，這個時間都比你想像的長，油價會維持在相對高檔一段時間，假如三到六個月的話，這對能源公司的表現就會非常有幫助。」

另一方面，外資紛紛上調國內高價股目標價，其中花旗環球率先將穎崴股價推升至1萬元，主要是樂觀看待大宗客戶訂單能見度穩固，以及伺服器CPU與AI ASIC需求強勁；另一方面，摩根大通上調股王信驊股價至14000元，高盛更喊出15000元，顯示外資仍樂觀看待企業強勁獲利能力。專家分析，未來兩年國內AI相關企業，基本面依然穩健，一旦戰事平息，資金將回流至上中下游供應鏈，有望進一步挹注台股動能。

群益投顧總經理 范振鴻：「AI供應鏈相關的上游部分，我覺得應該都是不錯的買進機會，比如說向上游的材料，或者是CCL等等，或者是晶圓代工，反正這些相關的基本上在今年、明年的接單，大致上除了確定之外的，都還有持續調高的空間，去年11月我們覺得今年整年的預估獲利是成長21%，現在拉高到28%。」

專家點出，現階段台股的整理區間與時間，主要取決於油價的波動，若油價逐漸跌破百美元，對台股走勢將帶來正面影響，且若美國私募信貸問題有望緩解，第二季台股將呈現盤整，也會是投資人進場布局的好時機。（記者 謝旻蓁、詹明樺／台北採訪報導）

news.Title: 瞄準AI.先進封裝 光罩：今年營收看增雙位數
news.Content:
[ 非凡新聞 ]

AI需求持續升溫，帶動半導體供應鏈全面轉熱，其中關鍵材料「光罩」，成為市場新焦點。尤其在台積電擴大晶圓製造2.0帶動下，供應鏈分工更趨緊密，甚至出現訂單外溢效應。台灣光罩，就表示目前產能已經滿載，今年營收可望有兩位數成長，重回成長軌道。

在半導體製程中，光罩就好像一台印刷機，把電路圖透過光影「刻」在晶圓上。如今，在AI的帶動下，也熱了起來。

台灣光罩總經理陳立惇：「今年半導體的發展是非常好，根據市調報告顯示，會超過26%以上的成長。比較幸運的是，我們光罩在去年年底、今年年初的時候，也開始投入AI相關供應鏈，包括一些先進封裝製程的大尺寸光罩製造。」

先進製程加上大尺寸光罩，是台灣光罩今年成長的重要動能；同時也看到龍頭廠台積電擴大晶圓製造2.0，讓供應鏈分工更加緊密。在產能吃緊與成本考量下，部分訂單開始外溢。

台灣光罩總經理陳立惇：「我們今年會持續佈建AI這方面的關鍵產能，跟我們重要的客戶，這方面的一些亮點或是一些營收效益呈現，應該是在今年下半年到明年的時候會比較多。」

光罩樂看本業獲利，可望逐季成長；再加上旗下轉投資結構重整，以及先進封裝產能等三大引擎帶動下，正從過去的「配角」，逐步走向關鍵角色。

台灣光罩 總經理陳立惇：「我們在過去幾年，有積極投資佈局在一些關鍵的產能上；所以如果以談稼動率來講，稼動率是滿滿的，超過90%。今年開始，我們會加多在12吋，尤其90奈米以下。」

要從成熟製程轉向高階製程，同時也跟隨晶圓廠，包括台積電、世界先進等客戶的擴廠腳步。

世界先進董事長 方略：「過去這些年來，我們在高效能電源管理晶片和分離式元件，都有投入很多的研發資源；產能的建置到2026年，這樣子一個投資跟投入，現在看起來，我們2026年也正在發酵需求，全年看起來也是會非常健康的一個成長。」

從成熟製程打底到切入AI先進封裝，台灣半導體供應鏈不只關鍵地位拉升，營運也將重回成長軌道。（記者 朱月英、姚尹哲／新竹採訪報導）

news.Title: 不只拼硬體！台灣「機器人新戰法」：複製台積電精神打全球
news.Content:
在機器人產業競爭升溫之際，台灣正嘗試從單一硬體製造，轉向整合應用與系統的生態系布局。機器人跨域共創聯盟會長、凌群電腦總經理劉瑞隆今（24）日表示，台灣不只要聚焦在「實體機器人」，目標在於整合實體機器人、開源技術與智慧代理系統等，發展完整應用場景，並以「台積電精神」為借鏡，推動台灣機器人產業邁向全球市場。

劉瑞隆指出，不只要聚焦在「實體機器人」，也需要包含虛擬世界中的人偶、智慧電腦的agent，希望將這些技術賦予不同的專長領域，例如交通、醫療、旅遊等，讓其發展成智慧電腦人。

他強調，機器人與智慧代理若缺乏主控系統，將無法有效運作，因此聯盟要形成一個完整的ecosystem，而非單點技術突破。

在產業定位上，劉瑞隆認為台灣具備關鍵優勢，包括成熟的製造能力與已接軌國際的供應鏈體系，聯盟會員中亦涵蓋大型企業，並已連結美國供應鏈。

他表示，透過跨域共創模式，可望整合台灣強項，在全球機器人競爭中取得一席之地。

他也引用台積電經驗指出，「大家想到台積電會感到驕傲，因為是台灣之光，但台積電的機台並不是自己全部生產，而是來自全球供應鏈，但台積電能做到世界第一，甚至領先十年、二十年，這是值得我們學習的。」

他表示，台灣機器人產業應學習「台積電精神」，從POC（概念驗證）到POS（規模化落地），將應用真正推向市場並產生影響力。

此外，劉瑞隆提醒，當機器人進入生產現場後，「人機協同」將成為新常態，既有的工業工程流程與動作設計需重新調整。

他指出，一家公司可能有一半以上是人機協同的工作型態，例如100人中，有30至40個是電腦人，其餘為真人。在此趨勢下，他認為，除了硬體與軟體開發，行為模型與人機互動機制也將成為重要研究領域，並呼籲導入產學資源，共同推動相關研究與論文發展。

劉瑞隆強調，聯盟成立近一年，目標在於整合實體機器人、開源技術與智慧代理系統，發展完整應用場景，並以「台積電精神」為借鏡，推動台灣機器人產業邁向全球市場。

news.Title: 《台北股市》台股最慘恐下探至26,000點 專家最新看法 2件事定成敗
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】美伊戰爭消息面持續紛亂，群益金融集團24日舉辦「2026年第二季投資展望說明會」，展望第二季，群益投顧總經理范振鴻預估，在中東戰火延續下，台股單季維持區間震盪機率高，預期單季指數將維持在31,000～34,000點盤整，但若戰事延遲至6月底結束，加上美國私募信貸問題擴大，則指數拉回幅度將會較深，最壞情況單季指數低點恐下探26,000點。

范振鴻指出，美伊戰況、美國私募信貸隱憂將是影響後續行情的兩大關鍵，並以三情境分析後續市場走勢，情境一，若戰爭1～3個月停火達成協議，如川普預期在4月9日前結束戰爭，加上美國私募信貸問題不擴大，則金融市場可望迅速回穩，台股恢復多頭，加權指數第二季再創新高機率高，年底前更有機會上看40,000點。

情境二，若戰爭在3～6個月結束或軍事衝突淡化，且美國私募信貸問題並未擴大，則預期前三個月國際油價高檔震盪、金融市場反應激烈，低點恐出現在第二季，但後續隨美國啟動降息與期中選舉效應，將推升股市緩步震盪上漲。

至於情境三，若戰爭時間超過6個月以上才結束，國際油價衝高引發通膨改變美國利率政策，甚至引發停滯型通膨，中東地區戰火影響全球資金動向，或美國私募信貸問題惡化擴散，股市會轉為空頭市場，第二季大幅下跌後震盪走低，並直至第三季才見低點，第四季出現反彈。（新聞來源：工商即時 鄭郁平）

news.Title: 《陸股》滬指漲1.78%、日K翻紅；恆指重回25000點
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-24 17:23:50 新聞中心 發佈

中國滬深兩市三大指數今(24)日集體開高，指數盤初震盪回檔，盤中反彈波動，臨近尾盤大幅上漲，三大指數最終同步收漲。盤面上，電力、環保板塊漲幅居前，醫藥、鋼鐵、地產、有色、化工、半導體等板塊均上揚，軍貿概念、綠電、光通信、創新藥概念等活躍。整體來看，兩市個股呈普漲態勢，上漲個股逾5,100檔，滬指終場收漲1.78%，日K翻紅。

上證指數今日收於3881.28點，上漲68.00點或1.78%；深證成指收於13536.56點，上漲191.05點或1.43%；創業板指收於3251.55點，上漲16.33點或0.50%；科創50收於1290.79點，上漲29.35點或2.33%；中證A500收於5542.36點，上漲75.46點或1.38%。

成交量方面，滬市今成交9,314億元(人民幣,下同)，深市成交1兆1,514億元，兩市成交額2兆828億元，較前一交易日量縮約14%。

從盤面上來看，兵裝重組概念、電力、環保設備板塊漲幅居前；油氣開採及服務板塊下跌。

港股方面，香港恆生指數今日開盤上漲1.55%，呈現開高走高行情，最終重回25000點。盤面上，恆生科技指數漲2.51%，大型科網股普遍上漲，美團、騰訊漲逾3%，阿里巴巴漲近3%，百度、快手、嗶哩嗶哩漲逾2%。恆指終場收於25063.71點，上漲681.24點或2.79%，日K翻紅。

《陸股》滬指跌0.85%、日K連四黑；恆指日K翻黑

《陸股》滬指失守4100點、跌0.81%；恆指日K連三黑

資料來源- MoneyDJ理財網

news.Title: 外資3月狂砍8166億 記憶體「失憶」大逃殺｜Yahoo財經掃描
news.Content:
隨著中東戰事出現降溫訊號，川普宣稱美伊已展開「具實質成果」的談話，並將對伊朗電力與能源設施的攻擊延後5天，帶動油價大跌、風險情緒回暖，四大指數走強。道瓊工業指數上漲1.38%，那斯達克指數上漲1.38%，標普500指數上漲1.15%，費城半導體指數上漲1.34%。個股表現方面，科技股率先反彈，Palantir大漲6.74%，特斯拉反彈3.5%，美超微回升5.11%，台積電ADR上漲2.80%，但美光逆勢下跌4.38%，反映市場對其擴產與資本支出升高的疑慮未消。

亞股表現同步回神，日股上漲1.43%，韓股走揚2.74%，港股強彈2.79%，上證指數上漲1.78%。

回到台股，今(24)日加權指數終場下跌110.26點，收在32,612.24點，成交金額6,656.06億元，盤中一度大漲逾600點後翻黑，呈現開高走低格局； 台積電(2330) 早盤一度衝上1,850元，但尾盤漲幅收斂，終場收平盤1,810元，成為權值撐盤主力； 鴻海(2317) 、 聯發科(2454) 收黑。整體盤面結構仍偏弱，記憶體股成為重災區，載板族群跌勢沉重，AI族群則是有零星個股表現抗跌，突顯資金雖守住指數，卻持續從電子題材族群撤出。

📝盤點熱門股：群創、力積電、主動統一台股增長

今日熱門成交股方面，前五檔全數收跌。 群創(3481) 以38.64萬張居冠，主因近期股價震盪明顯、連日躍居成交量王，今早又隨大盤由強翻弱，吸引短線買賣持續進出；記憶體股則由 力積電(6770) 與 華邦電(2344) 同步擠進前五，股價走弱，主要反映DRAM現貨價拉回、市場對記憶體「超級循環」是否提前見頂的疑慮升溫，加上美系外資下修相關個股評等與目標價，帶動賣壓出籠、成交放大。

至於ETF方面， 主動統一台股增長(00981A) 與 凱基台灣TOP50(009816) 表現同樣疲弱，不過在台股盤中一度大漲逾300點帶動下，ETF族群整體明顯出量，市值型與主動式產品皆吸引資金進出，交投維持熱絡。

📌三大法人買賣超

三大法人今日合計賣超200.77億元，籌碼面偏空。其中外資賣超121.87億元，持續站在調節一方；自營商也賣超104.84億元，顯示內資操作同樣偏向保守。相較之下，投信買超25.94億元，成為三大法人中唯一偏多力量。整體來看，今天仍是外資、自營商聯手提款，投信獨撐買盤的格局。

✨本日議題股：BBU概念股

本日盤面聚焦BBU概念股，受惠AI伺服器對備援電力模組需求升溫，加上輝達GB200、GB300平台將BBU列為重要配置，相關族群再度獲資金點火；盤面上以 新盛力(4931) 表現最為強勢，早盤亮燈攻頂， 加百裕(3323) 、 順達(3211) 、 興能高(6558) 同步大漲， AES-KY(6781) 、 台達電(2308) 與 系統電(5309) 也聯袂走揚，顯示市場對電力架構題材仍高度買單。另一方面，BBU熱潮也帶旺周邊個股輪番表態...... 點我看更多

👥勞部抓慣老闆：

本日話題聚焦勞動部最低工資違規開罰，去年共有153件違規、開罰330萬元，其中以支援服務業最多，製造業、批發零售業與住宿餐飲業也都上榜。勞動部指出，最常見違規職業是保全人員，主因多與工時換算及薪資結構認定錯誤有關，後續...... 點我看更多

🔭重大財經事件展望

周三國際財經事件，先看美國市場，焦點落在美國2月進出口價格與去年第四季經常帳，市場將藉此觀察貿易成本變化與外部收支情況；歐洲方面，英國將公布2月CPI、德國則發布3月Ifo企業信心指數，兩項數據都將牽動市場對歐洲景氣與貨幣政策走向的判斷；亞太區則聚焦日本央行公布上次會議記要、韓國3月消費者信心指數，以及澳洲2月CPI；港澳方面，港股進入財報揭露高峰，泡泡瑪特、安踏體育、快手將公布2025年第四季與全年業績，預料將成為市場關注重點。

台灣方面，國內焦點包括遠東商銀與台灣高檢署簽署MOU、高雄國稅局記者會、台經院發布3月景氣動向調查報告，以及台灣國際工具機展、台北國際自行車大展、台灣國際運動及健身展、亞太國際風力發電展等大型產業活動登場；企業動態則以股東會及法說會為主，股東會有 大眾控(3701) ，法說會可留意幾家指標大廠，包括 華南金(2880) 、 鈊象(3293) 、 東洋(4105) 、 91APP*-KY(6741) 、 美利達(9914) 、 巨大(9921) 與 裕融(9941) 等；此外， 神基(3005) 、 全國電(6281) 、 億豐(8464) 等除息， 有成精密(4949) 、 衛司特(6894) 同步除息，另 宏碁遊戲(6908) 轉上市、 民盛(7811) 轉上櫃，短線資金流向與股價反應也值得留意。

市場變化難捉摸？面對股市的大起大落，總會有看不懂、跟不上、難決策的時刻！ Yahoo財經掃描 每個台股交易日晚間6點上菜，整理市場最重要的財經熱點，從國際財經趨勢、產業焦點到個股異動，迅速掌握投資脈動，帶你快人一步，掌握市場先機。

註：加權指數成交金額含盤後定價，不含零股、鉅額、拍賣及標購

（核稿編輯：劉凱銘）

news.Title: 第二季台股展望 群益投顧：美伊局勢居關鍵 指數區間26000-34000點
news.Content:
▲群益金鼎證券總經理李文柱（右三）、投顧董事長蔡明彥（右二）、總經理范振鴻（右一）等出席投資說明會。

群益金融集團今（24）日舉辦「第二季投資展望說明會」，由群益投顧總經理范振鴻及投顧團隊剖析全球經濟展望及台股投資趨勢。群益投顧預估第二季台股指數區間為31,000-34,000點。若美伊戰事延遲到6月底結束時，指數區間修正為26,000 -32,000點。

展望第二季，市場策略觀點分三種情境：美以2月底轟炸伊朗後，戰事變化是全球與台灣第二季金融市場最大變數，擬定投資策略時宜先冷靜判斷戰爭變化、進行情境分析再擬定因應的投資策略。

情境1：若戰爭1-3個月停火達成協議，而且美國私募信貸問題不擴大，金融市場迅速回穩，台股恢復原先多頭格局，AI股領軍，2026年底前指數上看40,000點。

情境2：若戰爭能在3-6個月結束或是軍事衝突淡化，而且美國私募信貸問題不擴大，前3個月國際油價高檔震盪，金融市場反應激烈，低點可能出現在第二季，隨著美國啟動降息與期中選舉效應推升股市緩步震盪上漲。

情境3：若戰爭時間超過6個月以上才結束，國際油價衝高引發通膨改變美國利率政策，甚至引發停滯型通膨(滯脹)，中東地區戰火影響全球資金動向，或美國私募信貸問題惡化擴散，股市會轉為空頭市場，第二季大幅下跌後震盪走低，直到第三季才見低點，第四季出現反彈。

預測市場平台Polymarket資料顯示：美伊戰爭於6月底前結束機率達80%，全球股市可望有驚無險；當然國際政治局勢詭譎多變，加上美國、伊朗世仇無解，不能排除未來各種非理性的變數，資金控管可能是最重要的策略。

地緣政治不確定性升溫與美國私募信貸問題導致市場風險偏好下降影響，操作核心應以風險控管優先、資金效率最大化為原則因應系統性風險。當市場急跌時，恐慌性賣壓是相對理想的進場時點；反之，若出現快速反彈則應適度提高出場紀律，此階段操作節奏速度以短週期資金運作為主。

籌碼面穩定性成為選股重要依據：建議優先關注成交量相對溫和，但具備投信持續買盤支撐，且股價能維持區間穩定的個股。此類標的通常具備內資防守力道，在市場震盪期間相對抗跌，並於反彈階段具備較佳延續性。

整體而言，2026年可能是多空易位的一年，市場處於震盪交錯階段，操作重點不是預測戰爭結果或點數絕對位置，而是掌握節奏與控制風險。市場資金豐沛，即使美伊戰事曠日廢時，甚至重創全球經濟與企業獲利，但根據新冠疫情、俄烏戰爭、對等關稅…等經驗，投資機會是留給有準備(資金)的人。

產業部分，電子產業投資焦點；在AI驅動下，2026年全球半導體產值仍將維持雙位數增長，台積電(2330 TT)是成長動能最高的先進製程絕對領先者，營運動能強勁。先進封裝設備昂貴且認證期長，資本支出門檻高 (Capex Barrier)，大廠具優勢，小廠難以跨越，投資首選以龍頭廠日月光(3711 TT)為主。另台積電不斷開出新產能，設備、廠務、耗材等業者可望受惠半導體廠擴大資本投資，志聖(2467 TT)、閎康(3587 TT)、聖暉*(5536 TT)、鴻勁(7769 TT)、昇陽半導體(8028 TT)等業者未來業績看旺。

AI server是重要需求重心，受惠於被動元件漲價趨勢成形，龍頭廠國巨*(2327 TT)可望受惠。除了伺服器需求的增長，更高的算力代表更高的功率需求與散熱要求，營運受惠者包台達電(2308 TT)、奇鋐(3017 TT)等。

AI需求的持續，過去包括組裝、機櫃、滑軌業者業績當然可望持續成長。然而AI需求的排擠效應也逐漸發酵，2H25開始的記憶體價格大漲就是最明顯副作用，因此價格敏感度極高的PC、手機、消費電子等需求將面臨極大的成本壓力。在產品價格不得不漲的情況下，需求必然下滑。而華碩(2357 TT)伺服器的成長的挹注可望超越PC需求減少的負面因素。因此在股價來到合理的評價水準後，反而出現適合的投資價位。

傳產部分，中東戰火綿延，投資策略聚焦題材兼具殖利率概念(1)美以伊戰爭突顯貨櫃產業題材性：供給面，2026年新船投入增速有望放緩；此外，依照以哈停戰經驗來看，即便以美伊戰爭在短期內停止，但彼此關係仍緊張，航商將持續謹慎觀察，2026年紅海復航機率大幅降低。需求面，05/2026開始進入傳統需求旺季，隨著供給及需求的不確定性減少，旺季運價表現可期。重點股，長榮(2603 TT)、陽明(2609 TT)。

(2) 520軍工概念：2026年台灣國防預算將拉高至GDP的3.32%，驅使未來國防預算重點從過往的戰鬥機，轉向成無人機、潛艦、通訊產品。1.25兆元的強化防衛韌性及不對稱戰力特別預算近期已進入立法院審議。重點股，長榮航太(2645 TT)、豐達科(3004 TT)、晟田(4541 TT)、jpp-KY(5284 TT)、駐龍(4572 TT)、漢翔(2634 TT)。

(3)金融、營建具殖利率題材：看好金融與營建獲利穩健成長及高現金殖利率概念。重點股，長虹(5534 TT)、華固(2548 TT)、遠雄(5522 TT)、達麗(6177 TT)、兆豐金(2886 TT)、富邦金(2881 TT)、永豐金(2890 TT)、中信金(2891 TT)。

(4) 2026世足賽加惠運動休閒產業：國際主要運動用品及服飾品牌廠庫存水位健康且預期消費回升，對2026年的銷售展望樂觀。此外，2026年世足賽在美國舉辦，預期帶來的經濟效益為近年最大，有利於運動用品的銷售成長。重點股：聚陽(1477 TT)、儒鴻(1476 TT)、柏文(8462 TT)、世界健身-KY(2762 TT)。

news.Title: 台積電慈善基金會去年足跡擴及海外，張淑芬透露張忠謀腳傷好轉、心情佳
news.Content:
【財訊快報／記者李純君報導】台積電(2330)創辦人張忠謀夫人張淑芬，今日針對業界關心張忠謀的健康，表示，他的腳有比較好，兩個人都蠻健康的。而針對與NVIDIA執行長黃仁勳表示，張淑芬表示，我們是很好的朋友，他每次來台灣都一定會到我家。張淑芬表示，台積電去年運動會，張忠謀因身體不適缺席，那是因為他當時腳不舒服，走路不方便，最近腳有比較好，且他心情蠻開心的。而針對黃仁勳與他們兩的交情，張淑芬表示，是很好的朋友，他每次來台灣都一定會到張忠謀家，也喜歡張淑芬煮的麻婆豆腐。

台積電慈善基金會志工人數達到2萬3053人，去年度志工總時數13萬6065小時，估計有56萬7000人次受益，覆蓋臺灣、大陸、熊本與美國。身為基金會董事長的張淑芬表示，去年最大成果是走到台灣之外做公益，足跡遍及日本、中國和馬來西亞。台積電慈善基金會投入的公益有3大主軸，教育培力、健康長壽、環境保育。

news.Title: 張淑芬：張忠謀腳好多了　黃仁勳曾一天來回台美探視
news.Content:
（中央社記者張建中台北2026年03月24日電）台積電慈善基金會董事長張淑芬今天出席基金會公益記者會時說，台積電創辦人張忠謀的腳有比較好了，他們兩人都很健康，還透露輝達執行長黃仁勳曾一天內搭乘飛機往返台灣和美國探視張忠謀。

台積電慈善基金會今天舉辦公益記者會，張淑芬現身分享基金會去年做公益的成果，她受訪說，基金會成果不用與張忠謀分享，「他知道我出去，回來就回來了」。

台積電去年運動會，張忠謀因身體不適缺席，張淑芬表示，張忠謀當時腳不舒服，走路不方便，最近腳有比較好。張忠謀心情蠻開心的，「至少我們都很健康」。

張淑芬表示，輝達（NVIDIA）執行長黃仁勳有探視張忠謀，黃仁勳為了探望張忠謀，有一次單日搭乘飛機往返台灣和美國。

黃仁勳先前預告，今年6月將來台參加電腦展，媒體問是否會與黃仁勳見面、一起吃飯，張淑芬說，「我們是很好的朋友」，吃飯很簡單，他如果在台灣，應該會約，不用計畫，「他每次來台灣都一定會到我家」，還透露黃仁勳喜歡她煮的麻婆豆腐，味道重的。

三大法人賣超前十大個股，依類別分，6檔電子股、1檔金融股及3檔傳產股；依股價分，1檔百元股、4檔中價位股及5檔銅板股；賣超張數方面，5檔賣超上萬張，榜首為記憶體族群的力積電(6770)，賣超8萬9808張，群創(3481)、同為記憶體族群的華邦電(2344)名列二、三名，分別賣超4萬6502張及賣超3萬6713張。第四及第五名為友達(2409)、台聚(1304)，其他依序為仁寶(2324)、臺企銀(2834)、陽明(2609)、長興(1717)及京元電子(2449)。

news.Title: 《台北股市》三大法人24日賣超前十大 瞄準記憶體 2檔砍破10萬張
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股24日加權指數收32612.24點，跌110.26點或0.34%，總成交值6846.95億元。三大法人賣超200.77億元，其中外資賣超121.87億元，投信、自營商分別買超25.95億元及賣超104.85億元。

三大法人賣超前十大個股，依類別分，6檔電子股、1檔金融股及3檔傳產股；依股價分，1檔百元股、4檔中價位股及5檔銅板股；賣超張數方面，5檔賣超上萬張，榜首為記憶體族群的力積電(6770)，賣超8萬9808張，群創(3481)、同為記憶體族群的華邦電(2344)名列二、三名，分別賣超4萬6502張及賣超3萬6713張。第四及第五名為友達(2409)、台聚(1304)，其他依序為仁寶(2324)、臺企銀(2834)、陽明(2609)、長興(1717)及京元電子(2449)。

至於前十大個股中賣超金額前三大，則為力積電(6770)56.31億元、華邦電(2344)36.53億元、京元電子(2449)12.52億元。

●三大法人24日賣超前十名：

1.力積電(6770)收62.70元，跌6.40元或9.26%，成交量36.97萬張，三大法人賣超8萬9808張、估56.31億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超14萬2849張、估93.8億元。

2.群創(3481)收24.50元，跌0.50元或2.00%，成交量38.85萬張，三大法人賣超4萬6502張、估11.39億元，由前一交易日買超6894張轉為賣超。

3.華邦電(2344)收99.50元，跌8.00元或7.44%，成交量22.28萬張，三大法人賣超3萬6713張、估36.53億元，由前一交易日買超5715張轉為賣超。

4.友達(2409)收14.65元，跌0.20元或1.35%，成交量7.57萬張，三大法人賣超2萬5457張、估3.73億元，為連5日賣超，累計賣超11萬1757張、估17.04億元。

5.台聚(1304)收15.35元，跌1.20元或7.25%，成交量5.99萬張，三大法人賣超1萬197張、估1.57億元，由前一交易日買超2663張轉為賣超。

6.仁寶(2324)收31.70元，跌1.00元或3.06%，成交量2.49萬張，三大法人賣超8311張、估2.63億元，由連2日累計買超7480張轉為賣超。

7.臺企銀(2834)收15.05元，漲0.20元或1.35%，成交量2.38萬張，三大法人賣超5433張、估0.82億元，為連17日賣超，累計賣超26萬9148張、估40.92億元。

8.陽明(2609)收52.40元，跌0.40元或0.76%，成交量1.97萬張，三大法人賣超4867張、估2.55億元，為連4日賣超，累計賣超2萬936張、估11.39億元。

9.長興(1717)收60.20元，跌1.80元或2.90%，成交量3.23萬張，三大法人賣超4708張、估2.83億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超1萬9588張、估12.06億元。

10.京元電子(2449)收278.50元，跌7.50元或2.62%，成交量1.63萬張，三大法人賣超4495張、估12.52億元，為連4日賣超，累計賣超2萬3244張、估68.48億元。

●前三大個股近五日籌碼：

3.華邦電(2344)三大法人累計賣超6萬7266張，其中外資賣超12萬767張、投信買超6萬2524張、自營商賣超9023張；融資減3707張、融券減9728張，券資比0.20%。
