news.Title: 《台北股市》外資24日買超前十大 記憶體止血了？旺宏、南亞回頭搶
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股24日加權指數收32612.24點，跌110.26點或0.34%，總成交值6846.95億元。三大法人賣超200.77億元，其中外資賣超121.87億元，投信、自營商分別買超25.95億元及賣超104.85億元。

外資買超前十大個股，依類別分，4檔電子股、3檔金融股及3檔傳產股；依股價分，2檔百元股、2檔中價位股及6檔銅板股；買超張數方面，5檔買超上萬張，榜首為南亞科(2408)，買超1萬3066張，大聯大(3702)、新興(2605)名列二、三名，分別買超1萬2925張及買超1萬1815張。第四及第五名為南亞(1303)、旺宏(2337)，其他依序為第一金(2892)、長榮航(2618)、兆豐金(2886)、元大金(2885)及瑞軒(2489)。

至於前十大個股中買超金額前三大，則為南亞科(2408)28.29億元、旺宏(2337)13.94億元、大聯大(3702)11.72億元。

●外資24日買超前十名：

1.南亞科(2408)收216.50元，跌6.50元或2.91%，成交量14.97萬張，外資買超1萬3066張、估28.29億元，為連2日買超，累計買超1萬5764張、估34.31億元。

2.大聯大(3702)收90.70元，股價持平，成交量3.05萬張，外資買超1萬2925張、估11.72億元，為連7日買超，累計買超7萬7657張、估70.24億元。

3.新興(2605)收41.70元，漲1.85元或4.64%，成交量4.92萬張，外資買超1萬1815張、估4.93億元，由連2日累計賣超1萬1206張轉為買超。

4.南亞(1303)收72.30元，跌1.50元或2.03%，成交量5.19萬張，外資買超1萬307張、估7.45億元，由連6日累計賣超6萬7880張轉為買超。

5.旺宏(2337)收136.50元，跌12.00元或8.08%，成交量4.03萬張，外資買超1萬213張、估13.94億元，由連2日累計賣超6514張轉為買超。

6.第一金(2892)收28.55元，漲0.45元或1.60%，成交量2.03萬張，外資買超9539張、估2.72億元，由前一交易日賣超4046張轉為買超。

7.長榮航(2618)收35.30元，漲0.45元或1.29%，成交量3.23萬張，外資買超9498張、估3.35億元，為連7日買超，累計買超6萬4456張、估22.57億元。

8.兆豐金(2886)收38.85元，漲0.50元或1.30%，成交量1.94萬張，外資買超9395張、估3.65億元，由連3日累計賣超1萬3492張轉為買超。

9.元大金(2885)收45.10元，漲0.90元或2.04%，成交量3.16萬張，外資買超9312張、估4.20億元，由前一交易日賣超2143張轉為買超。

10.瑞軒(2489)收31.50元，跌3.30元或9.48%，成交量9.27萬張，外資買超9105張、估2.87億元，為連2日買超，累計買超9252張、估2.92億元。

●前三大個股近五日籌碼：

1.南亞科(2408)三大法人累計賣超7萬4319張，其中外資賣超6萬2066張、投信賣超7409張、自營商賣超4843張；融資減9602張、融券增353張，券資比0.33%。

2.大聯大(3702)三大法人累計買超5萬5440張，其中外資買超5萬5365張、投信買超1863張、自營商賣超1788張；融資減620張、融券增238張，券資比10.64%。

3.新興(2605)三大法人累計買超1萬2325張，其中外資買超1萬3424張、投信買超1張、自營商賣超1099張；融資增1451張、融券增66張，券資比3.07%。

註：台股總成交值不含鉅額交易；個股買賣超僅24日未加計鉅額交易；資券統計截至前一交易日。(編輯：龍彩霖)

news.Title: 《韓股》韓股匯雙揚 外資大賣超
news.Content:
【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】受美以聯手攻打伊朗的戰爭，可能很快就會結束的預期激勵，前一個交易日重挫超過6%的韓股，24日一開盤就漲破5600點整數關卡，最後上漲148.18點或2.74%，收5553.92點，盤中最高一度來到5643點。另外，韓元走升，外資大幅賣超兩兆韓元。

分析師指出，股市因為TACO(川普總是臨陣退縮)而大幅度走升，但地緣政治的不確定性依舊存在，故韓股的漲幅跟著慢慢收斂。伊朗否認跟美國交談過，荷姆茲海峽這條重要的石油運輸通道，實際上來說還是關閉的，投資人對未來還是保持謹慎。

科技大廠三星電子漲1.44%，競爭對手SK海力士更是大漲5.68%。SK海力士公司宣布，將斥資12兆韓元，約79億美元，從荷蘭ASML採購先進的生產設備，滿足日益增加的記憶體晶片需求。根據了解，這批EUV曝光機計畫最晚將在2027年12月之前交貨，生產下一代記憶體產品。

現代汽車漲1.44%，起亞卻大跌2.4%。韓國交通部24日宣布，擔心影響到行車安全，將在韓國全境召回，約5.8萬輛現代汽車旗下的混合動力休旅車Palisade。

韓元兌一美元在1,495.2價位附近，韓元大幅升值22.1元。

news.Title: 川普TACO也喚不回！三大法人再捲200億逃離...台股下挫110點　股民嘆：大媽之力也沒力了
news.Content:
[ FTNN新聞網 ]記者張書翰／綜合報導

儘管美國總統川普（Donald Trump）再度急轉彎，表示美伊雙方已進行具建設性對話，並宣布延後對伊朗能源設施打擊，市場預期中東衝突降溫，卻依舊無法說服外資進場。台股加權指數今（24）日開高走低，終場以32612.24點作收，下跌110.26點，跌幅0.34%，成交金額為6656.06億元。據證交所盤後公布籌碼動向，外資今賣超約121.86億元，自營商（合計）賣超約104.84億元；僅投信買超約25.94億元，三大法人合計賣超約200.76億元。

觀察集中市場三大法人今天籌碼動向，外資及陸資（不包括外資自營商）賣超約121.86億元；自營商（合計）賣超約104.84億元，其中自營商自行買賣部分，賣超約16.98億元，避險部分賣超約87.85億元；僅投信買超約25.94億元，三大法人合計賣超約200.76億元。

電子權值股收盤部分，台積電（2330）維持平盤，收1810元；鴻海（2317）下跌1元，跌幅0.51%，收195元；台達電（2308）上漲5元，漲幅0.35%，收1415元；聯發科（2454）下跌5元，跌幅0.31%，收1620元；廣達（2382）上漲1.5元，漲幅0.54%，收281.5元。

AI族群方面，指標股英業達（2356）上漲0.3元，漲幅0.72%，收42.25元；緯創（3231）上漲3.5元，漲幅2.79%，收129元；至於其他AI股方面，宏碁（2353）上漲0.15元，漲幅0.56%，收27.15元；技嘉（2376）上漲2.5元，漲幅1.08%，收235元；至於世芯-KY（3661）則下跌80元，跌幅2.51%，收3110元。

對此，股民紛紛表示「大媽之力也沒力了」、「大媽沒錢了」、「怎麼賣這麼少」、「小兒沒東西可以賣了」、「大哥輕鬆撐住」、「結果是自營殺的，笑死」。

◎《FTNN新聞網》提醒您：本資料僅供參考，投資人應獨立判斷，審慎評估並自負投資風險。

news.Title: 台股拚「亞洲那斯達克」金管會擬再放寬創新板3大增資限制
news.Content:
金管會為配合政府擬將台股拚「亞洲那斯達克Nasdaq」，挖掘台灣第一家獨家獸新創企業，針對亞洲創新籌資平台計畫，推動企業掛牌後轉板機制（上櫃轉上市創新板或創新板轉為一般上市櫃公司），擬修法放寬創新板3大增資限制條件。

過往上市上櫃的申請流程繁雜，從申請到通過需花費較長時間，門檻與標準也相對嚴格，使得市面上許多新創公司難以進入市場籌資。為了活絡市場，證交所於2021年籌設「創新板」，只要符合政府產業發展策略，擁有關鍵核心技術，及創新能力（例如物聯網、人工智慧、大數據等新技術之應用）或創新經營模式，不必有獲利必要條件，且縮短新創公司進入資本市場籌資之前置時程，亦鬆綁相關的條件，滿一年符合基本條件，即可轉為一般板的上市股票；之後也放寬創新板投資人的投資人條件限制。

創新板目前有23家 已5家直接轉一般上市

證期局副局長黃仲豪表示，據金管會統計，民國110年至今轉板情況11家上櫃轉上市；從創新板轉一般上市有5家，有創新板轉到上櫃公司有1家。目前創新板共有23家，擬掛牌加入後為29家。黃仲豪說，創新板跟上市櫃一般股只剩要簽風險預告書，以及創新板略多一些監理要求外，其他都一樣了。

金管會證期局今日表示，為完備企業掛牌後板塊轉換之選擇機制，已開放一般板上市公司得申請改列為創新板上市公司，及上櫃公司得申請轉創新板或第一上市，金管會打算配合修正「發行人募集與發行有價證券處理準則」及「外國發行人募集與發行有價證券處理準則」相關規範。

創後板再放寬增資件三限制

同時，此次放寬增資限制，證期局副局長黃仲豪表示，此次擬修法修正重點有三。

一是為因上開申請改列或轉板，而辦理現金增資發行新股以達股權分散標準之案件，得豁免適用募集與發行有價證券計畫具必要性規定；若申請改列或轉板，有關大量閒餘資金限制之規定亦可豁免適用；

二是申報生效期間從21天改為7個營業日；

三是為利企業遵循，本次併將舊有及新增之改列或轉板規範予以整併。

金管會表示，為廣納多方意見以期修正方向臻於周延，除將刊登行政院公報外，亦將於金管會網站刊登法規草案之總說明及條文對照表，各界如有任何意見，請於公告翌日起60日內，於金管會「主管法規查詢系統」網站之「草案預告」網頁內陳述意見或洽詢金管會證期局。

葉憶如／財經特派記者組長，主跑金管會、金控壽險、航空等

news.Title: 《台北股市》群益：Q2台股26～34K震盪 中東局勢成關鍵
news.Content:
【時報記者林資傑台北報導】群益金融集團今（24）日舉辦「2026年第二季投資展望說明會」，由群益投顧總經理范振鴻及投顧團隊剖析全球經濟展望及台股投資趨勢，預估2026年第二季台股指數將於31000～34000點區間震盪，若中東戰事遲至6月底才結束，指數區間修正為26000～32000點。

群益投顧指出，美以2月底轟炸伊朗後，戰事變化成為全球與台灣第二季金融市場最大變數，擬定投資策略時宜先冷靜判斷戰爭變化、進行情境分析再擬定因應的投資策略。目前市場策略觀點分為三種情境。

首先，若是中東戰爭在1～3個月停火達成協議、且美國私募信貸問題不擴大的「情境1」，群益投顧認為金融市場將迅速回穩，台股恢復原先多頭格局，在AI股領軍帶動下，指數2026年底前上看40000點。

若是戰爭能在3～6個月結束或軍事衝突淡化，且美國私募信貸問題不擴大的「情境2」，則前3個月國際油價高檔震盪，金融市場反應激烈，指數低點可能出現在第二季，隨著美國啟動降息與期中選舉效應，推升股市緩步震盪上漲。

若是戰爭時間超過6個月才結束，國際油價衝高引發通膨改變美國利率政策，甚至引發停滯型通膨，中東地區戰火影響全球資金動向，或美國私募信貸問題惡化擴散的「情境3」，股市會轉為空頭市場，第二季大幅下跌後震盪走低，直到第三季才見低點，第四季出現反彈。

預測市場平台Polymarket資料目前顯示，市場預期美伊戰爭在6月底前結束的機率達80％，全球股市可望有驚無險。然國際政治局勢詭譎多變，加上美國、伊朗世仇無解，無法排除未來各種非理性變數，群益投顧提醒資金控管可能是最重要的策略。

盤勢操作與交易策略方面，受地緣政治不確定性升溫與美國私募信貸問題導致市場風險偏好下降影響，群益投顧認為操作核心應以風險控管優先、資金效率最大化為原則，以因應系統性風險。當市場急跌時，恐慌性賣壓是相對理想的進場時點。

反之，若股市出現快速反彈，則應適度提高出場紀律，此階段操作節奏速度以短周期資金運作為主。技術面部分，則應注意多頭時期各天期均線支撐強度，並持續觀察國際市場變數與資金流向，以調整後續操作節奏。

群益投顧指出，籌碼面穩定性成為選股重要依據，建議優先關注成交量相對溫和，但具備投信持續買盤支撐、且股價能維持區間穩定的個股。此類標的通常具備內資防守力道，在市場震盪期間相對抗跌，並於反彈階段具備較佳延續性。

整體而言，群益投顧認為2026年可能是多空易位的一年，市場處於震盪交錯階段，操作重點為掌握節奏與控制風險。由於市場資金豐沛，即使美伊戰事曠日廢時，甚至重創全球經濟與企業獲利，但根據新冠疫情、俄烏戰爭、對等關稅等經驗，投資機會是留給有準備資金的人。

news.Title: 馬斯克落實晶片夢 向台灣開職缺劍指台積電
news.Content:
[ 非凡新聞 ]記者洪芷茵,攝影林家弘

特斯拉執行長馬斯克晶片布局計劃開始啟動，近日更在LinkedIn招募台灣產業人才，劍指一線大廠的核心工程師。業界憂心台灣半導體人才不足，特斯拉挾「馬斯克光環」，恐怕引發新一波人才磁吸效應，讓全球半導體展產業人才競爭更加激烈！

特斯拉業務不只遍及地面範圍，還延伸至太空領域，就連其中的關鍵晶片，執行長馬斯克都加緊佈局，宣布啟動「TeraFab」晶片製造計畫，預計打造2奈米先進晶圓廠。而最新特斯拉官網更釋出人才招募訊息，有意向台灣半導體人才招手，尤其是直指先進製程關鍵核心的「流程整合工程師」崗位。

台灣大學電機博士 張勤煜：「晶圓製造那主要啦，我們就知道半導體的整個最重要的製程，那有分成很多道不同的製程。剛開始跨進來這個領域，他就需要有人，第一個我要很懂這些所謂的製程，比如說黃光是什麼、薄膜是什麼、沉積是什麼，各個不同的製程是什麼，他要能夠做整合的工作，必須是可能是有多年的經驗。」

就是因為，根據馬斯克尋求的人才條件，幾乎直接對標目前在台積電先進封裝，和大型IC設計公司中負責先進節點量產與良率爬升的關鍵人才。而台灣穩居半導體領域的龍頭寶座，頂尖人才自然極具吸引力，不過業界憂心是否將掀起半導體人才流失效應，進而衝擊整體產業？對此專家認為，儘管人才招募訊息將吸引部分人才流動，不過仍難以撼動台灣在半導體產業的優勢地位。

台經院產經資料庫總監 劉佩真：「馬斯克在TeraFab，首先將會是在物理極限跟良率的鴻溝，畢竟2奈米所採取的是GAA的架構是極為複雜。台積電因為擁有在數十年的製程參數優化，跟龐大的生態體系的專利屏障，並非只是單純的一個蓋廠就可以跨越的技術鴻溝。」

產業專家認為，儘管特斯拉具備晶片設計相關技術，不過在量產方面是缺乏經驗，以及完整的封測體系，短期內仍將面臨許多考驗。（記者洪芷茵、陳昱志／台北採訪報導）

news.Title: Should You Buy Micron Stock While It's Under $500?
news.Content:
Most artificial intelligence (AI) development happens inside centralized data centers, where thousands of specialized chips, called graphics processing units (GPUs), deliver the necessary computing power. Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices are two of the world's top GPU suppliers.

Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) provides those chipmakers with a very important component called high-bandwidth memory (HBM), which helps unlock maximum processing speeds from each GPU by facilitating the seamless flow of data. The company is experiencing astronomical demand for its HBM solutions right now, which triggered a near- threefold increase in its revenue during the fiscal 2026 second quarter (ended Feb. 26).

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Micron stock is up by an eye-popping 330% over the last 12 months alone, and it was trading at $444.27 as of the market close on March 19. However, its attractive valuation suggests it could soon rocket past $500, so should investors take this opportunity to buy?

Memory is critical in AI workloads

HBM sits alongside GPUs and other components in the AI data center hardware stack. It stores information until GPUs are ready to process it, so capacity is critical. A low HBM capacity creates bottlenecks, forcing GPUs to pause workloads while they wait to receive more data. This would create a horrible user experience for anyone trying to run AI chatbots or AI agents.

Micron's HBM3E solution for the data center offers 50% more capacity than the competition while consuming 30% less energy, so it's perfect for AI companies seeking the fastest processing speeds at the lowest cost. But Micron is upping the ante with its new HBM4 solution, which offers a further 60% increase in capacity compared to HBM3E, along with a 20% improvement in energy efficiency.

Nvidia is using Micron's HBM4 in its latest Vera Rubin GPUs, which currently lead the industry in terms of AI processing performance.

But Micron's AI opportunity transcends the data center, because it's also a top supplier of memory solutions for personal computers (PCs) and smartphones. The company says PCs with agentic AI capabilities require up to 32 gigabytes of dynamic random access memory, which is twice as much as the average PC. Similarly, around 80% of flagship smartphones from top manufacturers are shipping with at least 12 gigabytes of memory, compared to just 20% of smartphones one year ago. More memory means more revenue for Micron.

Story Continues

Micron's revenue and earnings are soaring

Micron generated a record $23.8 billion in total revenue during its fiscal 2026 second quarter, up 196% from the year-ago period. The result exceeded management's forecast of $18.7 billion by a wide margin.

Micron's cloud memory business (where it accounts for HBM sales) contributed $7.7 billion in revenue, up by 163% year over year. The company's mobile and client segment (where it accounts for PC and smartphone memory sales) also generated $7.7 billion in revenue, but that was up by an even greater 245%.

The soaring demand for AI-related memory solutions has given Micron an incredible amount of pricing power. As a result, its generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) earnings rocketed higher by 756% during the second quarter, coming in at $12.07 per share. Earnings typically drive stock prices, so this number could play a key role in shareholder returns from here. But more on that in a moment.

Micron provided some extremely bullish guidance for the current fiscal 2026 third quarter, which will conclude at the end of May. The company expects to deliver a whopping $33.5 billion in revenue along with earnings of $18.90 per share, representing staggering year-over-year increases of 260% and 1,025%, respectively.

Micron stock still looks cheap

Based on Micron's trailing-12-month earnings of $21.18 per share and its stock price of $444.27, its price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio is just 20.9. That means that despite more than quadrupling over the past year, Micron stock is still cheaper than the S&P 500 index, which trades at a P/E ratio of 24.1, and the Nasdaq-100 index, which trades at a P/E ratio of 30.3.

Micron is also substantially cheaper than Nvidia, which has a P/E ratio of 36.4. The stark valuation gap doesn't make much sense, in my opinion, because Nvidia is using Micron's HBM -- therefore, investors who believe Nvidia will continue selling truckloads of GPUs should be equally bullish on Micron.

Looking ahead, Wall Street's consensus estimates (provided by Yahoo! Finance) suggest Micron could deliver earnings of $36.67 per share during fiscal 2026, followed by $57.31 per share in fiscal 2027. That places its stock at forward P/E ratios of 12.1 and 7.7, respectively.

In other words, Micron stock would have to climb by 171% over the next 18 months just to maintain its current P/E ratio of 20.9, without even factoring in the potential for an even higher valuation. That would translate to a stock price of $1,203, so surpassing $500 might be a mere formality.

However, it's important to remember the AI semiconductor cycle is far from normal. In the past, data center operators would build infrastructure and use it for several years before upgrading it. The capital investment cycle has been reduced to one year (and even less in some cases) because companies like Nvidia are launching new GPUs so quickly.

But even the wealthiest tech giants won't be able to maintain the current pace of infrastructure spending forever, which is something Wall Street is likely factoring into Micron's valuation. Nevertheless, I think $500 will be an easy hurdle to clear from here.

news.Title: 台積電竟成產業瓶頸！大客戶急發聲 PCB、雷射也卡死了
news.Content:
幾年前，博通才認為台積電的產能是「無限的」沒想到現在卻出現180度大轉變。博通認為，台積電產能滿載已成為產業瓶頸，且除了核心晶片之外，緊繃狀況已經擴散至雷射元件與PCB印刷電路板等領域，使交貨周期大幅延後。

綜合外媒報導，博通實體層產品部門產品行銷總監Natarajan Ramachandra表示，儘管台積電預計在2027年持續擴充產能，但2026年已陷入供應瓶頸，甚至可能導致供應鏈受阻。

目前雷射元件出現明顯的供應限制，就連PCB印刷電路板也陷入意外的瓶頸，Natarajan Ramachandran坦言，目前大陸與台灣的相關廠商皆出現產能限制，使得產品周期被迫拉長。博通並未公開確切的廠商名稱。

由於市況極度不穩定，產業界面臨結構性改變，博通透露，許多客戶傾向與供應商達成長達3至4年的長期協議，進一步確保產能承諾。目前三星也有類似做法，正與主要客戶簽訂3至5年的長約，避免需求波動與供應穩定。

台積電為全球先進製程晶片的領導者，囊括超過9成的產能。若是全球晶片市場，台積電也握有7成市佔率，目前除了在台灣擴廠，歐洲、美國及日本皆有產能開出。

台積電也在一月法說會上表示，由於人工智慧基礎設施建設的蓬勃發展，佔了大部分先進晶片生產線，產能確實相當吃緊。董事長魏哲家強調，海外擴廠是雙軌進行，同時也擔憂台灣的電力供應，電力足夠才能持續擴充產能。

news.Title: 《台股盤後》收跌110點，日K連四黑
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-24 16:18:32 新聞中心 發佈

美股四大指數周一全面反彈，台股今(24)日由台積電領軍開高、早盤一度強漲逾600點，不過因伊朗否認川普對戰事說法，加上美股電子盤走跌與原油期貨漲升逾3%、日經225指數漲勢亦不繼，輔以威剛、富喬、台光電、鴻勁、智邦等前波AI指標股弱勢，且鴻海也仍未能收復200元關卡、台積電尾盤亦遭大單摜壓至平盤，導致加權指數終場收在32612.24點，下跌110.26點或0.34%，日K連四黑、成交量6643.52億元。

川普稱與德黑蘭政府展開「能產出實質結果的談話」，並表示已下令要美軍暫緩攻擊伊朗發電廠與能源基礎設施，消息傳來激勵美國四大指數全面反彈。亞洲股市方面，因美軍暫緩攻擊伊朗發電廠與能源基礎設施、美股昨日上漲，激勵日經225指數今日反彈，終場上漲1.43%；韓國KOSPI指數收盤上漲2.74%。

台股今日電子族群漲跌互見，台積電早盤最高上漲40元，不過尾盤遭賣壓拉回至平盤1810元，聯發科、鴻海都收黑；同時先前強勢的記憶體、PCB等族群表現仍疲軟，欣興、金像電、華邦電都有超過7%的跌幅。不過中小型與題材股則仍有資金進駐，如為升、宏齊、希華、連宇均收漲停。

以19大類股來看，貿易百貨與觀光餐旅因基本面或觀光需求回溫題材受到買盤青睞，分別上漲1.63%、1.13%，表現最亮眼；造紙類股則受成本調整與訂單回溫訊號支撐，同樣表現亮眼、漲幅1.11%。跌勢部分則以塑膠股跌1.1%，表現為最差。

三大法人動向方面，今日三大法人總計賣超200.76億元；其中，外資及陸資(不含外資自營商)賣超121.86億元，投信買超25.94億元，自營商賣超104.84億元。

news.Title: 《日股》科技股領軍 日經震盪收漲1.4%
news.Content:
【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】24日，日經指數開高震盪一度大漲超過2%，在上漲動能似乎熄火下，漲幅慢慢縮小，最後日經收在52,252.28點，漲736.79點或1.43%。同時，東證收3,559.67點，也漲73.23點或2.1%。

分析師表示，應該只有少部份認為，川普非常渴望想跟伊朗達成協議。因為，伊朗政府高層否認，跟美國之間，有任何直接或間接的會談，這只是川普的一種緩兵之計，所以當股價上漲後，很多人就選擇先獲利了結。

東京海上控股漲停鎖死並飆升17%，原因是股神巴菲特的波克夏控股公司宣布，將斥資約18億美元收購這家日本保險公司大概2.49%的股份，作為一項新的策略合作計畫。

科技類股上漲居多，半導體的東京威力科創大漲2.2%，迪思科小漲0.14%。同時，人工智慧AI概念股的軟銀漲0.57%，藤倉大漲5.2%，古河電氣工業漲1.5%，住友電氣工業也大漲超過3%。

食品價格平穩加上經濟成長趨緩，日本二月整體CPI(消費者物價指數)已連續第四個月都在下降。不過，經濟學者認為，目前的中東危機尚未解除，原油價格大漲下，日本的的生活成本未來還是可能會增加。

根據24日公布的數據，日本民間企業三月成長放慢腳步，無論是製造業還是服務業，都失去成長的動力，另外，中東衝突造成的生產成本增加，也讓這些日企的經營備感壓力。

news.Title: 京站搶台積電商機高雄打造商場　9強競逐北車拚奪標
news.Content:
（中央社記者曾智怡台北2026年3月24日電）台鐵台北車站商場共9家業者投標，京站總經理王大政今天表示，此次強敵環伺，若得標，預計增加服務與互動設施，並導入AI。此外，瞄準台積電高雄廠商機，京站將前進高雄打造2層樓商場，預計4年後完工亮相。

京站時尚廣場今年女王節活動4月2日開跑，京站總經理王大政今天表示，中東戰火導致油價、機票大漲，相信很多人會因此留在台灣，因此他看好今年女王節、暑假檔期表現，其中，女王節業績目標成長3%。至於今年改裝重點在餐飲，金色三麥、Mo-Mo-Paradise預計上半年完成改裝。

展店方面，京站母公司日勝生集團日前宣布，獲選為「高雄捷運R17世運站周邊A5街廓」都市更新事業公開評選案最優申請人。此為配合台積電高雄廠設廠，預計投資新台幣88.5億元，其中地上18層企業安家住宅，出租給台積電及相關供應商員工居住使用。

王大政表示，該處位在台積電高雄廠外圍，經京站評估，此處擁有台積電與其供應鏈、捷運利多，有一定發展性，因此預計打造面積約800坪的2層樓百貨商場，將京站經營觸角延伸至南台灣重要交通樞紐，預計4年後完工。

至於桃園店進度，他指出，因工法調整、土方問題，約會延誤一年半時間，最快2028年底開幕。

此外，台鐵台北車站商場近期啟動新一輪招標，微風已宣示持續經營決心，據了解，此次共9家業者參與投標。王大政表示，台鐵北車商場營運坪數約3300坪，台鐵最在意的是旅客能滿意動線、商品，京站若得標，希望增加服務與互動設施，並導入AI。

據透露，9家投標業者包含微風、新東陽、潤泰、統一、JR東日本、昇恆昌、京站、誠品、新光三越。王大政說，這次強敵環伺，例如JR東日本包括鐵道、飯店都有實績，微風也表態勢在必得，畢竟與台鐵合作多年，了解其需求，他們確實擁有最有利條件。

news.Content:
（下午4時51分更新外資買賣超個股）

台股今（24）日開高走低，加權指數終場收跌110.26點至32,612.24點，3萬3千點整數關卡得而復失。三大法人合計賣超200.77億元，其中外資續砍121.87億元，賣超力積電（6770）逾8萬張居冠，反手買超南亞科（2408）、旺宏（2337）合計逾2.3萬張。

外資今日賣超前三大：力積電、群創、友達

力積電（6770）今日遭外資大舉賣超80,805張，成為今日最大提款對象；其次為群創（3481）賣超42,488張；第三名則是友達（2409）賣超25,183張，面板雙虎同步遭調節。

其餘賣超標的包括元大台灣50（0050）賣超21,894張、元大滬深300正2（00637L）賣超17,416張；記憶體族群中的華邦電（2344）亦遭砍14,642張。

外資今日買超前三大：南亞科、大聯大、新興

買超方面，南亞科（2408）獲買超13,066張居冠；其次為電子通路指標股大聯大（3702），獲12,925張買盤進駐，第三名為散裝航運大廠新興（2605）買超11,815張。

其餘外資買超個股，塑化族群南亞（1303）買超10,307張、記憶體股旺宏（2337）買超10,213張；金融股也成為資金避風港，包括第一金（2892）買超9,539張、兆豐金（2886）買超9,395張、元大金（2885）買超9,312張皆名列前十。此外，航空股長榮航（2618）買超9,498張與顯示器代工大廠瑞軒（2489）買超9,105張，亦見外資布局。

外資續砍121億元！3月來賣超台股8166億元

根據證交所公布籌碼動向，三大法人今日合計賣超200.77億元，其中外資賣超力道較昨日收斂許多，但仍賣超達121.87億元，累計4個交易日賣超達2273.68億元，自3月以來大砍8166.11億元。

投信續站在買方，買超25.95億元，呈連3買；自營商賣超104.85億元，其中自行買賣部分賣超16.99億元，避險方面賣超87.86億元。

回顧昨（23）日三大法人連袂大砍台股1044.99億元，其中外資賣超839.23億元，創下史上第11大賣超金額紀錄；自營商單日亦賣超311.12億元寫史上第6大。

外資、自營商賣超幅度收斂是正向訊號？股市名師這樣看

針對外資與自營商賣超幅度今日同步收斂，是否可視為正向訊號？蔡明翰認為，這並不代表行情已經全面轉多。若今晚中東局勢再起變化，或美股再度拉回，外資賣超規模明天仍可能再度擴大，因此單看一天的收斂現象，無法當作明確轉強依據。

從目前情勢來看，較正面的地方在於市場最強烈的恐慌高峰大致已過，意味未來再度出現連續重挫的機率相對不高，但同樣地，短線要快速大幅回升的機率也有限，整體仍是大區間震盪整理格局。在這樣的盤勢中，投資人若過度追價或頻繁短打，反而容易犯錯。

因此，蔡明翰建議，現階段操作重點仍是保持冷靜與紀律，不必因看到外資在反彈時持續賣超就過度恐慌，也不要因短線反彈就急著大幅加碼。他強調，投資人應維持既有操作策略，以觀望為主，若後續指數再出現較大幅度拉回，特別是靠近季線時，再尋找相對安全的布局點會比較合適。整體來看，目前盤勢仍處於多空未明的大幅震盪期，控制持股比重、審慎因應，仍是較穩健的做法。

馮萱榕／財經特派記者，主跑財政部、電腦通路、觀光餐飲等

news.Title: 《日股》日經彈736點；東京海上、板硝子飆漲停
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-24 14:35:16 蔡承啟 發佈

因美軍暫緩攻擊伊朗發電廠與能源基礎設施、美股昨日(23日)上漲，激勵日經225指數24日反彈，終場漲1.43%(漲736.79點)，收52,252.28點，3個交易日來首度走揚。

東證股價指數(TOPIX)終場大漲2.10%(漲73.23點)，收3,559.67點，3個交易日來首度走揚。

東證Prime今日上漲家數1,507家、下跌家數59家、維持平盤家數15家。東證Prime今日成交額為6兆7,567億日圓。

就類股表現來看，全部33類股中僅其他製品類股下跌、其餘32類股盡數上漲，其中以保險業類股漲幅最大，其次依序為非鐵金屬、石油/煤製品和玻璃土石製品。

根據MoneyDJ XQ全球贏家系統的報價顯示，截至日本股市24日收盤(台北時間14點30分)為止，日圓相對於美元匯率貶值0.15%至158.67日圓兌1美元；日圓相對於美元匯率23日升值0.53%、3個交易日來第2度走升。

日本玻璃製造商日本板硝子(Nippon Sheet Glass)狂飆19.75%(漲80日圓)亮燈漲停。板硝子24日宣布，在投資基金與銀行團的資金支持下，計畫於2026年度下半年(2026年10月-2027年3月)股票私有化(非公開化)、下市。

日本保險業者東京海上HD狂飆17.07%(漲1,000日圓)亮燈漲停。 波克夏海瑟威持續投資日本企業，將入股東京海上、取得約2.5%股權。

豐田汽車漲0.62%。 因應需求，豐田汽車將對美國2座工廠投資10億美元，增產電動車(EV)、休旅車(SUV)。

機器人廠Fanuc大漲2.44%。 搶攻「實體AI」需求，Fanuc將在美國興建新廠、擴增機器人產能。

(圖片來源：Shutterstock)

搶"實體AI"需求 Fanuc在美蓋新廠、增產機器人

news.Title: 《韓股》油價下跌提振 KOSPI指數上漲2.74%
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-24 14:33:50 黃文章 發佈

韓國股市週二上漲，中東局勢有望趨緩以及油價下跌提振市場情緒。韓國綜合股價指數（KOSPI）3月24日收盤上漲2.74%或148.17點，收5,553.92點。

韓國央行週二公布的數據顯示，在農業成本上升與全球油價走高的帶動下，南韓2月生產者物價連續第六個月上升。作為未來消費者通膨重要指標的生產者物價指數（PPI），2月較前一個月上升0.6%，達到122.56。與去年同期相比，2月生產者物價上升2.4%，為2024年7月以來的最快增速。

韓國國際貿易協會（KITA）週二表示，儘管中東衝突導致物流成本飆升，但在半導體需求強勁的支撐下，預計南韓今年第二季的出口將有所改善。KITA表示，其今年4月至6月期間的出口企業景氣指數（ESBI）為106.6。指數高於100代表樂觀者多於悲觀者，而低於該基準則表示相反。此次調查針對約2,000家出口企業進行。

KITA表示，由於用於智慧型手機與人工智慧（AI）的晶片需求強勁，預計半導體出口條件將改善。該機構也預期，隨著荷姆茲海峽關閉推高油價，石油相關產品的出口價格可能上升，因此其出口條件亦將改善。但該協會預測，由於關稅負擔以及來自中國製產品的價格競爭，家電產品的出口條件將惡化。

同時，在受訪的出口企業中，超過五分之一將原材料價格與物流費用上升列為下一季面臨的最大挑戰。中東衝突不僅推高能源價格，也影響全球航運路線與運費，使出口成本進一步上升。這種成本壓力可能侵蝕企業利潤，甚至削弱價格競爭力，特別是對中小企業而言影響更為顯著。

從更宏觀的角度來看，南韓出口結構的「兩極化」趨勢日益明顯。一方面，高科技產業如半導體受益於全球數位化與AI發展，呈現強勁成長；另一方面，傳統製造業則面臨成本上升與競爭加劇的雙重壓力。這種結構性分化意味著，未來出口表現將更加依賴少數關鍵產業，整體經濟的抗風險能力可能因此下降。

南韓氣候部週二表示，為因應中東持續緊張局勢下可能出現的石油供應中斷，政府將嚴格執行公部門強制性的五日制車輛輪流使用制度，並同時推行額外的節能措施。自週三起，政府將加強監督公部門對於以車牌號碼為基礎的配額制度之遵守情況。在該制度下，車輛依據車牌號碼最後一碼分為五組，每一組在指定的工作日禁止上路行駛。

該制度早已實施，但過去執行較為寬鬆。電動車與氫能車則不受此類限制。具體而言，該部門將發布公部門執行該制度的詳細指引，並推動對未遵守規定的公共機構施以懲處。該部表示，由於政府上週已將韓國四級國家資源安全危機警報體系中的石油供應中斷警戒提升一級至第二級，因此決定採取此項制度。

國際能源署（IEA）列出一些降低石油消費措施：1.盡可能居家辦公：取代通勤產生的石油使用，特別是適合遠程辦公的工作。2.高速公路限速降低至少10公里/小時：降低車速可減少乘用車、廂型車與卡車的燃料使用。3.鼓勵大眾運輸：從私家車轉向巴士與火車可以迅速減少石油需求。4.在大城市實施不同日期的私家車限行：車牌輪換制度可以減少擁堵與高耗能駕駛。5.增加共乘並採取高效駕駛習慣：提高車輛載客率與節能駕駛可快速降低燃料消耗。

三星電子(Samsung Electronics)(005930.KS)上漲1.83%至189,700韓元。電池大廠LG新能源(LG Energy Solution)(373220.KS)上漲10.25%至392,500韓元。DRAM巨擘SK海力士(SK Hynix)(000660.KS)上漲5.68%至986,000韓元。

（圖片來源：MoneyDJ理財網資料庫）

美擬釋柴油供應，緩解市場缺口/抑制價漲

news.Title: Broadcom flags supply constraints, says TSMC capacity a bottleneck
news.Content:
By Wen-Yee Lee

TAIPEI, March 24 (Reuters) - Chip designer Broadcom says it is seeing supply chain constraints across the technology sector, including capacity limits at its manufacturing ‌partner TSMC, as soaring demand for AI chips strains production.

"We are seeing that ‌TSMC is hitting (production capacity) limits," Natarajan Ramachandran, director of product marketing in Broadcom’s Physical Layer Products division, told reporters ​on Tuesday, adding he would have described TSMC's capacity as "infinite" until a few years ago.

More from Yahoo Scout

How is AI demand affecting TSMC production capacity?

What supply chain constraints is Broadcom experiencing?

What components beyond chips are facing shortages?

How are companies securing long-term supply agreements?

"They will be increasing the capacity to 2027, but that has become a bottleneck, or that has kind of choked the supply chain in 2026," he said.

TSMC did not immediately respond to an emailed ‌request for comment.

The Taiwanese firm, ⁠the world's main producer of advanced AI chips, said in January that capacity was tight, as the boom in AI infrastructure buildout has soaked ⁠up much of its advanced production lines.

The world's largest contract chipmaker, whose major customers also include Nvidia and Apple - also said at the time that it was working hard to narrow the ​gap between ​supply and demand.

SUPPLY STRAINS BEYOND CHIPS

Ramachandran said the ​shortages extend beyond semiconductors, affecting several ‌adjacent supply chains.

"Even though there are multiple suppliers in the industry today ... there is definitely a supply constraint in the laser space,” he said, adding that printed circuit boards had also emerged as an "unexpected" bottleneck.

He cited PCBs used in optical transceivers, saying lead times have stretched from about six weeks to six months.

Both Taiwanese and Chinese PCB suppliers are facing capacity limits, ‌contributing to the delays, Ramachandran said, without naming ​the suppliers.

He said that he is not overly concerned ​about the industry as new entrants and ​capacity expansion are expected to ease supply constraints over time.

Many customers ‌are now entering long-term agreements with suppliers ​to secure capacity commitments ​for as long as three to four years, he added.

The trend was underscored by memory chipmaker Samsung Electronics, which said last week that it is working with major ​customers to shift to longer ‌contracts of three to five years.

The move reflects customers’ desire for longer-term supply ​security and suppliers’ efforts to guard against demand swings.

(Reporting by Wen-Yee Lee; Editing ​by Miyoung Kim, Kevin Buckland and Louise Heavens)

news.Title: 《韓股》美伊和談有眉目？韓股又飆起
news.Content:
【時報編譯柯婉琇綜合外電報導】周二南韓股市早盤陷入震盪，KOSPI指數盤初大漲4.39%，之後漲幅一度收斂至不到1%，目前又大漲2.16%，報5522.48點。

美國總統川普聲稱已經與伊朗進行「良好且富有成效」的對話，因此他決定延後對伊朗電力與能源設施發動攻擊的行動，但投資人對美伊衝突的不確定性仍感不安。

Mirae Asset Securities分析師表示，由於地緣政治的不確定性，市場波動加劇的情況將持續一段時間。

周一美國總統川普在Truth Social上發文稱過去兩天，美國和伊朗就全面解決雙方在中東的敵對行動，進行了非常良好且富成效的對話，因此他已指示戰爭部將襲擊伊朗發電廠和能源基礎設施的軍事行動推遲五天。

伊朗否認已與美國進行協商，並稱川普是忌憚於伊朗強硬的報復警告才「臨陣退縮」。

路透社則報導，一位歐洲官員透露，雖然美伊兩國尚未「直接」進行談判，但埃及、巴基斯坦和波斯灣國家正在居中傳達訊息。一名巴基斯坦官員和另一名消息人士表示，美國和伊朗最快可能本周就結束這場戰爭，在伊斯蘭堡進行直接會談。

川普上周六警告，如果伊朗在48小時內不全面恢復荷姆茲海峽航行，他將「摧毀」（obliterate）伊朗的電力設施。伊朗則放話若美國這麼做，那麼伊朗將無差別攻擊在波斯灣地區的能源基礎設施與海水淡化設備。

南韓財政部長具潤哲（Koo Yun-cheol）表示，韓國政府將在本月底前備妥一項追加預算案，以支持在這場中東戰爭受到高油價衝擊的領域。

指標股方面，三星電子漲2%，SK海力士漲5.25%，電池大廠LG Energy Solution大漲9.55%，現代汽車漲1.75%，起亞跌近3%，浦項鋼鐵（Posco）漲3.7%，三星生物製藥（Samsung Biologics）漲1.12%。

外資賣超韓股6243億韓元。

在岸韓元急跌0.82%，報1499兌一美元；前收價為1486.7兌一美元。

news.Title: 【Hot台股】華邦電一度挫8%跌破百元大關...網哀「連彈都彈不起來」分析師揭關鍵支撐價位
news.Content:
今日社群台股最Hot議題

「華邦電還躺在地上 怎沒V」、「99華崩」。

「華邦電在聰三小？？？？？？？」、「記憶卡哇真的是不怕死 華邦電都能抄回100」、「華崩也快到區間下緣了」、「邦邦怎麼跌成這樣 快點抄底買個幾張壓壓驚」、「華崩主力可以用心一點嗎」。

針對網友熱議話題...專家這麼說！

HOT台股今日值班專家－瑞源投顧分析師魏明裕

魏明裕表示，今（24）日記憶體族群面臨強大賣壓，許多指標股如旺宏（2337）、宜鼎（5289）、威剛（3260）甚至觸及跌停，華邦電則最低來到97元，一度逼近跌停，午盤則在100元附近震盪，上揚季線跌破。

魏明裕說明，主要受到領先指標DRAM現貨價出現拉回修正，雖合約價（長約）相對穩定，引發市場對於記憶體「超級循環」是否提前見頂擔憂，另外資大摩（Morgan Stanley）調降其目標價，自155元降至140元。

華邦電本波自高點136元拉回約3成，目前位階是否算甜甜價？魏明裕則說，需視投資周期而定。短線因記憶體族群整體信心潰散，買進風險較高，建議先觀察前低支撐95元能否守住，再分批進場。中長期依預估EPS反推，本益比僅約6.4倍，根據記憶體供需缺口收斂至少看明年下半年而言，景氣循環持續向上，目前價位仍屬低估。

Hot台股主視覺--內文banner

王詩婷／財經特派記者，主跑勞動部、半導體、生技等

news.Title: 《日股》川普說變就變 日經漲幅收斂
news.Content:
【時報編譯柯婉琇綜合外電報導】周二日股早盤漲幅縮小，投資人對美國總統川普聲稱將暫緩打擊伊朗能源設施的說法抱持謹慎態度。

日經225指數早盤收漲394.93點或0.77%，為51,910.42點，盤初一度大漲2.3%。東證一部指數晨收漲1.56%，為3,540.79點，盤初一度大漲2.6%。

與2月27日這場戰爭爆發前的收盤相比，日經指數已經下挫近12%。

周一美國總統川普在Truth Social上寫道：我很高興報告在過去兩天，美國和伊朗就全面解決我們在中東的敵對行動，進行了非常良好且富成效的對話。基於這些深入、詳細、具建設性、將持續整個星期的對話要旨和基調，我已指示戰爭部將對伊朗發電廠和能源基礎設施的任何所有軍事打擊推遲五天，但一切仍取決於正在進行的會議和協商是否成功。

受此影響，周一原油價格重挫超過10%，美股四大指數收漲逾1%。伊朗否認與美國進行任何對話，使美股漲幅收斂。

伊朗政府表示，川普是忌憚於伊朗強硬的報復警告，才「臨陣退縮」。

川普上周六警告，如果伊朗在48小時內不全面恢復荷姆茲海峽航行，他將「摧毀」伊朗的電力設施。伊朗則放話若美國這麼做，那麼伊朗將無差別攻擊在波斯灣地區的能源基礎設施與海水淡化設備。

Matsui Securities資深分析師Tomoichiro Kubota表示，相信川普的發言有助於緩解荷姆茲海峽局勢的投資人似乎不多，許多人認為這些話比較像是「緩兵之計」，所以當股市上漲時，很快就湧現獲利了結。

晶片設備大廠愛德萬測試（Advantest）晨收跌1.69%，其同業東京威力科創（Tokyo Electron）漲1.28%，AI新創業大金主軟銀集團跌0.34%，

日本產險龍頭東京海上控股（Tokio Marine）亮燈漲停，波克夏海瑟威擬以2,874億日圓（逾18億美元）買進2.49%股份。

news.Title: 《美股》焦點股：6月蘋果WWDC登場；特斯拉資本支出估大增
news.Content:
【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】焦點股：

●蘋果23日宣布，下一屆全球開發者大會(WWDC)預訂於六月8至12日在線上舉行，並在加州蘋果總部同步舉行現場活動。根據了解，本次大會主要是聚焦在人工智慧上面，同時推出iOS、macOS、tvOS、watchOS多種系統更新，以及發布全新軟體與開發者工具等。依照慣例蘋果通常會在WWDC公布旗下全系列產品的新軟體與新功能。外媒指出，本次蘋果年度盛會直播，將在蘋果開發者App、蘋果官網，以及蘋果開發者YouTube上看到。至於中國地區，將在蘋果開發者嗶哩嗶哩頻道同步播出。

●OpenAI表示，跟微軟緊密合作的關係，可能成為營運上的潛在風險，因為微軟會影響到OpenAI，像是融資等與交易相關的風險以及業務。外媒指出，OpenAI想要爭取額外的100億美元資金挹注，本輪融資應可在3月底前完成。如果微軟修改或終止與OpenAI的合作，或者是無法實現多元化的話，OpenAI的業務、前景、經營以及財務狀況都可能受到不利的影響。不過，OpenAI強調，微軟無論現在與將來都是OpenAI重要的長期合作夥伴。不過，微軟則沒有做出立即性的評論。

●美國投資公司Wedbush指出，美光與整個記憶體產業，或許正進入一個強勁上漲周期，因AI需求已足夠強勁，故有機會推動記憶體價格的漲幅超過100%。Wedbush表示，DRAM價格在2026上半年跟2025年第四季相比，可能大幅成長130%至150%，同時，NAND的漲幅也緊追在後，供應持續吃緊，AI基礎設施的需求仍在加速增長。不過，美光的股價卻大跌4.4%，盤後小幅反彈0.14%。

●英國巴克萊銀行認為，由於特斯拉積極推動Terafab計畫，故可能會進入一個資本支出超乎預期大幅增加的時期。因此，特斯拉之前說得200億美元資本支出指南，現在看來應該過於保守，就算大增至500億美元也不一定夠用。特斯拉日前宣布，結合旗下xAI、SpaceX以及特斯拉的整體資源，要打造一個超大型的晶圓廠生產AI晶片，不過，馬斯克並沒有提供更具體的時間規劃。

news.Title: 《半導體》旺宏領頭一片跌停 記憶體利多出盡？
news.Content:
【時報記者葉時安台北報導】台股相對抗跌，但記憶體仍無力表態，美光上周公布財報後利多出盡，技術面突破前高失敗，逆勢下跌4.38%，為費半表現最差成分股。而台系族群，遭再次處置的旺宏(2337)摜殺跌停，早盤另有1.8萬張排隊欲賣；模組廠廣穎(4973)、晶豪科(3006)、宇瞻(8271)、凌航(3135)、創見(2451)等也全數慘摔跌停；DRAM雙雄華邦電(2344)、南亞科(2408)也雙雙下跌半根跌停。

DRAM方面，雖然今年第二季價格持續在上升期，下半年留意需求動能，且2027年底、2028年初在DDR4產能預估將陸續開出，牽動價格走勢，價格可能見頂，將進入高原期，不過長約可望對價格有所支撐，而同時各廠也將加速DDR5明年的供給，有助量價維持高檔。市場也提出不同看好，DDR4需求不只消費性電子產品，更擴散至多領域，包括交換器、儲存、網通、AI伺服器周邊、工控，DDR4需求結構改變，預估而非行情結束。

進一步就需求面分析，記憶體相關業者表示，AI發展從雲端訓練逐步擴散至終端推論應用，需求成長動能持續擴大，將帶動大量硬體升級需求。全球資料中心為搶占AI市場，持續擴大資本支出，亦同步帶旺記憶體需求。

研調機構Counterpoint Research指出，雖然三星、SK海力士、美光、長鑫及南亞等擴產，預計今年DRAM產量將成長26%、NAND成長24%，但新增產能最快要到2027年下半年才能產生實質緩解作用，記憶體供需何時能恢復正常仍無明確時間表。

至於NAND市況，近期報價波動正出現比DRAM更劇烈的價格漲勢。韓國媒體報導月初指出，三星計畫在今年第二季再次大幅調漲NAND價格，漲幅高達100%，這也是該公司連續第二季提高報價，且下半年報價更加易漲難跌。

低容量MLC NAND供給極度緊縮，受惠於在當前排擠效應下，NAND供不應求態勢帶動價格上漲，其中又以eMMC漲幅逾80%最為強勁，旺宏成為唯一受惠者，故帶動旺宏整體營收、獲利表現。預估旺宏今年首季即可望轉虧為盈，主因報價及產能利用率提升，而2026年積極調配產能予eMMC，eMMC市占大幅提高，預期2H26營運爆發性成長。

華邦電今年第一季受惠於DRAM與Flash報價持續強勁上行與訂單能見度提升，加上產能維持滿載與產品組合優化，DDR4比重提升，帶動出貨位元數與ASP同步成長，其中DRAM價格預估季增約78%，Flash相關產品則季增約25%，因供給收斂，包含中國競爭對手退出NOR Flash市場以及台灣競爭對手部分轉產，與終端需求回溫，包括車用、TWS、AI相關應用，推升獲利顯著成長。

華邦電DRAM與Flash的報價仍持續強勁，訂單能見度也較優於過往，再加上產能持續滿載以及產品組合優化，看好今年獲利將大幅飆升。而展望2027年，華邦將維持高成長，記憶體擺脫過往周期性的標籤。

華邦電今年年資本支出預估達421億元，其中生產設備約占95%，包括台中廠擴充NOR與NAND 產能，由月產能50Kwpm提升至57~58Kwpm，設備預計從5月開始裝機，7月開始投片，年底可以開始有實際產能貢獻，NOR與2D SLC NAND皆可彈性調配，及高雄廠擴充DRAM產能。

華邦電2027年受惠於DRAM與Flash供需缺口仍在高檔、價格維持上行趨勢，以及CUBE產品將開始貢獻營收，搭配高雄廠DRAM產能全面開出，由2026年底的月產能15Kwpm提升至約25Kwpm，帶動出貨位元數持續成長，然DDR4供給缺口仍難以填補，即使新增產能開出仍無法完全滿足市場需求，使價格維持高檔；同時SLC NAND與NOR Flash受供給收斂與應用需求提升，需求主要由AI Server、傳統Server及Edge應用推動，價格持續上行；CUBE架構具備高頻寬與低功耗優勢，頻寬可對標HBM且功耗僅約20%，於Inference與Edge Server市場滲透，有望成為新成長動能。但毛利率留意台中與高雄廠擴產後設備折舊開始認列而造成稀釋。

news.Title: 《熱門族群》旺宏出關延後摜跌停 8檔處置股跳水 DRAM三雄淪多空戰場
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】旺宏(2337)等8檔記憶體股被列處置股，且旺宏出關延後，旺宏盤中股價被摜到跌停，下游模組廠威剛(3260)也重挫觸及跌停，宜鼎(5289)大跌失守千元關卡。

隨著美國美光股價自高峰連四天回檔，美光失守月線，也引發台系記憶體股賣壓。旺宏等8檔處置股大跌，盤中跌幅至少逾4%，未被處置的三檔上游製造廠也走跌，跌幅都逾6%並吸引買盤承接，呈現多空爭戰但短空控盤。

目前力積電(6770)成交量居第二，華邦電(2344)、南亞科(2408)成交量也擠進個股成交第三及第四，華邦電失守百元大關，跌逾7%，南亞科強守200元之上，跌逾6%。力積電跌逾8%，盤中成交量近23萬張居於個股第二。

旺宏等8檔記憶體股被列處置股，旺宏盤中挫至跌停134元，宜鼎(5289)重挫失守千元關卡，廣穎(4973)、宇瞻(8271)也下殺跌停，晶豪科(3006)一度打到跌停價位，另外，威剛、創見(2451)盤中打到跌停價，鈺創(5351)盤中急拉跌約4%相對抗跌。

受到美國記憶體大廠美光上周公布亮眼財報後，股價連跌四天，且台系8檔被列處置股，股價承壓，今早台股開高震盪下壓，台系記憶體股全面重挫回檔。

今年記憶體嚴重供不應求，股價再拉一波強勁之漲勢，因短期飆漲，目前八檔被列處置股。包括威剛、旺宏、鈺創、廣穎、宇瞻、宜鼎、晶豪科、創見。

旺宏近5日漲24.79%，再被列處置股，處置期間3月24日~4月8日。（編輯：沈培華）

news.Title: 《美股》分析：股市蓄勢待發 找尋彈升動力
news.Content:
【時報編譯張朝欽綜合外電報導】美股分析：

●美國總統川普稱與伊朗進行富有成效對話，並暫緩軍事攻擊，隨後油價急跌，美股反彈。Ingalls & Snyder的資深投資組合策略師Tim Ghriskey說，你永遠不知道該相信誰，儘管伊朗強烈否認，但川普似乎確實試圖與伊朗某個人展開討論，以解決戰爭。這導致今日股市大漲，儘管伊朗否認後，有所回落，但仍是大幅上漲。

●Crossmark全球投資的投資長Bob Doll說，波動可能會持續下去，而這一切都取決於油價。短期內，其它因素都變得不重要，因此當油價下跌時，股價上漲，反之亦然。今天上漲最多的股票，不意外都是經濟敏感的股票。

●B.Riley財富管理策略師Art Hogan說，市場一直迫切獲得好消息，而此一消息，至少在表面上是我們所能期待的最佳消息。如果我們能看到能源價格面臨下行壓力，那麼市場就如同壓緊的彈簧，一直在尋找一個向上彈升的理由。

Baird的投資策略師Ross Mayfield表示，今日市場的走勢更多反映了市場認為，美政府希望朝著這個(和解)方向走。但考慮到各種複雜因素，我嚴重懷疑本周能夠達成協議讓一切恢復正常。即便我們在本周結束這一切，這些破壞是否會從結構上改變油價？

news.Title: 《美股》消息面：對伊不同調「美緩打、以續轟」；亞洲欲買美油
news.Content:
【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】消息面：

●當地時間23日，跟美國總統川普打電話的以色列總理納坦雅胡表示，以色列還會繼續空襲伊朗和黎巴嫩。納坦雅胡在這場視訊會議中，跟川普分享美國和以色列至今獲得的戰果，以及利用協商來實現戰爭目標，這份協議將會確切地維護美以核心利益。這位以色列總理強調，一定會捍?以色列的根本利益，所以會持續轟炸伊朗和黎巴嫩，摧毀伊朗的導彈以及核計畫，並重創黎巴嫩真主黨。

●另方面，美東23日川普總統在自己的社群平台Truth Social網站上發文，在跟伊朗會談後，已下令美軍暫緩攻擊伊朗發電廠和能源基礎設施五天。川普跟美媒CNBC表示，非常渴望與伊朗達成協議。不過，伊朗政府高層在Telegram上指出，對川普上述言論有異議，因為美國與伊朗之間，並未有任何直接或間接的會談。伊朗表示，川普打這種心理戰既不會讓荷姆茲海峽恢復到戰前狀態，也不會讓能源市場恢復正常。川普指出，如果為期五天的停火談判進行順利，雙方最終可能會解決這個問題，否則美國將繼續瘋狂地轟炸伊朗。

●美國內政部長Doug Burgum於23日接受CNBC採訪時指出，亞洲國家已表達意願，希望能購買更多的美國能源，減少對中東油氣的依賴，從荷姆茲海峽出口的石油對日本、韓國和台灣非常重要，現在因伊朗襲擊波斯灣的商船，使得行經荷姆茲海峽的油輪數量大幅減少。三月初曾訪問日本的Burgum直言，美國的盟友可以從美國這裡購買能源，而不是被迫從那些發動戰爭或資助恐怖主義的國家購買。有高達90%的石油進口依賴荷姆茲海峽的日本表示，已把尋找替代能源的供應國家列為優先事項。

●美國芝加哥聯邦準備銀行行長Austan Goolsbee認為，美國失業率保持相對穩定，通膨是美國經濟成長目前面臨到最大的風險之一，不過伊朗的衝突事件若能快速解決，聯準會在2026下半年時還是有可能會降息。Goolsbee指出，高油價可能影響消費者的預期心理，通貨膨脹在聯準會的優先處理順序上應高於就業市場，通膨原本就已經在相對高點，現在又可能因為油價上漲而走高，這是個嚴峻的時刻，希望不會對經濟造成持久性的衝擊。

●美國總統川普23日表示，針對伊朗進一步軍事打擊已被延後，美國10年期公債殖利率應聲下降超過四個基本點，至4.348%。先前，因交易員擔心聯準會今年不會降息，甚至還可能會升息，這個基準的公債殖利率一度大幅上升至去年七月以來最高。同時，對政策較為敏感的兩年期公債殖利率也降至3.848%，30年期下跌四個基本點，降到4.92%。摩根大通認為，除了油價的因素外，公債殖利率也需要平穩，股市才能穩定發展。

●紐約期油一度重挫14.11%，每桶跌至84.37美元，收盤時還是大跌10.28%，為88.13美元。同時，倫敦布蘭特期油也一度急挫14.43%，每桶跌至96美元，收盤在99.94美元，還是大跌10.92%。不過在亞洲交易時，美國WTI大漲4%，每桶漲至91.7美元，倫敦布蘭特也大漲近4%，每桶漲至103.7美元，重回百元大關以上。

●黃金價格延續近期的頹勢，23日早盤時，現貨金價一度大跌超過5%，每盎司來到4,262.5美元，之後小幅反彈至4321.81美元，但還是大跌3.78%。至於黃金期貨結算價跌0.7%，每盎司為4,574美元，之前一度大跌近10%。交易員指出，上周金價大跌近10%，跌幅曾創自2011年9月以來最大。從一月底衝高至每盎司5,594.92美元的歷史天價後，現貨金價迄今累計重挫約25%。

●美匯指數一度下跌0.77%，但目前在99.38點，上漲0.44%。美元兌歐元走貶，來到0.86左右。另外，美元兌日圓也出現貶值，日圓升至158.62附近。至於比特幣一度大漲6.5%，目前在70,513.53美元，還是大漲3.22%。

news.Title: 關禁閉首日打跌停！旺宏帶頭6檔記憶體全倒 另有1.6萬張排隊等賣
news.Content:
台股今（24）日回神，早盤一度大漲逾600點，順利站上3萬3關卡，但記憶體族群仍呈現逆風，開盤後不到10分鐘，紛紛翻黑跳水，包括剛被關禁閉的旺宏（2337）遭打入跌停，截至上午9時30分，成交量8千餘張，另有1.6萬張排隊等賣；力積電（6770）、華邦電（2344）和南亞科（2408）則重挫逾6%。

台股23日狂瀉逾千點，創史上第八大跌點紀錄，但在川普釋出中東衝突可能緩和訊號，晚間美股4大指數收漲逾，帶動台股今日早盤拉升至3萬3361.46點，勁揚638點，電子、半導體與櫃買指數皆走升約2%，而先前強勢股記憶體族群股價仍疲軟。

證交所23日指出，旺宏最近6個營業日累積收盤價漲幅達33.42%，遭列為注意股，且為近30個交易日內第2次處置，今日起分盤交易撮合時間由5分鐘一次改採20分鐘一次。

截至上午9時30分，威剛（3260）、宇瞻（8271）、宜鼎（5289、創見（2451）、廣穎（4973）和旺宏（2337）亮燈跌停鎖死。南亞科、華邦電、力積電、群聯（8299）重挫逾6%；華東（8110）、鈺創（5351）也有5%以上跌幅。

摩根士丹利（大摩）最新報告示警，雖然DDR4近期價格飆漲，但下半年的強勢定價能力恐因終端需求轉弱而面臨嚴峻挑戰，降評指標股南亞科與華邦電至中立，並把投資首選替換為旺宏。

王詩婷／財經特派記者，主跑勞動部、半導體、生技等

news.Title: Bank of America revamps Apple price target
news.Content:
Apple ( AAPL ) stock has lost about 7% year to date, at the time of writing, Monday afternoon, March 23, according to Yahoo Finance . Meanwhile, the SPDR S&P 500 index ( SPY ) is down about 3.82% in the same period.

The stock is lagging behind the S&P 500, which isn’t doing great either, and is not much of a surprise. Investors still expect an AI turnaround from Apple.

Apple's earnings report on January 29 provided a decent boost to the stock. Read the article “ Apple stock price swing with $143B record ” by TheStreet’s Aparajita Chatterjee for a recap of Apple’s Q1 report.

Unfortunately, all the gains from that report have been wiped out. The stock took a significant hit on February 12, dropping 5% due to reports of Siri delays and regulatory scrutiny of the company’s news app, as reported by CNBC .

Bloomberg recently reported that the foldable iPhone will launch this fall.

Bank of America analyst Wamsi Mohan and his team updated their opinion on Apple shares after consulting with their supply chain sources regarding the foldable iPhone launch.

Bank of America lowers Apple price target

Mohan wrote: “Our checks in Asia suggest that Apple will likely introduce its first foldable iPhone in 2026.”

He said that he expects the device to have an inner screen of 7.7" to 7.8" and under 10mm thickness when folded. The team believes that the initial demand for the device will be strong, and the supply chain is gearing up for a range of outcomes from 10 million to 20 million units.

More Tech Stocks:

Morgan Stanley sets jaw-dropping Micron price target after event

Nvidia’s China chip problem isn’t what most investors think

Quantum Computing makes $110 million move nobody saw coming

Analysts noted that this is a much higher number of units than competing products. They said their supply chain sources indicate that Apple will launch the foldable and the Pro models (Pro/Pro Max) in the usual September time frame.

Related: Who owns Apple? Institutional holdings & executives’ shares

Mohan said that the base model, Air, and the “e” models are expected to launch in the first half of 2027.He noted that this delayed launch creates a shift in units from the September and December to the March quarter and changes the seasonality, which is not yet reflected in the consensus. This will create a push out for fiscal year 2026, which will cause lower units and revenues.

Analysts changed their EPS estimates for 2026, 2027, and 2028 to $8.36, $9.53, and $10.64 from $8.51, $9.77, and $10.86, respectively.

In a research note shared with me, Mohan reiterated a buy rating for Apple stock and lowered the price target to $320 from $325, due to the “staggered” launch of base, Air, and “e” models and associated gross margin changes, based on a 32 multiple of his estimates for EPS in 2027 of $9.94.

Analysts noted downside risks for Apple:

Weaker iPhone cycle on consumer spending risk,

Weaker near-term services trajectory,

Gross profit dollars declining YoY next few quarters,

iPads/Macs reverting to pre-COVID levels,

Stronger dollar,

Antitrust lawsuit,

Potential trade conflicts, tariffs.

Apple's upside is:

Stronger sales of Pro iPhone models,

Potential new products (AR/VR) and services,

Stronger than expected iPhone cycle,

Tailwinds from lower memory costs,

Faster than expected recovery in emerging markets.

MacBook Neo has already sold out

Apple launched MacBook Neo, a cheap laptop with a price tag of just $599, on March 4. It didn't take long for the laptop to become a hit.

Apple CEO Tim Cook posted on X on March 20:

“Mac just had its best launch week ever for first-time Mac customers. We love seeing the enthusiasm!”

It was certainly a great launch. 9to5Mac reported that as of March 20, all eight MacBook Neo models are sold out online until next month.

When MacBook Neo was announced, I wrote an in-depth analysis of the importance of that launch in my article “ Apple’s latest product is a game-changer ,” and I am not surprised that it is sold out.

What surprised me was that there were almost no reactions from the analysts. The exception is Rosenblatt, which reiterated a Neutral rating for Apple stock and raised its price target to $268 from $267, as reported by Investing .

I believe the weak analyst reaction is due to the expectation that gross margin on the MacBook Neo will be very low. But the purpose of that laptop is to get millions of new customers into the Apple ecosystem, and it will be successful in doing that. This means more revenue from various Apple services.

Related: History of Apple: Company timeline and facts

This story was originally published by TheStreet on Mar 23, 2026, where it first appeared in the Investing section. Add TheStreet as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

news.Title: 《美股》收盤速報：TACO再現 四大指數齊揚 油價崩跌11%
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】由輝達、蘋果、亞馬遜等「科技七雄」領軍，周一(3月23日)四大指數均漲逾1%，台積電ADR漲2.8%。布蘭特期油重挫近11%，跌破被視為經濟榮枯轉折點的每桶100美元大關。川普聲稱美國與伊朗已進行富有成效的對話，因此他將暫緩執行48小時內荷姆茲海峽未全面恢復通行就襲擊伊朗電廠與能源設施的計畫，投資人看好這場大幅推升油價並引發全球衰退疑慮的中東衝突可能接近尾聲。惟伊朗政府透過官媒否認與美國對話，並稱川普是因伊朗強硬的報復警告而「臨陣退縮」。

news.Title: 《台北股市》投信23日賣超股：南亞、台玻、華邦電
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股23日加權指數收32722.50點，跌821.38點或2.45%，總成交值7125.78億元。三大法人賣超1044.99億元，其中外資賣超839.23億元，投信、自營商分別買超105.35億元及賣超311.12億元。

投信賣超前十大個股，依類別分，5檔電子股、5檔傳產股；依股價分，6檔百元股、3檔中價位股及1檔銅板股；賣超張數方面，榜首為南亞(1303)，賣超9590張，台玻(1802)、華邦電(2344)名列二、三名，分別賣超6117張及賣超5945張。第四及第五名為南亞科(2408)、華新(1605)，其他依序為裕融(9941)、國巨*(2327)、旺宏(2337)、臻鼎-KY(4958)及中砂(1560)。

至於前十大個股中賣超金額前三大，則為南亞科(2408)11.64億元、南亞(1303)7.08億元、華邦電(2344)6.39億元。

●投信23日賣超前十名：

1.南亞(1303)收73.80元，跌4.60元或5.87%，成交量7.06萬張，投信賣超9590張、估7.08億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超1萬255張、估7.6億元。

news.Title: 《台北股市》注意股：24日48檔 群聯、旺宏、上詮、波若威、燿華等
《美股》川普稱與伊朗良好對談+暫緩攻擊 費半嗨
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-24 07:10:01 郭妍希 發佈

美國總統川普(Donald Trump)稱與德黑蘭政府展開「能產出實質結果的談話」(productive conversations)後、已下令要美軍暫緩攻擊伊朗發電廠與能源基礎設施，消息傳來激勵美國四大指數全面反彈。

道瓊工業平均指數3月23日終場上漲1.38%(631點)、收46,208.47點。那斯達克指數上漲1.38%(299.15點)、收21,946.76點。標準普爾500指數上漲1.15%(74.52點)、收6,581.00點。費城半導體指數上漲1.34%(102.52點)、收7,773.13點。

川普23日在Truth Social的貼文中 寫道 ，「美國與伊朗在過去兩天內，為徹底解決兩國在中東的敵對行動，進行了非常良好且極具成效的對話。」

「這些對話深入、詳盡且建設性，預計本週仍將持續進行，基於雙方談話時的整體氛圍與核心意旨，我已指示戰爭部(Department of War)在未來五天內，暫緩所有針對伊朗發電廠與能源基礎設施的軍事行動。不過，一切仍須視後續會議與討論的進展而定。」

週一(23日)稍晚，川普進一步表示，伊朗與美國雙方都有意「達成協議」(make a deal)，且兩國「今天大概會透過電話展開會談」。

然而，伊朗官媒引述一未具名的資深安全官員在Telegram的發文，反駁川普對雙方對話的描述，聲稱華盛頓與德黑蘭之間並未進行任何直接或間接談判。

官媒引述該官員說法表示，「過去沒有、現在也沒有任何協商。在這種心理戰之下，荷姆茲海峽既不會恢復到戰前狀態，能源市場也不會有和平的一天。」

川普於週一稍晚反擊，堅稱美伊雙方「已進行強而有力的對話」，並達成「重大共識」，其中包括德黑蘭「絕不會擁有核武」。

油價應聲跳水。西德州中級(WTI)原油期貨23日終場重挫10.28%、收在每桶88.13美元。布蘭特原油期貨也大跌10.92%、收每桶99.94美元。

美國總統川普(Donald Trump)甫於上週六(21日)發出最後通牒，要求伊朗在48小時內重新開放海峽，否則美軍將摧毀伊朗境內的發電廠。然而，伊朗週日強硬回擊，稱若電力設施遭到攻擊，將「徹底關閉」海峽。

Baird投資策略師Ross Mayfield接受CNBC專訪時表示，市場今日的走勢，更多是反映投資人察覺到政府想走的方向。但考慮到種種複雜因素，對於本週就能搞定一切並恢復常態的說法，他持高度懷疑態度。

B. Riley Wealth Management市場策略長Art Hogan表示，市場對於任何利多消息都非常渴望，從表面看來，這至少是大家能期待的最好消息。只要能源價格有下滑跡象，金融市場就會像是一個被壓扁的彈簧，隨時都在等待時機往上噴發。

芝加哥選擇權交易所(CBOE)用來衡量市場恐慌氣氛的波動率指數(VIX) 23日稍早一度飆漲16%至31.04點、創3月9日以來盤中新高，終場翻黑下跌2.35%、收26.15點。

標普500十一大類股全面走揚，其中非必需消費品類股跳漲2.46%成為領漲族群，防衛型類股諸如健康照護、民生必需品類股漲勢則較緩。

小型股指標羅素兩千指數(Russell 2000)勁揚2.3%；上週五收盤已較1月22日的歷史收盤高拉回超過10%、確認進入修正領域。

半導體製造不可或缺的關鍵化學原料——氦氣(Helium)，因卡達產線遭襲而面臨供應中斷，伊朗戰事有緩解希望，帶動AI晶片類股回神。輝達(Nvidia Corp.)、台積電(2330) ADR、荷蘭半導體設備業龍頭艾司摩爾(ASML Holding N.V.) ADR分別上漲1.57%、2.8%、3.98%。

對油價高度敏感的航空類股走強。阿拉斯加航空(Alaska Air)、聯合航空(United Airlines)分別勁揚4.66%、4.46%。挪威遊輪(Norwegian Cruise Line)、嘉年華遊輪集團(Carnival Corp.)也分別跳漲6.17%、5.51%。

個股變化方面， 矽谷大數據分析商Palantir Technologies Inc.勁揚6.74%、收160.84美元。據外媒 報導 ，美國戰爭部決定將Palantir開發的AI系統「Maven Smart System」正式升格為「制式裝備」(Programs of Record/POR)。此舉象徵Palantir AI技術全面納入美軍核心作戰體系，並為Palantir帶來穩定長期的國防預算挹注。

電子設計自動化(EDA)軟體商新思科技(Synopsys)上漲2.89%、收432.48美元。市場 謠傳 ，億萬富翁Paul Singer創立的知名維權避險基金Elliott Investment Management，已建立價值數十億美元的新思部位，想要推動這家EDA軟體巨頭改善營運並提高變現能力。Elliott 強調，隨著AI加深晶片複雜度，新思處於絕佳戰略位置。

news.Title: ON Semiconductor Corp. (ON) Rises Yet Lags Behind Market: Some Facts Worth Knowing
news.Content:
ON Semiconductor Corp. (ON) ended the recent trading session at $59.89, demonstrating a +1.06% change from the preceding day's closing price. The stock lagged the S&P 500's daily gain of 1.15%. Elsewhere, the Dow saw an upswing of 1.38%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq appreciated by 1.38%.

Prior to today's trading, shares of the semiconductor components maker had lost 14.25% lagged the Computer and Technology sector's loss of 5.27% and the S&P 500's loss of 5.69%.

The investment community will be paying close attention to the earnings performance of ON Semiconductor Corp. in its upcoming release. The company is expected to report EPS of $0.62, up 12.73% from the prior-year quarter. Meanwhile, the latest consensus estimate predicts the revenue to be $1.49 billion, indicating a 2.9% increase compared to the same quarter of the previous year.

For the entire fiscal year, the Zacks Consensus Estimates are projecting earnings of $2.92 per share and a revenue of $6.28 billion, representing changes of +24.26% and +4.81%, respectively, from the prior year.

It's also important for investors to be aware of any recent modifications to analyst estimates for ON Semiconductor Corp. These revisions help to show the ever-changing nature of near-term business trends. As such, positive estimate revisions reflect analyst optimism about the business and profitability.

Our research demonstrates that these adjustments in estimates directly associate with imminent stock price performance. To capitalize on this, we've crafted the Zacks Rank, a unique model that incorporates these estimate changes and offers a practical rating system.

The Zacks Rank system, running from #1 (Strong Buy) to #5 (Strong Sell), holds an admirable track record of superior performance, independently audited, with #1 stocks contributing an average annual return of +25% since 1988. Over the last 30 days, the Zacks Consensus EPS estimate has witnessed a 0.32% increase. Right now, ON Semiconductor Corp. possesses a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).

In terms of valuation, ON Semiconductor Corp. is presently being traded at a Forward P/E ratio of 20.31. This represents a discount compared to its industry average Forward P/E of 34.55.

Investors should also note that ON has a PEG ratio of 0.63 right now. The PEG ratio is akin to the commonly utilized P/E ratio, but this measure also incorporates the company's anticipated earnings growth rate. Semiconductor - Analog and Mixed stocks are, on average, holding a PEG ratio of 1.17 based on yesterday's closing prices.

Story Continues

The Semiconductor - Analog and Mixed industry is part of the Computer and Technology sector. This group has a Zacks Industry Rank of 50, putting it in the top 21% of all 250+ industries.

The Zacks Industry Rank gauges the strength of our individual industry groups by measuring the average Zacks Rank of the individual stocks within the groups. Our research shows that the top 50% rated industries outperform the bottom half by a factor of 2 to 1.

Make sure to utilize Zacks.com to follow all of these stock-moving metrics, and more, in the coming trading sessions.

news.Title: Qualcomm Stock Is Down 28%. Is This a Once-in-a-Lifetime Buying Opportunity?
news.Content:
It's been tough to be a Qualcomm (NASDAQ: QCOM) shareholder lately. The stock price is down 28% just since its early January peak and is still knocking on the door of a new 52-week low.

Blame the global shortage of memory chips, mostly, which undermined its guidance for the fiscal second quarter now underway. The company's looking for sales of somewhere between $10.2 billion and $11 billion, versus consensus estimates of $11.1 billion. Growing competition and the stock's frothy valuation were also a concern. The market's response to all of it makes enough superficial sense.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Now take a step back and look at the bigger picture. The pullback hurt, to be sure, but it only considers what's happening right now. Smart investors are looking a year, five years, and even 10 years into the future. And in those time frames, Qualcomm is an incredibly promising prospect. Looking back, this dip is apt to be an incredible buying opportunity.

Versatile, high-performance computing tech

You may know Qualcomm best as a smartphone name. Although it no longer makes its own branded handhelds, its tech -- like the powerful Snapdragon processor -- is still found in plenty of mobile devices.

That's not all this company is anymore, though. While mobile is still its big profit center, its future is far more diversified. The same power-efficient, artificial intelligence (AI)-capable know-how that's packed into the Snapdragon processor found in several smartphones, as it turns out, is just as capable of handling applications ranging from "smart" automobiles to AI-capable laptops to advanced autonomous robotics to virtual reality goggles, and more.

On the surface, all of it just seems like the inevitable evolution of a respectable computer processing platform. And in some regards, that's exactly what it is.

The need for such a processor is finally materializing ... enormously

Qualcomm's Snapdragon is so much more than such an assessment suggests, however. It's very much in the right place at the right time, as one of the few -- maybe even the only -- chips ready to fill a relatively new and unexpected role within the computing arena.

It's called "edge" computing. In the simplest terms, this is just the processing of digital information somewhere between a remote data center and the person utilizing or benefiting from this work. Autonomous or connected vehicles, automated factories, traffic management platforms, cashier-less retailing, and smart utility meters are all examples of solutions that need computerized control, but don't need to punt this work to a data center located somewhere else -- it can be handled more than adequately enough on-premise. The advent of artificial intelligence and the mountain of digital data it requires and creates, of course, has only expanded the need for more localized high-performance computing.

Story Continues

And the opportunity isn't insignificant. An outlook from Precedence Research suggests the worldwide edge AI industry is poised to grow at an average annual pace of 21% through 2034, when it will be worth more than $140 billion a year.

Here's the head-turner for any investors who have been looking for an underappreciated growth opportunity or are already eyeing Qualcomm stock: As CEO Cristiano Amon recently told the Wall Street Journal earlier this year, "The biggest opportunity [of 2026] is the opportunity that exists on the edge."

He may be biased, but he doesn't seem wrong. While most chipmakers have been hyper-focused on high-performance silicon for artificial intelligence data centers or turning smartphones (still consumers' favorite personal tech) into AI-capable stand-alone devices, the semiconductor industry hasn't fully appreciated just how much computing is going to be done within the billions of devices found and functioning somewhere between those two points.

Worth the likely wild initial ride

Although the tailwind is already blowing, this doesn't necessarily mean Qualcomm shares are guaranteed to be at their ultimate bottom just yet. Investors may not be comfortable stepping back in until the broader weakness from most AI stocks has run its full course. And the market may also be holding out until they know for sure the memory shortage is in the rearview mirror. Both work against QCOM stock in the meantime.

From a risk-versus-reward perspective, though, most of any downside has already been wrung out. With shares priced at less than 12 times this year's earnings consensus of $11.17, the bigger risk at this point is arguably missing out on whatever upside awaits whenever it materializes, even if actual growth isn't expected to begin again until 2028. The market's got a knack for predicting and pricing in recoveries well before they happen.

The kicker(s): This stock's sizable sell-off has pumped up its forward-looking dividend yield to 2.7% -- rarely seen income from a technology stock -- offering newcomers some immediate reward for the patience they may need to muster by stepping into a stake in this company today. Moreover, Qualcomm recently announced a $20 billion stock buyback. Its current market cap stands at $140 billion, for perspective, translating into roughly a 15% upside in per-share benefit. That's not a bad way to start out a new long-term trade that's likely to get off to a volatile start.

news.Title: Nvidia, AMD Rally As War Fears Ease
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Nvidia ( NASDAQ:NVDA ) and AMD ( NASDAQ:AMD ) helped lead a strong bounce in chip and AI stocks on Monday , as easing tensions between the U.S. and Iran gave markets a bit of breathing room.

Both stocks were up around 2.8% in late morning trading after President Donald Trump signaled that recent talks with Iran had been productive, along with a temporary pause on planned military strikes. That shift in tone helped calm fears around potential energy disruptions and broader geopolitical fallout, which had been weighing on sentiment.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 5 Warning Signs with AMD.

Is AMD fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The relief rally spread quickly across the semiconductor space. Broadcom, Intel, Texas Instruments, Marvell, Analog Devices and Qualcomm all moved higher, with Texas Instruments also getting a boost from new data center module announcements. On the AI side, Oracle and Microsoft rose more than 1%, while Palantir stood out with a sharper 5% gain.

news.Title: Adeia CEO Highlights Patent Growth, OTT Deals and “Seminal” AMD License at Roth Conference
news.Content:
Key Points

Adeia has expanded its patent portfolio from about 9,500 assets at separation to roughly 13,750 today, positioning the company as an R&D-led patent-licensing business focused on media and semiconductors.

Media still generates over 90% of revenue but the mix is shifting away from pay-TV (projected ~35–40% this year) as Adeia diversifies into OTT (notably Amazon and Disney deals) and e-commerce, with an e-commerce pipeline of over 100 potential customers.

In semiconductors Adeia is pitching hybrid bonding as a platform technology and closed a “seminal” multi-year license with AMD ; semiconductor revenue rose to $26 million last year with a longer-term recurring revenue target of $100 million , and the company is also developing thermal-management tech (“RapidCool”).

Interested in Adeia Inc.? Here are five stocks we like better.

Two Value Tech Stocks Trading Near a Breakout Level

Adeia (NASDAQ:ADEA) CEO Paul Davis outlined the company’s evolution since its separation from Xperi in October 2022, highlighting a strategy centered on research and development, patent licensing, and what he described as an expanding opportunity set across both media and semiconductors. Davis spoke during a fireside chat with Roth analyst Scott Searle at the Roth 30th Annual Conference.

Business model and patent portfolio growth

Davis described Adeia as a technology R&D company that primarily monetizes its inventions through patent licensing. He said the company’s portfolio is anchored in two main areas: media (with legacy roots from TiVo and Rovi) and semiconductors (stemming from technologies including Tessera and Ziptronix).

→ Planet Labs: The Satellite Stock That Keeps Shooting to the Moon

He emphasized that invention is “at our core” and pointed to growth in the company’s patent holdings since the split. Davis said Adeia had about 9,500 patent assets at separation and has grown that figure to approximately 13,750 patent assets.

Media remains the revenue base as mix shifts away from pay TV

While much of the investor focus has shifted to semiconductors, Davis said media still represents “over 90%” of Adeia’s revenue today. Within media, he said the pay-TV portion has been shrinking and is projected to represent about 35%–40% of total revenue this year, down from being the bulk of media revenue at the time of separation.

→ Active ETFs Surge Past Passive, and These Are in the Lead

Davis said this shift was anticipated and that Adeia has been working to diversify media licensing into OTT and adjacent markets. He cited an Amazon OTT deal signed at the end of 2024 as a major “proof point,” adding that the company had smaller OTT licensees earlier, including Starz and DAZN. Davis also discussed a litigation-driven path to licensing in OTT, noting that Adeia filed litigation against Disney roughly a month before the Amazon deal and later reached a licensing agreement with Disney in December, about 13 months after filing.

Story Continues

When asked for examples of media IP, Davis pointed to areas including:

Search and recommendation technologies (including content discovery and personalization)

Content delivery network (CDN) related technology

User interface and navigation for smart TVs and OTT platforms

Imaging-related IP

→ Trash to Treasure: 3 Waste Removal Stocks to Minimize Volatility

On pay TV trends, Davis said a large pay-TV provider agreement that had a more fixed structure expired at the end of last year, which he said had previously moderated the impact of industry declines. Going forward, he said Adeia expects the pay-TV component to more closely “mirror” industry trends with “moderate declines,” and he suggested the broader market could see some stabilization, referencing Charter’s reported video subscriber increase in Q4 for the first time since 2017 and ongoing subscriber growth at YouTube TV.

Expanding pipeline in e-commerce and a broader customer set

Davis said Adeia’s pipeline of licensing opportunities has grown “substantially” as the company has diversified beyond a relatively narrow set of large pay-TV operators and a limited number of major semiconductor targets (particularly in memory). He described e-commerce as a newer avenue that increases the number of potential customers significantly, drawing parallels between recommendation algorithms in entertainment and product discovery in retail.

He said Adeia has completed four e-commerce deals so far, describing them as relatively small in dollar value compared to other agreements, but added that the company’s e-commerce pipeline includes “over 100” potential customers and continues to grow. Davis also mentioned “video try-on” as an emerging area where Adeia’s imaging portfolio may be relevant as e-commerce platforms become more sophisticated.

Semiconductors: hybrid bonding and a “seminal” AMD deal

Davis framed hybrid bonding as a platform technology, saying “all roads lead to hybrid bonding” in semiconductors and describing it as relevant across logic and memory. He said hybrid bonding is already in high-volume manufacturing in NAND and image sensors and credited AMD as an early logic adopter that “pioneered hybrid bonding for chiplet architectures.” He also said Broadcom, Intel, and Marvell have hybrid bonding on product roadmaps and asserted that NVIDIA is likely to adopt it at some point, calling it “a matter of when, not if.”

Davis recounted Adeia’s entry into hybrid bonding through its 2015 investment in Ziptronix, a North Carolina company he said invented hybrid bonding. He described Adeia’s work expanding the technology from wafer-to-wafer bonding into die-to-wafer bonding, which he said is relevant for logic and memory.

On licensing progress, Davis called the AMD agreement “huge” and “a seminal deal” as Adeia’s first major logic license. He said the company’s semiconductor revenue was $26 million last year, up from $18 million the year prior, and reiterated a longer-term target of $100 million in recurring semiconductor revenue, arguing that additional logic licenses could move the company closer to that level.

Regarding deal structure, Davis said customer agreements are confidential but provided general framing: Adeia characterized the AMD agreement as “multi-year” (which he defined as five years or less), while describing the Disney agreement as “long-term” (greater than five years). He also said the AMD deal was reached four months after Adeia filed litigation, which he said was unusually fast.

DRAM and RapidCool: watching HBM adoption and thermal management

In memory, Davis discussed hybrid bonding in the context of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) roadmaps, saying Adeia remains “bullish” on adoption around the HBM4E/HBM5 timeframe. He addressed recent JEDEC-related discussion about relaxing height requirements, arguing that the key value of hybrid bonding is not only pitch or form factor but also performance and thermal benefits. He referenced public commentary from Kioxia and SanDisk on NAND, citing large performance and thermal management gains, and suggested similar benefits could apply to HBM. Davis said he expects key memory renewals around 2028 and indicated 2027–2028 as a window he would expect for HBM-related adoption dynamics.

Davis also highlighted “RapidCool,” an Adeia technology initiative focused on thermal management. He said the concept involves bonding a cold plate directly to a chip using silicon-to-silicon bonding, removing thermal interface material (TIM). Davis said early work showed roughly 70% efficiency improvement “out of the gate” and described a potential advantage in designing cooling channels to target chip hotspots. He said RapidCool is “mid to long term” from a revenue standpoint and described it as in a late-stage development cycle with customer discussions underway, adding that it is designed to be “plug and play” within existing liquid-cooled data centers.

Capital allocation: debt reduction, buybacks, dividend, and acquisitions

On capital structure, Davis said Adeia has a “balanced” approach. He said debt has fallen from $759 million at the time of separation to about $400 million today, reflecting more than $300 million of paydown over three years. He said the company’s target range is roughly $300 million to $400 million, with plans to refinance into a more fixed debt structure. Davis added that Adeia also expects to continue stock buybacks, pay a dividend, reinvest in the business, and consider tuck-in acquisitions.

About Adeia (NASDAQ:ADEA)

Adeia Inc (NASDAQ: ADEA) is a technology licensing company that focuses on acquiring, managing and monetizing intellectual property assets in the electronics and communications sectors. The company’s core business involves the strategic purchase of patent portfolios followed by the negotiation of licensing agreements, collaborative partnerships and, where necessary, enforcement actions to generate revenue from those assets. Adeia’s technology coverage spans semiconductor design, data communications, wireless networking, imaging systems and other advanced electronics applications.

By assembling a diversified collection of high-value patent families, Adeia works closely with original equipment manufacturers, semiconductor suppliers and service providers across North America, Europe and Asia.

The article " Adeia CEO Highlights Patent Growth, OTT Deals and “Seminal” AMD License at Roth Conference " was originally published by MarketBeat.

news.Title: Samsung just found a shocking new way into AMD’s AI push
news.Content:
Samsung’s new HBM4 memory deal with AMD (AMD) is far more than just about components. It's an early sign. A warning shot that the chipmaker is looking to push its way into AMD’s lucrative artificial intelligence supply chain .

Samsung Electronics may have found a back door into one of the hottest businesses in the stock market.

The South Korean tech giant recently inked an amazing agreement. Under the auspices of the deal, Samsung will supply sixth-generation high-bandwidth memory, or HBM4 , for Advanced Micro Devices’ next flagship artificial intelligence accelerator system , Reuters reported.

On its face, that sounds like a straightforward memory win . But it may be bigger than that.

A report from Chosun Biz , cited by SamMobile , says Samsung is trying to turn that HBM4 relationship into something far more valuable: a share of AMD’s advanced chip manufacturing work .

If that goes down, it will mark a line in the sand, as AMD (AMD) has long leaned on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing ( TSM ) for leading-edge logic production.

That's why this story matters to investors.

This is not just a supplier footnote buried in the semiconductor weeds. Instead, it's a possible huge power play in the boom in artificial intelligence hardware, where control over memory , packaging , and manufacturing capacity can determine who gets the next wave of revenue.

Why Samsung investors should care:

AMD is one of the largest AI names on the stock market.

Samsung is looking to transform and become more than just a memory supplier .

Any shift in AMD’s supply chain will lead to major implications for a future revenue , margins and competitive positioning .

All of this will lead to fresh pressure on TSMC’s dominance in advanced chip production.

AMD stock is no stranger to attracting attention. Shares closed at $201.33 on March 20 , giving the company a market value of about $258.8 billion .

That valuation helps explain why any change around AMD’s AI supply chain will get Wall Street 's attention at a moment's notice.

Samsung may be trying to turn an AI choke point into leverage

Samsung’s apparent strategy is simple, and that is what makes it dangerous for rivals.

HBM is becoming one of the most crucial cogs inside modern AI accelerators .

The faster companies such as AMD try to scale their data-center GPU business , the more they become vulnerable to constrained supply of advanced memory.

More Tech Stocks:

Morgan Stanley sets jaw-dropping Micron price target after event

Nvidia’s China chip problem isn’t what most investors think

Quantum Computing makes $110 million move nobody saw coming

Story Continues

That gives memory suppliers unusual leverage.

AMD’s own numbers give away the magnitude of the opportunity. The company said 2025 was a “defining year,” with record revenue and earnings , and said it entered 2026 with a strong wind behind it, thanks to outsized investments in the data-center AI franchise .

That means Samsung is not chasing a side hustle. It may be trying to wedge itself into one of AMD’s highest-stakes growth engines .

What makes this financially important for AMD:

The need for AI GPUs is growing quickly. AI GPU demand is rising at a rapid clip.

HBM supply is necessary to get those products to their destination on time.

A second major manufacturing partnership could make the supply chain more flexible.

Better visibility of the supply chain could help keep AI's revenue growth going in the future.

And AMD’s customer pipeline is getting only better from here. In February, AMD and Meta announced a multi-year deal, under which Meta plans to deploy up to six gigawatts of AMD Instinct GPUs , with shipments for the first gigawatt deployment slated for the second half of 2026 .

That kind of scale raises the pressure on AMD .

Why this could be a real financial story for Samsung

For Samsung , it couldn't be clearer.

The company’s memory business is already taking full advantage of the AI boom . Samsung said in its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings report that it had record-high quarterly revenue and operating profit.

Related: Jensen Huang sends shocking message on Nvidia’s OpenAI stake

It also said HBM sales grew despite limited supply and that it made more money selling higher-value products such as HBM, server DDR5, and enterprise SSDs .

The Device Solutions division posted KRW 44.0 trillion in sales and KRW 16.4 trillion in operating profit in the quarter.

But the foundry business does not inspire the same level of confidence. In its second-quarter 2025 earnings materials, Samsung said foundry revenue rose at a very significant pace. But earnings are still low because of changes in the value of inventory caused by U.S. export restrictions on advanced AI chips to China and low use at mature nodes.

That is the financial tension at the center of this story.

Samsung holds a memory business taking advantage of AI demand and a foundry business that is still on the lookout of a headline-making validation win. AMD could provide exactly that.

What Samsung stands to gain:

Fresh foundry revenue

A better, stronger position in the AI chip supply chain

More trustworthy because of its advanced process technology

A big win with a marquee customer that could help bring in more big clients

A slice of AMD’s advanced manufacturing business will not just boost revenue.

It also will give Samsung something just as valuable: proof that a big AI chipmaker is willing to trust its process technology in a market that is still mostly TSMC .

That kind of credibility could help Samsung chase more high-margin business later.

The real threat is not to AMD; it's to TSMC’s grip

TSMC is still serving as the clear heavyweight.

The company has said it continues expanding advanced technology and packaging capacity, including 3-nanometer , 2-nanometer , and CoWoS capacity, across several locations.

In other words, TSMC is not standing still.

This is not about AMD suddenly cutting ties with its main manufacturing partner, but about Samsung trying to become too strategically useful to ignore.

If Samsung can use HBM4 supply to win a small slice of AMD’s logic-chip business , that suggests the rules of competition in AI semiconductors are changing.

Why this could matter for Samsung, AMD stocks

Investors might start to see HBM suppliers as more than just component vendors; they might see them as strategic power brokers.

AMD could benefit from a lower risk in the supply chain.

Samsung could tell a more interesting story about its growth by talking about how its foundries are recovering.

TSMC might have to answer new questions about whether its hold on big AI customers is getting weaker.

Memory is no longer just a component sale. It's leverage. And Samsung , after years of foundry frustration, might be readying to use that leverage to attack one of the most prized supply chains in tech.

That is the stock angle investors need to worry about.

For AMD , any Samsung tie-up could reduce supply chain risk as AI demand starts to heat up. For Samsung , it becomes a path to richer foundry revenue and an enhancement in badly needed prestige. For TSMC , it could be a bad sign that one of its biggest competitors may have finally found a weak spot.

Right now, this still looks like an opening , not a done deal.

But in a market obsessed with artificial intelligence winners , openings matter a lot. When hundreds of billions of dollars in market value ride on who controls the AI hardware stack , even a “small” supplier shift can turn into a huge story.

Related: Micron CEO drops a bombshell after Micron’s huge earnings beat

news.Title: Will Strength in the Automotive Business Drive Revenues for Qualcomm?
news.Content:
Qualcomm Incorporated QCOM has been witnessing healthy traction in the automotive segment over the past few quarters. During the first quarter of 2026, the company reported a $1.1 billion in revenues from this segment, up 15% year over year.

Its core product strategy involves providing a modular automotive platform that combines Digital Cockpit (infotainment), advanced driver assistance, connectivity that is 5G, telematics and AI compute capabilities inside the vehicle. The platforms that offer these are Snapdragon Cockpit Platform, Snapdragon Ride Flex Platform and Snapdragon Elite platforms. It boasts a worldwide client base that includes leading automakers and technology companies like Volkswagen Group, Toyota, Hyundai Mobis, Leapmotor, Li Auto and several other OEMs.

Several technology trends and other factors are driving this adoption. Qualcomm’s full-stack solution reduces complexity for OEMs and streamlines the deployment process. This also locks customers into the ecosystem, making it harder for them to switch. In the connectivity part, Qualcomm dominates in 5G, WiFi and Bluetooth integration, V2X. Along with the connectivity dominance, its growth prowess in edge AI effectively supports features like voice assistants, driver monitoring and autonomous decision systems. These factors are major growth drivers.

More than 75 million vehicles are already using Qualcomm Snapdragon Cockpit platforms. Management expects a staggering 35% year over year growth from Automotive in the second quarter of 2026. The growth is driven by growing design wins, new vehicle launches and expansion of ADAS + cockpit integration.

How Are Competitors Faring?

In the automotive segment, the company faces competition from the NVIDIA Corporation NVDA and Intel Corporation INTC.

NVIDIA’s automotive sales in the recent quarter were $604 million, up 6% year over year backed by growing adoption of self-driving platforms. NVIDIA DRIVE solutions include a comprehensive stack of hardware and software tools designed to support autonomous vehicles. It is also swiftly working on V2X technology, specifically in AI-driven connectivity for autonomous vehicles. The company is collaborating with more than 320 automakers, tier-one suppliers, automotive research institutions, HD mapping companies and start-ups to develop and deploy AI systems for self-driving vehicles.

Intel’s Mobileye has rapidly expanded in the autonomous car technology market. With its comprehensive Mobileye business offerings related to cameras, in-car networking, sensor chips, roadway mapping, cloud software, machine learning and data management, Intel is gaining ground in the autonomous car technology market. Several manufacturers, such as Toyota, Volkswagen, Ford and Zeekr, use Intel’s Mobileye solution for driver assistance systems.

Story Continues

QCOM’s Price Performance, Valuation and Estimates

Qualcomm’s shares have lost 18.8% over the past year against the industry’s growth of 57.3%.

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Going by the price/earnings ratio, the company's shares currently trade at 11.51 forward earnings, lower than 26.99 for the industry.

Earnings estimates for fiscal 2026 have declined 7% to $11.16 over the past 60 days, while those for fiscal 2027 have also decreased 7.46% to $11.41.

Qualcomm stock currently carries a Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell).

You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

news.Title: nLIGHT's Evolving Mix: Defense Programs and Sensing Fuel Next Phase
news.Content:
nLIGHT LASR builds the high-power laser building blocks that are increasingly central to modern defense systems. The company’s position spans directed energy lasers and optical sensing, alongside a smaller commercial footprint in advanced manufacturing.

The key theme for investors is the mix. In 2025, aerospace and defense became the dominant driver of revenue and visibility, supported by funded backlog, late-stage programs, and a sensing portfolio that began moving from design wins into production.

nLIGHT’s 2025 Mix Shows Defense as the Center of Gravity

In 2025, total revenue was $261.3 million, up 31.6% from 2024. Laser Products accounted for 68.6% of revenue, while Advanced Development contributed 31.4%. The end-market mix underscores where the center of gravity sits. Aerospace and defense represented 67% of 2025 revenue, while microfabrication and industrial comprised 18.1% and 14.9%, respectively. nLIGHT’s top customers include BAE Systems, Northrop Grumman, Raytheon Technologies, and the U.S. Government.

Defense momentum accelerated through 2025 and culminated in record fourth-quarter aerospace and defense performance tied to HELSI-2 shipments and the DE M-SHORAD delivery. The DE M-SHORAD milestone included delivery of a 50-kilowatt coherent beam-combined high-energy laser and beam director. That milestone dynamic is also why quarterly outcomes can move around: development revenue can surge when deliveries land and then reset as milestones roll forward.

Visibility increasingly hinges on funded backlog. Funded backlog was about $162 million as of Dec. 31, 2025, essentially flat year over year, and management pointed to it as a key support for continued growth. The setup also matters for 2026, with late-stage programs such as HELSI-2 viewed as a substantial contributor as deliveries expand.

nLIGHT’s Laser Sensing Adds a Second Defense Growth Track

Sensing became a more tangible growth track in 2025 as programs progressed from design wins to production. A $50 million contract signed in the third quarter of 2025 for a long-running U.S. missile program highlighted embedded positions tied to munitions restocking.

In the fourth quarter of 2025, the company began low-rate initial production on a new classified sensing program. Management also pointed to near-term growth from existing programs already in full-rate production, alongside a broader mix as additional programs move through low-rate initial production over the next one to two years.

Strategically, sensing helps diversify defense exposure beyond directed energy. As programs mature, the portfolio can reduce reliance on milestone-driven development swings and support a higher-value mix driven by product shipments.

Story Continues

LASR’s Manufacturing Footprint and Capacity Plans

Capacity and execution readiness are central because directed energy deliveries and sensing ramps require dependable throughput. nLIGHT is building out additional capacity in Longmont, Colorado, adding 50,000 square feet of leased manufacturing and office space intended to more than double current capacity and enable multiple simultaneous beam-combined laser builds.

The company is also investing in internal manufacturing and supplier takt-time improvements to reduce lead-time risk. That focus is meant to tighten schedules and improve delivery reliability as volumes scale on large, milestone-driven programs.

Balance sheet strength supports this buildout. A February 2026 follow-on offering strengthened liquidity, with gross proceeds of roughly $201 million before fees and management citing more than a quarter-billion dollars of pro forma cash to fund capacity expansion and supply chain investments.

LASR’s Outlook and Management Commentary

For the first quarter of 2026, nLIGHT expects revenues between $70 million and $76 million, with an expected mix of approximately $54 million in product revenue and $19 million in development revenue. Development revenue is guided down sequentially due to the 4Q’25 delivery of the DE M-SHORAD 50-kW module.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for first-quarter 2026 revenues is pegged at $70.6 million indicating 36.6% growth from the figure reported in the year-ago quarter. The consensus mark for earnings is currently pegged at 8 cents per share, up couple of cents over the past 30 days. LASR reported loss of 4 cents per share in the year-ago quarter.

nLight Price and Consensus

nLight price-consensus-chart | nLight Quote

For 2026, nLIGHT expects total revenue growth versus 2025, with A&D expected to grow double digits year over year, supported by existing backlog. The company highlighted a ~$25 million to $30 million fiscal 2026 revenue headwind from the exit of cutting and welding, with modest contribution in the first half and near zero by the second half.

Zacks Rank & Stocks to Consider

nLIGHT currently has a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell).

Guidewire Software GWRE, Applied Materials AMAT and Qnity Electronics Q are some stocks worth buying in the broader Zacks Computer and Technology sector. All three stocks currently sport a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) each. You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank stocks here.

Long-term earnings growth for Guidewire, Applied Materials and Qnity Electronics is pegged at 12.6%, 20.2% and 11.5%, respectively. In terms of share price movement, Guidewire shares have declined 20.9%, while Applied Materials and Qnity Electronics have surged 44.9% and 43.7%, respectively, on a year-over-year basis.

news.Title: What’s Behind Applied Materials, Inc. (AMAT)’s AI Push
news.Content:
Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMAT ) is among the 12 Tech Stocks with Best Earnings Growth in 2026 . On March 10, Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMAT) presented at the Cantor Fitzgerald Global Technology & Industrial Growth Conference, highlighting its strategic vision in the changing semiconductor landscape. The company outlined the growing demand for AI systems, which is projected to accelerate growth across its leading-edge logic, DRAM, and advanced packaging markets.

Although concerns related to lagging NAND growth exist, the overall outlook remains bullish, the leadership noted. All thanks to AI system demand and strategic investments in data centers, Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMAT) is well-positioned for strong growth. The company targets a free cash flow return of 80%-100%, with past year’s distributions at 86%. In 2026, the company’s silicon revenue is estimated to climb more than 20%, with potential for sustained double-digit growth in the upcoming year.

electronics-6055226_1280

Additionally, investments by leading cloud service providers in AI data centers are projected to surge to $700 billion from $600 billion in 2027. Leading logic, DRAM, and advanced packaging are poised to rise over 20% this year, with ICAPS remaining flat. As stated by Brice Hill, CFO of Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMAT),

“We’ve seen a lot of pull from our customers for more advanced logic, more DRAM, and more advanced packaging solutions.”

Back on March 5, Erste Group started coverage on Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMAT) with a Hold rating. The firm believes that the acceleration of AI is driving massive investments in advanced semiconductors, DRAM, and the latest technologies. The firm’s optimism aligns with the majority, as 78% of the analysts covering the stock are bullish on the company.

Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMAT), based in California, is a provider of manufacturing equipment, services, and software to industries such as the semiconductor and display industries. Founded in 1967, the company operates through Semiconductor Systems, Applied Global Services, and Display segments.

news.Title: Chip Stocks Pop as Trump Hits Pause on Iran Strikes
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Nvidia ( NASDAQ:NVDA ) and Advanced Micro Devices ( NASDAQ:AMD ) led gains in semiconductor stocks Monday after President Donald Trump said the U.S. and Iran held productive talks toward resolving hostilities in the Middle East.

Both AMD and Nvidia Jumps 2% on the news.

The president said he instructed the Department of War to postpone military strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure for five days while discussions continue this week. Nvidia shares climbed about 3% in premarket trading, while AMD added roughly 2%.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 4 Warning Signs with NVDA.

Is NVDA fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Other chipmakers including Broadcom ( NASDAQ:AVGO ), Intel ( NASDAQ:INTC ), Texas Instruments (TXN), Marvell Technology ( NASDAQ:MRVL ), Analog Devices ( NASDAQ:ADI ) and Qualcomm (QCOM) also traded higher. Artificial intelligence-linked stocks such as Oracle (ORCL), Microsoft (MSFT) and Palantir Technologies ( NASDAQ:PLTR ) rose as well.

The gains came as investors appeared to welcome signs of reduced geopolitical tensions that had raised concerns about potential disruptions to supply chains and energy markets. The talks are expected to continue through the week.
