news.Title: 外資狂賣839億元 逆勢加碼華邦電與群創 3月以來賣超逾8000億元
news.Content:
台股今 (23) 日量縮重挫 821 點，創台股第 11 大跌點，收在 32722 點，摜破 3 萬 3 千點大關、回測季線，外資持續賣超 839.22 億元，但對雙 D 族群操作不同調，記憶體方面，大買華邦電 1.3 萬張，但調節力積電 3.4 萬張，面板則青睞群創，但減碼友達。

中東戰事近期愈演愈烈，加重台股近期跌勢，連續三天跌掉 1626.08 點，同一期間外資也加大賣超共 2152.82 億元，累計 3 月以來，外資賣超金額已高達 8044.23 億元。

三大法人今天合計賣超 1046.27 億元，外資賣超 839.22 億元，自營商賣超 311.12 億元，外資與自營商皆呈現連 3 賣，投信則逆勢加碼 104.07 億元，連 2 買；台指期方面，外資增加空單 1351 口，未平倉空單口數提升至 37717 口。

外資今日針對記憶體與面板族群操作不同調，記憶體方面，大買華邦電 (2344-TW) 達 1.3 萬張，但調節力積電 (6770-TW) 約 3.4 萬張，面板則青睞群創 (3481-TW) 約 8500 張，但減碼友達 (2409-TW) 約 3.2 萬張。

傳產方面同樣有所差異，外資偏愛長榮航 (2618-TW)，一共掃貨 2 萬張，但雙雙賣超中鋼 (2002-TW)、中石化 (1314-TW)，一共賣超 7.2 萬張。

投信與自營商也同步調節記憶體族群，投信提款華邦電與南亞科 (2408-TW) 共 1.1 萬張，自營商瞄準旺宏 (2337-TW)、力積電 (6770-TW)、華邦電，三檔共賣超約 1.5 萬張。

news.Title: 全球最大嵌入式展報捷！擷發科入列聯發科AI生態系 搶攻歐洲Edge AI商機
news.Content:
ASIC設計服務與AI軟體業者擷發科技（ 7796 ）在德國紐倫堡登場的Embedded World 2026傳出捷報。公司指出，此次不僅與NVIDIA 等業者同列聯發科AIoT合作夥伴名單，也因鎖定Edge AI與客製化ASIC的整合方案，帶動展位來客與跨國合作洽詢同步升溫。聯發科先前亦透露，今年在Embedded World將以Genio與IoT平台為核心，攜手生態系夥伴展示超過50項應用，凸顯邊緣AI已成兵家必爭之地。

市場解讀，這次擷發能與國際大廠同列聯發科AIoT陣營，象徵其在Edge AI軟體部署、異質平台整合與ASIC客製化設計能力，已進一步被國際供應鏈看見。尤其在AI應用從雲端加速向終端裝置延伸之際，工業電腦、智慧製造、安防監控、無人載具與智慧城市等場域，正成為邊緣AI商轉速度最快的一環。Embedded World官方也指出，今年展會主軸之一正是智慧、連網與具AI能力的嵌入式系統。

擷發此次主打的AIVO視覺AI平台，除原有安防辨識應用外，也進一步切入自主無人機場景。公司表示，該方案可支援暴力行為、火焰、煙霧與刀械等多元偵測，並讓無人機在通訊受干擾環境下，仍可維持一定程度自主運作，鎖定消防安保、工安巡檢、地貌測繪及廣域畜牧管理等市場。先前公司在展前就已對外揭露，無人機與歐美市場拓展將是今年重要布局方向。

除軟體平台外，擷發也強打XEdgAI跨平台部署能力，並攜手工業電腦與歐洲AI晶片夥伴進行整合展示，訴求降低系統整合商導入高效能AI應用的門檻。董事長楊健盟先前指出，邊緣AI真正痛點不只在模型本身，而在軟體流程與底層異質硬體間的碎片化問題；公司近年即瞄準這塊缺口，從AI模型部署一路延伸到ASIC設計服務，希望把AI專案從POC推進到量產。

在ASIC端，擷發透露，已接獲整合高算力、低功耗NPU的Edge AI ASIC SoC專案需求，目標鎖定高隱私、高資安應用場域，使終端設備可在不依賴雲端條件下執行大型語言模型推理。若後續客戶專案順利推進，將有機會進一步邁向晶圓代工投片。法人認為，隨各國企業對資料主權與在地運算需求提高，具備「AI軟體平台 + 客製晶片」整合能力的台廠，將更有機會切入歐洲工業、車用與國防周邊應用鏈。

此外，集團旗下Arculus System推出的EDA工具，也成為此次展會另一亮點，主打以AI協助ASIC設計流程中的連線檢查與效能分析，藉此降低手動開發負擔、提早找出瓶頸。隨先進製程與異質整合使晶片開發複雜度大幅攀升，EDA工具的重要性同步提高，也讓擷發從過去較偏單一設計服務業者，逐步朝兼具平台、工具與晶片整合能力的解決方案供應商轉型。

整體來看，擷發這次在Embedded World 2026的能見度提升，不只是單一展會曝光，更反映出台灣中小型IC設計與AI軟體業者，正透過聯發科、 NVIDIA等國際生態系鏈結，加速切入全球Edge AI應用市場。隨歐洲對工業AI 、低功耗運算與在地部署需求持續升溫，擷發後續跨國專案轉單與商業化進度，將成市場關注焦點。

news.Title: 《陸股》滬指跌3.63%、險守3800點；恆指跌3.54%
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-23 17:40:50 新聞中心 發佈

受中東局勢升級影響，亞洲股市集體下跌，中國滬深兩市三大指數今(23)日集體開低，早盤呈現低檔震盪，午前跌幅擴大，午後指數進一步走弱；盤中滬指接連跌破3900點、3800點，尾盤跌幅小幅收斂，最終險守3800點，三大指數收盤跌幅均超過3%。盤面上，算力硬體、AI應用、雲端運算、消費電子、半導體、人形機器人等題材紛紛下挫，黃金、航空、券商、房地產板塊跌幅靠前；煤炭股逆勢上漲。整體來看，兩市個股呈普跌態勢，下跌個股近5,200檔，滬指險守3800點，終場收跌3.63%，日K連三黑。

上證指數今日收於3813.28點，下跌143.77點或3.63%；深證成指收於13345.51點，下跌520.69點或3.76%；創業板指收於3235.22點，下跌116.88點或3.49%；科創50收於1261.44點，下跌56.87點或4.31%；中證A500收於5466.90點，下跌192.15點或3.40%。

成交量方面，滬市今成交1兆862億元(人民幣,下同)，深市成交1兆3,453億元，兩市成交額2兆4,315億元，較前一交易日量增約6%。

從盤面上來看，煤炭開採加工、油氣開採及服務板塊漲幅居前；貴金屬、旅遊及酒店、元件板塊跌幅居前。

港股方面，中東衝突再升級，香港恆生指數今日開盤下跌1.93%，呈現開低走低行情。盤面上，恆生科技指數跌3.28%，大型科技股、大金融股(銀行、保險、券商)、中字頭股等權重齊挫，引領大市下跌，其中，百度跌逾5%，快手、網易、小米、美團、阿里巴巴、京東等跌逾3%，騰訊跌近2%。恆指終場收於24382.47點，下跌894.85點或3.54%，日K連三黑。

《陸股》滬指漲0.32%、日K翻紅；恆指收復兩萬六

《陸股》滬指跌0.85%、日K連四黑；恆指日K翻黑

資料來源- MoneyDJ理財網

news.Title: 《數雲端》昕力資訊揪群聯等12家夥伴 AISO主權AI產業聯盟成軍
news.Content:
【時報記者張漢綺台北報導】昕力資訊(7781)舉行AISO主權AI產業聯盟簽署儀式，宣布攜手群聯電子(8299)，集結包含Gogolook、凱鈿、台灣銘板(6593)、大同世界科技、安圖斯、興創知能、景宜、智聯服務、精英電腦、豐康科技等12間國內知名軟硬體夥伴，與指導單位工研院共同展開策略合作，並與國泰金控(2882)與台達電子(2308)兩家重量級企業完成簽約。

昕力資訊表示，2026年被視為全球「主權AI元年」，AISO聯盟主打AI軟硬體整合式方案、整機交付的商業模式切入企業需求，降低企業在成本與維運上的門檻，推動主權AI方案更快進入規模化應用，並以拓展海外市場為方向，聯盟不只著眼於這塊市場遠景，而是打造一套「AISO Inside」的台灣標準，能確保100% 地端部署與物理隔離，完全符合歐盟AI法案與台灣資安規範。

昕力資訊董事長姚勝富指出，企業AI應用的關鍵瓶頸在於「企業端導入」，市場不缺AI技術，真正困難的是將AI能力對接到企業實際流程，同時兼顧資料治理與資安合規要求，為加速企業端應用落地，公司因此整合多家產業夥伴共同成立AISO聯盟，建立軟硬整合平台，以完整生態系，滿足金融、醫療、公部門等高價值產業之需求。

業界認為，AISO聯盟以平台、營運商、成員三方協作的模式，可解決企業導入AI最棘手的「最後一哩路」工程，一站式的服務，也能打破導入AI時的多頭馬車困境。

台達電資訊長曾立峰說明，群聯aiDAPTIV技術結合昕力軟體內建的一體機方案，讓企業在相同預算下，同時擁有高效能系統與 AI 應用軟體，可透過「AISO Inside」模式實現100%自主管理，加速落實 AI 智慧化轉型。

news.Title: 昕力資訊攜群聯等夥伴成立AISO主權AI產業聯盟 以整機交付加速企業導入
news.Content:
昕力資訊（7781）攜手群聯電子（8299）及多家軟硬體夥伴，成立AISO主權AI產業聯盟，鎖定企業導入AI最棘手的「最後一哩路」，主打軟硬整合、整機交付與交機即用模式，加速主權AI落地。

該聯盟提出「AISO Inside」架構，強調100％地端部署、資料不出境與物理隔離，鎖定金融、醫療與公部門等高監管產業需求。

國泰金控（2882）、台達電（2308）亦簽約加入，顯示主權AI已由技術驗證邁向實際商轉。法人看好，台廠可望憑藉ICT硬體優勢結合軟體整合能力，搶攻全球主權AI商機。

news.Title: 昕力資攜手群聯推AISO聯盟 已集結12家業者 今年可望見成果
news.Content:
興櫃資訊服務商昕力資訊（7781）攜手群聯電子（8299）今（23）日正式成立AISO主權AI產業聯盟，瞄準企業導入AI的兩大痛點。昕力資訊董事長姚勝富表示，聯盟將降低企業導入門檻與複雜度，現階段聯盟已有12家成員，預計今年內可望看到具體成果。

姚勝富指出，目前AI在企業端落地面臨兩大挑戰，一是硬體成本昂貴，二是軟體導入流程繁複，從安裝、整合到測試均需耗費大量時間與資源，「這些因素都讓AI難以真正進入百工百業」。AISO聯盟透過平台整合軟硬體資源，降低導入門檻並提升效率，使企業能以更低成本、更快速度完成部署。

在角色定位上，姚勝富表示，昕力資訊將同時扮演軟體供應商與平台營運者，負責制定技術規範、整合軟硬體夥伴資源，並依客戶需求媒合適合解決方案。現階段聯盟已有12家成員，未來也將持續擴大，邀請更多不同領域業者加入。

在市場布局方面，昕力資除深耕台灣外，也已拓展至東南亞與日本市場，包括越南、泰國、馬來西亞及菲律賓等地，未來將加速區域擴張，推動AI解決方案輸出海外。

群聯電子執行長潘健成表示，企業導入AI時最在意的並非單一硬體，而是能否直接使用的完整系統解決方案，「使用者要的是系統，不是硬體」。他指出，過去群聯在硬體領域具優勢，但在軟體整合與推廣上相對有限，透過與昕力合作，可強化整體解決方案能力。

潘健成進一步指出，目前全球企業普遍面臨AI導入困境，「知道要做AI，但不知道怎麼做，且成本過高」，導致實際落地案例有限。AISO聯盟目標在於建立可複製的成功模式，只要完成少數成功案例，便可快速複製至各產業，加速AI普及。

他強調，AI應用的關鍵在於整合能力，現實上沒有單一軟體公司能滿足企業所有需求，因此需透過平台整合不同專長業者，提供完整解決方案，讓企業從「無法導入」走向「可商業化運作」。

在硬體配置方面，聯盟採開放架構，透過伺服器整合多家供應商資源，依不同應用情境與算力需求進行客製化配置，提升彈性並降低成本。

姚勝富表示，目前已開始與企業客戶接洽，包括金融機構等指標客戶已進入測試階段，若測試順利將進一步擴大導入規模。整體而言，聯盟對後續發展持樂觀看法，並將持續擴大合作與投資，推動台灣AI產業由硬體導向轉向軟硬整合發展。

湯暖萱／財經特派記者，主跑主計處、營建、百貨紡織等

news.Title: 《韓股》韓股重挫逾6%觸發熔斷 韓元貶新低
news.Content:
【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】23日，開低的韓國股市收盤時重挫，主要是中東危機持續惡化，加上美伊兩國在荷姆茲海峽的問題上仍在對峙，局勢絲毫沒有緩和的跡象。韓股KOSPI最後大幅下跌375.45點或6.49%，收5,405.75點。同時，韓元兌美元匯價也貶至十幾年以來最低，外資法人合計共賣超高達7.5兆韓元。

分析師指出，自從二月底美以聯手攻打伊朗之後，韓股波動劇烈，三月四日一度崩跌超過12%，隨後在下一個交易日五日又出現強勁反彈，但在接下來的一周，又大跌超過5%。韓股23日崩跌，韓國交易所曾暫停程式化交易五分鐘，已是今年第六次執行這種交易限制令。

韓國青瓦台表示，李在明總統計畫在23日召開並親自主持一般性的安全會議，評估韓國的安全情勢與因應措施。同時，韓國總統府也否認，國家安全顧問要訪問美國華府的媒體報導。不過，軍事專家指出，李在明連普通的安全會議都要參加，代表整個國際局勢，似乎正朝著另個險峻的方向在前進。

市值最高的三星電子重挫6.57%，記憶體晶片競爭對手SK海力士也大跌7.35%。另外，韓國最大的汽車製造商現代汽車下挫6.19%，軍工大廠韓華航空航太大跌3.18%，大型金融集團KB大跌6.38%，韓國最大船公司hmm也大跌6.78%。

韓元兌一美元匯價貶至17年來新低，也就是說，上次韓元重貶時，是出現在2009年3月9日全球金融海嘯期間，當時韓元一度貶至1549。目前，韓元在1511.64附近。

news.Title: 玉山投信稱，能源供應中斷風險升溫，上週台韓印成賣超重心
news.Content:
【財訊快報／記者劉居全報導】全球能源供應中斷風險升高，市場資金也開始提高戒備，玉山投信指出，上週國際資金持續自亞股撤出，除了東北亞雙雄台灣與南韓分別遭賣超56.46億美元、22.08億美元外，印度也遭提款26.67億美元，成為亞股中最悲情的三大市場。不過從亞股表現來看漲跌互見，反映出內資態度並非與外資同步，玉山投信表示，上週韓股大舉反彈5.36%，完全無懼外資賣超影響，泰國、馬來西亞單週也有逾1%漲幅，但相對地，陸股、越南股市分別下跌2.19%、2.86%。

保德信全球藍籌ETF(009810)經理人黃相慈表示，上週布蘭特原油一度衝破112美元，加大通膨反彈的壓力，市場也擔心將影響聯準會降息節奏，甚至部分交易員認為今年可能轉為升息，美債殖利率隨之走高，進一步壓抑美股走勢，短期地緣政治風險升高、油價高檔震盪與利率政策不確定性的影響之下，預估短期市場波動恐加劇。不過，過去5次重要戰爭事件發生後半年與一年，美股平均漲幅約12%，顯示地緣政治衝擊多屬短線干擾，中長期趨勢仍回歸基本面與經濟成長動能。

黃相慈指出，此次FOMC會議雖上調今年核心PCE預估至2.7%，然而GDP也上修至2.4%，顯示聯準會官員對經濟動能的信心有所提升。至於AI趨勢指標之一的輝達GTC大會中，執行長黃仁勳表示AI晶片到明年的潛在商機將達1兆美元，整體來看，在美國經濟維持成長，同時AI商機持續擴張的情況下，美股仍具備基本面與獲利成長雙重支撐。

針對美股走勢，黃相慈分析，就技術面觀察，美股今年以來回檔5%，整體仍處於高檔修正階段，短期內指數走勢預期將維持震盪整理格局。目前下方支撐區有機會進一步測試位於6449點的年線位置，若指數能於年線獲得支撐並止穩，則有助於修復市場信心，並為後續反彈行情奠定基礎。

至於歐洲方面，黃相慈說明，歐洲央行(ECB)、英國央行(BoE)、瑞士央行及瑞典央行於上週均決議維持基準利率不變，不過，ECB會後聲明透露出偏鷹的政策訊號，受到中東戰事升溫推動能源價格大幅上行的背景下，歐元區正面臨通膨上行風險與經濟成長放緩壓力，若情勢進一步惡化，將準備採取必要行動，以確保通膨回到2%的目標。

黃相慈指出，ECB總裁拉加德表示，央行目前「處於良好位置，且具備充分政策工具」，足以因應潛在的重大外部衝擊，此番言論帶動利率預期快速調整，利率期貨市場報價顯示，市場預期今年ECB可能升息2碼以因應通膨風險，而市場也預估ECB最快將於4月開始討論升息，並且於6月開始實施。

陸股方面，PGIM保德信中國中小基金經理人陳舜津指出，受中東地緣衝突升溫影響，川普原定於兩週後出訪北京的行程延後約一個半月，市場目前預期將於5月中旬訪問中國，在短期缺乏正面催化劑的情況下，A股市場亦受到一定衝擊，指數呈現震盪走勢。

不過，陳舜津表示，從資金面來看，近期滬深兩市成交金額仍穩定維持在2兆人民幣以上，顯示市場買盤尚未明顯退潮，加上強勢人民幣匯率有望提升人民幣資產吸引力，外資主動型資金入市意願提升，為市場籌碼增加挹注。惟需留意，市場結構性分歧仍將持續，產業表現呈現輪動格局，其中以AI為核心的科技板塊，仍是企業獲利成長的主要驅動力，在基本面支撐下，由成長股主導的市場風格可望延續。

受到上週五美股大跌影響，今(23)日台股大跌2.45%，下挫821.38點，指數收在32722.5點，成交金額6770.28億元。保德信市值動能50 ETF(009803)經理人楊立楷指出，央行上修2026年經濟成長率至7.28%，搭配台灣外銷訂單前2月累計創新高，反映出台灣經濟基本面仍相當有力，科技業訂單及獲利維持強勁。然而受到美伊戰事持續緊繃，甚至有愈演愈烈的態勢，加上美歐台等央行的利率決策出現鷹派言論，預期短線將以整理方式消化賣壓，布局台股宜低接不追高。

楊立楷表示，雖然短期台股受到地緣政治事件左右，不過從上週全球科技業矚目的輝達GTC大會可窺知，AI已正式進入「大量實際使用」階段，重點不再只是訓練模型，而是讓AI持續進行思考、推理與執行任務，為未來資料中心的角色將從傳統資料儲存設施，轉變為生產AI推理代幣的智慧工廠，2027年整體AI基礎建設需求將達1兆美元以上。

楊立楷說明，整體來看，本次大會傳遞出AI應用加速落地的正向訊號，並有利於帶動AI伺服器、散熱、網通與相關零組件的長期需求成長。目前Vera Rubin平台已獲AWS、Google Cloud、微軟Azure、Oracle Cloud等雲端業者支持，預計下半年開始陸續上市，將挹注台廠相關供應鏈需求持續成長。

news.Title: 《日股》日經大跌1857點 今年漲幅幾近歸零
news.Content:
【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】中東戰事迄今沒有趨緩下，外界憂心通膨再起以及經濟成長受到衝擊，日經23日一開盤就跌破五萬三整數關卡，盤中賣壓不斷最後甚至失守五萬二，收51,515.49點，大跌1,857.04點或3.48%，今年迄今漲幅大幅縮水至2%左右。同時，東證收在3,486.44點，也大跌122.96點或3.41%。

路透社指出，日經早盤一度重挫超過5%，曾把今年以來的所有漲幅都吃掉。二月時，日經指數曾飆升至59,332.43點歷史新高，並有望突破60,000點大關，主要受惠於日相高市早苗的刺激計畫，可望加速日企成長。

因日本石油供應的關鍵水道荷姆茲海峽仍然被封鎖，故日經指數23日交易時的表現跟歷史高點相比，已大幅下挫超過14%，主要是因為，日本約有90%的石油運輸都依賴荷姆茲海峽。

分析師指出，在中東衝突持續沒有停歇之下，油價短時間內大漲，迫使日企可能不得不下調下一個財年的業績預估。另外，國際油價大漲也會拖累到經濟成長，進而影響到企業的獲利能力。

日本央行上周維持利率不變，但警告油價飆升可能會引起通膨大漲，因此保留提前升息的可能性。分析師表示，通膨可能會對全球央行帶來升息的壓力，對股市不利外，對債市也不好。日本10年期公債殖利率，一度上漲六個基本點，升至2.320%，創一月21日以來最高。

主要晶片類股大都走低，例如，愛德萬測試大跌5.2%，東京威力科創大跌2.6%。人工智慧AI概念股中，軟銀跌1.4%，藤倉大跌4%，古河電氣工業大跌近6%，住友電氣工業也重挫超過7%。

news.Title: 《台北股市》投信23日賣超前十大 台塑、華新集團各兩檔 淪前五
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股23日加權指數收32722.50點，跌821.38點或2.45%，總成交值7114.80億元。三大法人賣超1046.27億元，其中外資賣超839.23億元，投信、自營商分別買超104.07億元及賣超311.12億元。

投信賣超前十大個股，依類別分，5檔電子股、5檔傳產股；依股價分，6檔百元股、3檔中價位股及1檔銅板股；賣超張數方面，榜首為南亞(1303)，賣超9532張，台玻(1802)、華邦電(2344)名列二、三名，分別賣超6117張及賣超5895張。第四及第五名為南亞科(2408)、華新(1605)，其他依序為裕融(9941)、國巨*(2327)、旺宏(2337)、臻鼎-KY(4958)及中砂(1560)。

至於前十大個股中賣超金額前三大，則為南亞科(2408)11.64億元、南亞(1303)7.03億元、華邦電(2344)6.34億元。

●投信23日賣超前十名：

1.南亞(1303)收73.80元，跌4.60元或5.87%，成交量7.06萬張，投信賣超9532張、估7.03億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超1萬197張、估7.55億元。

2.台玻(1802)收51.90元，跌5.70元或9.90%，成交量8.32萬張，投信賣超6117張、估3.17億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超1萬749張、估5.84億元。

3.華邦電(2344)收107.50元，跌2.50元或2.27%，成交量14.87萬張，投信賣超5895張、估6.34億元，由前一交易日買超6萬7025張轉為賣超。

4.南亞科(2408)收223.00元，跌11.00元或4.70%，成交量7.87萬張，投信賣超5219張、估11.64億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超1萬1559張、估27.21億元。

5.華新(1605)收30.15元，跌1.70元或5.34%，成交量3.16萬張，投信賣超4947張、估1.49億元，由前一交易日買超1297張轉為賣超。

6.裕融(9941)收77.90元，跌1.10元或1.39%，成交量0.47萬張，投信賣超2656張、估2.07億元，為連9日賣超，累計賣超5812張、估4.55億元。

7.國巨*(2327)收253.00元，跌18.00元或6.64%，成交量2.85萬張，投信賣超1113張、估2.82億元，由前一交易日買超321張轉為賣超。

8.旺宏(2337)收148.50元，跌11.50元或7.19%，成交量4.46萬張，投信賣超1000張、估1.49億元，為連6日賣超，累計賣超3997張、估5.7億元。

9.臻鼎-KY(4958)收201.00元，跌8.50元或4.06%，成交量2.13萬張，投信賣超783張、估1.58億元，由連2日累計買超195張轉為賣超。

10.中砂(1560)收442.00元，跌32.00元或6.75%，成交量0.41萬張，投信賣超780張、估3.45億元，為連8日賣超，累計賣超3596張、估16.52億元。

●前三大個股近五日籌碼：

1.南亞(1303)三大法人累計賣超5萬429張，其中外資賣超6萬6056張、投信買超1萬9534張、自營商賣超3907張；融資增3192張、融券減1831張，券資比2.96%。

2.台玻(1802)三大法人累計賣超8萬3511張，其中外資賣超7萬9944張、投信賣超4619張、自營商買超1052張；融資增8692張、融券增844張，券資比17.19%。

3.華邦電(2344)三大法人累計賣超2萬9118張，其中外資賣超9萬581張、投信買超6萬7079張、自營商賣超5616張；融資增6142張、融券減4552張，券資比4.12%。

註：台股總成交值不含鉅額交易；個股買賣超僅23日未加計鉅額交易；資券統計截至前一交易日。（編輯：沈培華）

news.Title: 戰火恐慌最重殺1082點! 台股跌821點收32722
news.Content:
[ 非凡新聞 ]記者謝旻蓁,攝影詹明樺

台股又見黑色星期一，在中東衝突延燒下，台股開盤大跌超過千點，失守33000點整數大關，電子類股全面走弱，台積電最低下跌55元來到1785元，終場下跌30元收在1810元，鴻海跌破200元，收場收在196元，台達電、聯發科也有明顯跌幅，資金避風港聚焦在戰爭相關概念股，像是亞聚、台聚、欣天然等皆亮燈漲停，另外像是萬潤、弘塑等半導體設備概念股也湧現資金進駐，大盤指數終場下跌821點，收在32722點，成交量達6754億元。

民眾：「比較沒那麼在意，會再漲回來，基本上都在科技股，有南亞科然後台積電之類的，看有沒有跌破5日均線，或是月均線，有跌破就是止損。」

民眾：「我覺得可預期，就是之前很高，所以現在有點回彈，也是很正常，前兩周就大概做好準備，就有稍微出一些，就不會留這麼多。」

美伊隔空交火升級，投資人風險調控情緒再度反映在股市當中，台股周一（23）開盤大跌逾千點，最低觸及32461點，隨後低接買盤進場，跌幅逐漸收斂，但仍跌破月線，失守33000點大關。

統一投顧董事長 黎方國：「整個地緣政治的狀況，並沒有緩和，所以今天台股就是，直接就開低走低，而且持續下殺，而且整個多頭的線型受到破壞，目前是跌破月線，而且是整個月線往下彎曲，今天主要的類股都下跌，半導體類股是帶頭下殺的主要類股，比較好的狀況是傳產類股，相對之前沒有什麼漲到，抗漲的股票今天也相對抗跌。」

觀察盤面走勢，電子類股普遍走弱，台積電最低下跌55元來到1785元，後續在1800元附近整理；鴻海跌破200元整數大關，台達電、聯發科等大型電子權值股也成為外資提款機；記憶體族群多檔跌停，金融、航運也未能吸引買盤進駐。市場資金持續流入戰爭相關概念股，像是塑化股、天然氣概念股等；萬潤、弘塑等台積電設備概念股也吸引資金卡位。

專家分析，油價仍是這波股市重挫的導火線，後續觀察重點落在融資餘額水位，以及外資資金流向是否傳遞止穩訊號。

富邦投顧董事長 陳奕光：「油價落在120（美元）到130（美元），可能指數就會去挑戰，前波這部分的低點31529（點），油價再升破130來到150，大概130到150，那可能就會去挑戰整數關卡。新台幣在這個地方貶到32塊，假如說能夠止貶回升，那外資的賣超其實就會止住；看到單日的融資減到100億以上，那這也是一個止跌訊號。」

布局策略上，專家建議可留意季線的支撐力道，分批進場降低風險，同時聚焦在塑化類股、晶圓代工概念股，以及生技類股等領域，掌握市場輪動所帶來的投資契機。（記者謝旻蓁、詹明樺／台北採訪報導）

news.Title: 兆豐證券認購(售)權證終止上市彙總表
《熱門族群》記憶體殺聲隆隆 5檔吞跌停 後市多空交戰
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】AI需求噴發，前波記憶體缺貨以及漲價帶動行情，族群向上飛天，不過利空衝擊出現，外資直言，DDR4定價能力下半年將因需求轉弱而受到影響，應分別檢視各家記憶體廠產品表現，記憶體降評再開一槍，指標股南亞科(2408)、華邦電(2344)被點名，今日2檔同大盤走弱，記憶體族群殺聲隆隆，創見(2451)、晶豪科(3006)、威剛(3260)、廣穎(4973)、宇瞻(8271)苦吞跌停，帶有利多消息的旺宏(2337)同步受牽連，最低大跌逾8%。

中東戰事未歇，地緣政治風險持續干擾全球股市，不安情緒維持在高點，以及記憶體族群基本面先前已反映，外資於2月先降評群聯，至今不到一個月的時間，再度大動作降評人氣指標股南亞科及華邦電，並將首選轉移到旺宏，看好舊型快閃記憶體市場缺口，目標價估達202元，在上周五衝上170元歷史新高，今日族群整體走弱，並隨大盤下挫。

分析師指出，DRAM與NAND短缺情況加劇，記憶體仍具漲價優勢，但法人也預料，外資降評後將掀起各研究機構對記憶體後市的多空論戰。（編輯：邱致馨）

news.Title: 《韓股》外資殺紅眼！韓股崩跌、韓元創17年新低
news.Content:
【時報編譯柯婉琇綜合外電報導】中東戰火持續升高，周一南韓KOSPI指數目前暫跌約5.6%，報5459.81點，韓元則跌至17年新低。

美國總統川普和伊朗互嗆將攻擊對方的基礎設施，雙方的敵對行動若升高，恐將加劇區域危機，並使全球市場更加焦慮不安。

在岸韓元貶值0.3%，報1509.4兌一美元，創下2009年3月以來新低。

Eugene Investment & Securities分析師表示，這場戰爭很快會結束的希望正在消逝。我們不需要太過悲觀，因為受惠於強勁的科技領域，亞洲國家較以往更具優勢，且南韓政府也在規劃追加預算。不過，市場的耐心確實已所剩無幾。

南韓預算部長獲提名人Park Hong-keun周一表示，政府會盡快草擬一項追加預算案。前一日南韓政府和執政黨同意提出約25兆韓元（165.8億美元）的追加支出，為那些因油價飆漲而受到衝擊的族群提供支持。

周日獲提名出任南韓央行總裁的Shin Hyun-song則表示，隨著這場伊朗戰爭導致市場波動加劇及經濟不確定性升高，他將著眼於在通膨、成長與金融穩定之間達成平衡的政策。

指標股方面，三星電子目前跌5.52%，SK海力士跌6.26%，電池大廠LG Energy Solution跌3.99%，現代汽車跌5.13%，起亞跌4.51%，浦項鋼鐵（Posco）跌4.37%，三星生物製藥（Samsung Biologics）跌4.25%。

外資賣超韓股1.8兆韓元，給韓股帶來巨大壓力。機構投資人和散戶也站在賣方。

在這場伊朗戰爭爆發前，受惠AI行情帶動，KOSPI指數的卓越表現曾傲視全球。本月以來，該指數已下跌12%，但今年以來仍上漲30%。

在油價帶動通膨升高的疑慮籠罩下，南韓10年期公債殖利率跳升14.4個基點，至3.802%，創下2023年11月以來新高。

news.Title: 《台北股市》DRAM榮景僅剩9個月？操作策略老手一次評
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】大摩一紙報告造成記憶體股籌碼鬆動，資金在短線漲多後明顯踩踏，南亞科(2408)、華邦電(2344)等DRAM雙雄20日同步跌停，單日市值分別損失806億元、540億元，合計蒸發逾1345億元，淪台股重災區。

專家指出，記憶體族群雖遇亂流，卻非全面退潮，但考量該產業不再是齊漲齊跌的景氣循環遊戲，南亞科、華邦電雖有機會跌深反彈，但後續得靠提升DDR5的滲透率，來對抗跌價壓力；旺宏(2337)的觀察重點，不再是記憶體報價，而是MLC、SLC等特定規格的現貨價及產用率。

美系外資大摩指出，記憶體產業從全面性的「超級周期」走向「結構性分化」，針對南亞科、華邦電價評雙降，資深分析師陳榮華指出，即使超級周期步入尾聲，但並非全盤落幕，記憶體市場更不會同步降溫，而是出現明顯的資金與題材輪動。

長鑫DDR5擴產加速

大摩點出3項結構性利空，顯示其獲利高峰可能已過：

1.長鑫存儲（CXMT）的供給壓力：DDR4供給大增，長鑫預計2027年起月增1萬片產能，對以DDR3/DDR4為主要營收支柱的台系廠商，尤其是南亞科構成直接威脅，陸廠憑藉政策與成本優勢，導致市場供給結構性增加，抑制價格。

此外，長鑫在DDR5的擴產加速，雖然短期良率與品質尚在追趕，但長期將削弱國際大廠（三星、海力士、美光）及台廠在DDR5的定價權，加上力積電加入戰局，預計2027年導入1y/1z先進製程並放量，進一步增加DDR4供給，將使供需差距自下半年起縮小，並預期2027年下半年價格轉跌。

2.3C需求疲軟與價格觸頂：DDR4 8Gb合約價年增達752%，不僅基期高，也逼近報價上限，伴隨消費性電子（3C）需求縮減，下游廠商備貨意願降低，價格上漲的邊際效應遞減。

3.獲利了結壓力：華邦電過去1年股價飆漲超過5倍，目前本淨比（PB）已提前反應未來幾季的利多，外資選擇在基本面轉折前「Turning Selective」，先行獲利了結。

不過，大摩在DRAM降評的同時，卻將傳統Flash，特別是SLC/MLC NAND列為新寵，背後邏輯在於供給側的結構性收縮，與利基市場的定價權。

旺宏愛普合作IPD介面

陳榮華指出，三星、鎧俠、美光等國際大廠為追求更高的毛利，已將產能重心轉向高階的3D NAND（用於手機、SSD），導致2D NAND（如MLC、SLC）的產能幾乎消失。

然而，車用、工控、物聯網、通訊設備等利基市場對傳統Flash（2GB至64GB）的需求依然穩定。供給退出而需求仍在，造成高達40%的供給缺口，且客戶庫存水位僅6至9個月。

由於旺宏是全球少數仍能穩定且大量供應MLC NAND的廠商，在供給短缺的環境下，供應商擁有絕對的定價權，預期2026年價格將上漲超過200%，直接轉化為營收與毛利率的爆發。

此外，旺宏還預計在2028年與愛普*(6531)在IPD介面展開合作，暗示旺宏不僅受惠於傳統周期性漲價，還在布局3D IC、異質整合等高階技術，為長期的本益比重估，提供想像空間。

陳榮華並提出當前記憶體投資的3大策略要點：

1.避開標準型DRAM的「紅海」：南亞科與華邦電雖然體質良好，但面臨長鑫結構性產能過剩威脅，若DDR4報價在2026年下半年收斂、2027年轉跌，其獲利將面臨下修風險，這類族群短期反彈僅能視為跌深反彈，而非長線景氣復甦的起點。

記憶體投資3策略

2.緊抓利基型Flash的「稀缺性」：旺宏的抗跌並非單純的資金避險，而是基於實質的供需缺口，在國際大廠不願回頭生產低容量2D NAND的情況下，缺貨情況可能延續至2027年。對這類廠商，觀察重點不再是記憶體報價，而是特定規格（MLC/SLC）的現貨價與產能利用率。

3.時間節點的分歧：大摩示警DRAM超級周期僅剩9個月至1年半，也就是2027年下半年，而Flash目前正處於上升周期的起點。

總結來說，記憶體產業已不再是齊漲齊跌的景氣循環遊戲，南亞科、華邦電後續得靠提升DDR5的滲透率，來對抗跌價壓力，旺宏則正處於一個由供給斷層所驅動的獨立「小周期」之中，因其產品線避開陸廠傾銷，反而掌握了利基市場的瓶頸資源。

記憶體族群雖遇亂流，但並非全面退潮，市場資金正在從供給過剩、庫存升高的DRAM撤出，轉向供給斷層、需求穩健的傳統Flash。投資人若仍以過往「記憶體齊漲齊跌」的舊思維操作，可能會錯失結構性分化的行情。(新聞來源：旺得富理財網 李宗莉)

news.Title: 群聯：公司私募相關規劃，尚未有依法應公告之具體確定事項
news.Content:
公開資訊觀測站重大訊息公告

1.事實發生日:115/03/23

2.公司名稱:群聯電子股份有限公司

3.與公司關係(請輸入本公司或子公司):本公司。

4.相互持股比例:無。

5.傳播媒體名稱:聯合新聞網

6.報導內容:本公司就 聯合新聞網 近日刊載之報導「群聯躍輝達 GTC 會議簡報要角私募將引進大咖」所提及相關內容，說明如下：

一、媒體報導中提及之合作對象與合作內容等資訊，本公司一向秉持與客戶及合作夥伴之保密原則，不對特定客戶或個別合作關係進行評論。

二、有關報導中提及本公司私募相關規劃，本公司長期持續評估與上下游合作夥伴的各項策略性合作之可能性，惟目前尚未有依法應公告之具體確定事項。相關細節及執行情形，請依規定於公開資訊觀測站公告為準。

7.發生緣由:無。

8.因應措施:本公司對於單一客戶事件無法評論。

9.其他應敘明事項:無。

3/20櫃買市場鉅額交易日成交金額前20名排行

資料來源- MoneyDJ理財網

news.Title: 《日股》中東戰火止不住 日股債雙殺
news.Content:
【時報編譯柯婉琇綜合外電報導】中東戰火升溫加劇對通膨和經濟放緩的疑慮，周一日本股市和債市雙雙跌至數個月新低。

川普上周六警告，如果伊朗不在48小時內「全面開放」荷姆茲海峽航行，他將「摧毀」伊朗的電力設施。伊朗則放話若美國這麼做，那麼屬於美國和該地區政權的所有能源基礎設施、資訊科技（IT）設施及海水淡化設施，都將成為攻擊目標。

日經225指數早盤跌1,790.30點或3.35%，收在51,582.23點，盤初一度跌5%，將今年以來的漲幅全數回吐。東證一部指數晨收跌3.09%，報3,497.81點。

投資人看好日相高市早苖力推的激勵措施可望加快企業營運增長，日經指數在上個月站上59,332.43點歷史新高，並朝60,000點大關邁進。由於攸關日本約9成石油供應的荷姆茲海峽迄今仍然封鎖，周一，與該歷史高點相比，日經指數慘跌逾14%。

IwaiCosmo Securiies首席策略師Kazuaki Shimada表示，由於中東戰爭時間拉長導致油價飆升，日本公司也許必須下修對下一個會計年度的展望。石油價格上漲也將減緩和打擊經濟，進而影響企業獲利。

晶片股大跌，帶領日經指數下挫。晶片設備大廠愛德萬測試（Advantest）早盤收跌5.50%，其同業東京威力科創（Tokyo Electron）跌3%，稍早前一度跌近6%，半導體檢測設備廠Lasertec跌8.18%，半導體材料大廠Sumco跌5.32%，IC基板廠Ibiden跌6.91%，矽晶圓大廠信越化學工業（Shin-Etsu Chemical）跌4.52%。

重磅股、AI新創業大金主軟銀集團跌2.19%。

日本10年期公債殖利率大漲6個基點，來到2.320%，創下1月21日以來新高。

news.Title: 記憶體殺聲隆隆...南亞科力積電等13檔摔逾半根停板 分析師：小心接刀
news.Content:
台股今（23）日盤中跳水逾千點，記憶體族群殺聲隆隆，包括南亞科（2408）、力積電（6770）等13檔重挫逾半根停板，其中3檔打到跌停版。分析師表示，不建議在跌幅劇烈時立即進場，避免接刀風險；目前可先留意，指標股是否能重新站穩五日均線並展現轉強跡象。

截至上午9時30分，下跌逾5%個股包括南亞科、威剛（3260）、創見（2451）、品安（8088）、力積電、華東（8110）、鈺創（5351）、旺宏（2337）、晶豪科（3006）、福懋科（8131）、南茂（8150）。

另外宇瞻（8271）、宜鼎（5289）和廣穎（4973）仍鎖死在跌停版。

至於拉回是否為買點？顧德投顧分析師張家豪受訪表示，不建議在股價大跌時盲目進場承接，避免接刀的風險，觀察重點在於個股是否仍維持多頭走勢，建議採取見到轉強跡象再買入，而非在下跌過程中直接買入。他強調，若股票在拉回後一兩天內能重新上漲且趨勢架構不變，此時的拉回才是真正的買點。

在指標股觀察方面，張家豪指出，記憶體股王群聯（8299）被視為族群領頭羊，重要的觀察點位在於五日均線約 1922 元，若群聯能重新站，將對整個族群產生帶動與穩定的效果。

此外，另一檔值得關注的標的是前一波表現強勢的旺宏（2337），由於其股價較為接地氣，也是許多一般投資人會參考的指標，若重新站回五日均線則可適時介入。

伊朗衝突進入第四周且沒有任何降溫跡象，市場擔憂戰事可能比原先預期延續更久，對能源供應中斷的憂慮迅速擴大，也加深通膨隱憂及升息疑慮，再加上適逢今年首個四巫日，美股上周五四大指數齊重挫，拖累台股今日下挫逾千點，失守3萬3關卡。

王詩婷／財經特派記者，主跑勞動部、半導體、生技等

news.Title: 全球資金尋求多元化佈局 美股吸金力道減弱 歐亞新興市場受青睞
news.Content:
商傳媒 ｜方承業／綜合外電報導

全球主要投資機構正重新評估市場佈局。Natixis Investment Managers 上週在法國巴黎舉辦思想領袖高峰會，多位專家指出，在全球地緣政治緊張加劇及原油價格飆升的背景下，國際資金正持續從美國股市撤離，轉向歐洲及部分新興市場，以尋求更為多元的投資機會。

Harris Oakmark 客戶投資組合經理 Daniel Nicholas 表示，其投資組合對美國股市採「顯著低配」策略，而更偏好歐洲市場。他提到，去（2025）年美國市場獨強，經濟學家普遍看好並「超配」美股。然而，情勢已因兩大事件而改變：一為在2025年4月2日美國總統川普宣布對進口商品實施全面關稅，該日被稱為「解放日」；二為「伊朗戰爭」的爆發。今（2026）年，儘管美國最高法院裁定川普政府動用《國際緊急經濟權力法》（IEEPA）實施廣泛關稅超越其法定權限，川普總統仍依據1974年法案第122條，重新對進口商品課徵10%關稅，並計劃將稅率提高至15%。

Nicholas 進一步指出，去（2025）年華爾街預期歐洲股市未來12個月的獲利成長約7%，而今（2026）年預期已提升至11%。他認為，目前許多優質的歐洲企業估值相對偏低，投資人可找到具吸引力的標的。Pictet 財富管理也傾向於2026年降低投資人對美股的配置，並增加對歐洲及新興市場股市的曝險。

Mirova 主題股票投資長 Karen Kharmandarian 提到，去（2025）年儘管是美國獨強的一年，但今年「科技巨頭七雄」（Magnificent 7，包括亞馬遜、特斯拉、Meta、微軟、Alphabet、輝達和蘋果）的表現已不如預期，反觀南韓、日本和歐洲市場則有顯著表現。Natixis Investment Managers 全球市場策略主管 Mabrouk Chetouane 更點出，南韓、台灣及拉丁美洲市場蘊藏投資機會。Lombard Odier 首席經濟學家 Samy Chaar 則表示，儘管地區衝突不斷，他仍將持續投資佔新興市場七成的亞洲。

儘管資金流出美股的趨勢明顯，市場仍存在不同策略考量。Mabrouk Chetouane 近期因中東衝突及原油價格飆升，反而策略性地增加了對美國的曝險。匯豐私人銀行全球首席投資長 Willem Sels 亦指出，美國市場在伊朗衝突期間展現韌性，歸因於其能源及主導性的科技產業，這些產業對油價的敏感度較低。然而，DNCA Finance 歐洲股票投資組合經理 Fabio Di Giansante 觀察到，投資者為尋求多元化，資金持續從美國資產流出。Di Giansante 強調，即使今（2026）年美國獲利成長預計仍有兩位數，高於歐洲的中個位數，但選股依然是關鍵，並看好人工智慧（AI）及醫療保健產業的發展機會。

news.Title: 《美股》一周回顧：道瓊工業指數周跌2.11% 最弱
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】上周美股道瓊指數周線收跌2.11%、標普500指數周跌1.9%、那斯達克指數周跌2.07%、費城半導體指數周漲0.31%。時報資訊整理一周美股走勢大綱和重點消息，助投資人回顧過去展望未來。

※一周美股回顧

●周一：輝達領軍 美股反彈 「川習會」延後

周一(3月16日)油價回落，帶動美股反彈，標普500在連跌3周並創今年新低後站穩腳跟，投資人將持續緊盯中東局勢發展。Meta漲逾2%，儘管稱裁員逾20%的報導為「臆測」。GTC大會登場，「市值王」輝達壓寶AI推理，估到2027年其晶片營收機遇突破1兆美元，股價漲1.65%，帶動費半漲近2%。下午，川普宣布，因為伊朗戰爭，美國已要求將北京「川習會」延後「約一個月」。

四大指數：美股道瓊上漲387.94點或0.83%，收46,946.41點。S&P500上漲67.19點或1.01%，收6,699.38點。NASDAQ上漲268.82點或1.22%，收22,374.18點。費城半導體上漲149.61點或1.96%，收7,796.24點。

●周二：川普放話 漲幅收斂 傳輝達將推出陸版Groq

周二(3月17日)美股四大指數小漲，達美航空等航空觀光股帶來提振。川普暗示護航聯盟難成軍，油價反彈，使大盤漲幅收斂。在高油價和中東衝突陰霾籠罩下，聯準會展開為期兩天的政策會議，將成為市場關注焦點。輝達在GTC大會後跌0.70%，台積電ADR漲1.98%。黃仁勳稱輝達已重啟銷往中國的H200晶片生產。路透社引述消息人士報導，輝達準備推出可銷往中國的Groq新AI晶片。

四大指數：美股道瓊上漲46.85點或0.10%，收46,993.26點。S&P500上漲16.71點或0.25%，收6,716.09點。NASDAQ上漲105.35點或0.47%，收22,479.53點。費城半導體上漲40.58點或0.52%，收7,836.83點。

●周三：憂通膨 美股急跌 美光營收3倍跳 盤後1原因重挫

聯準會一如預期維持利率不變，但2月PPI、聯準會主席鮑爾談話和油價再度急升引發通膨疑慮，周三(3月18日)道指暴跌逾750點，並創今年新低。「科技七雄」全跌，標普500和那指跌幅亦超過1%。台積電ADR跌1.85%。伊朗在大型天然氣田遇襲後，攻擊卡達能源樞紐和沙烏地阿拉伯，布蘭特期油大漲近4%，收在每桶107.38美元。目前美國期油漲近3%，直逼100美元大關。美光盤後暫跌近5%，其上季營收數字和本季營收預測都是3倍跳，均較前一年同期成長約200%，但宣布2026年資本支出擴大50億美元，令投資人不安。

四大指數：美股道瓊下跌768.11點或1.63%，收46,225.15點。S&P500下跌91.39點或1.36%，收6,624.70點。NASDAQ下跌327.11點或1.46%，收22,152.42點。費城半導體下跌41.70點或0.53%，收7,795.13點。

●周四：油價緩漲 美股走低

國際原油價格雖仍在高檔但上漲速度減慢，美股三大指數周四(19日)全數下挫，且連兩天收黑。美國WTI期貨價格每桶收在96.14美元，小漲0.2%。同時，倫敦布蘭特原油收108.65美元，也漲1.2%，並創2022年七月以來最高收盤價。費半逆勢上揚，但NVIDIA與台積電ADR卻下跌，財報亮眼的美光，因受到獲利了結的影響，股價大跌近4%。不過，英特爾以及AMD都出現大漲。中東情勢方面，以色列指出，目前正在協助美國，準備重新開放荷姆茲海峽，總理納坦雅胡也表示，這場戰爭可能比外界想像的還要更早就可結束。

四大指數：美股道瓊下跌203.72點或0.44%，收46,021.43點。S&P500下跌18.21點或0.27%，收6,606.49點。NASDAQ下跌61.73點或0.28%，收22,090.69點。費城半導體上漲68.18點或0.88%，收7,863.30點。

●周五：美國派兵中東、通膨風險驟增 美股全面潰敗

戰爭進入第四周，衝突有擴大跡象，拖累美股周五(3月20日)收挫。美國最新向中東增派軍隊，部署了一艘兩棲攻擊艦，其中包括數千名新增的海軍陸戰隊員和水兵，加劇了人們對戰事擴大、通膨和可能升息的擔憂。華爾街市值最高的幾家公司都下跌，輝達和特斯拉都跌超過3%，Alphabet、Meta和微軟都跌約2%，台積電ADR也跌2.8%。美超微非法向中國輸出AI技術，股價暴跌33%。周五道瓊跌0.96%；標普跌1.51%，來到9月以來最低點；那指跌2.01%，較10月29日創下的歷史最高收盤點，下跌近10%；費半跌2.45%。本周道瓊跌2.11%，標普跌1.9%，那指跌2.07%，費半則升0.31%。

四大指數：美股道瓊下跌443.96點或0.96%，收45,577.47點。S&P500下跌100.01點或1.51%，收6,506.48點。NASDAQ下跌443.08點或2.01%，收21,647.61點。費城半導體下跌192.70點或2.45%，收7,670.61點。（編輯：柯婉琇）

news.Title: 《美股》戰事延燒+三巫日 那指破200日線瀕修正
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-23 06:16:16 郭妍希 發佈

隨著美國、以色列對伊朗的戰爭進入第四週，加深通膨隱憂及升息疑慮，美國四大指數20日全面下挫，適逢三巫日(triple witching day，即股票選擇權、股票指數期貨及股票指數選擇權同步到期的日子)更讓震盪加劇。

道瓊工業平均指數3月20日終場下跌0.96%(443.96點)、收45,577.47點，創2025年10月10日以來收盤新低；週K線下跌2.11%，已連續第4週走跌。那斯達克指數下跌2.01%(443.08點)、收21,647.61點，創2025年9月3日以來收盤新低；週K線下跌2.07%，已連續第4週走跌。標準普爾500指數下跌1.51%(100.01點)、收6,506.48點，創2025年9月8日以來收盤新低；週K線下跌1.9%，已連續第4週走跌。費城半導體指數下跌2.45%(192.7點)、收7,670.61點；週K線上漲0.31%。

自伊朗戰爭2月28日開打以來，標普500、那指、道瓊與費半已分別下挫5.41%、4.5%、6.94%、5.28%。值得注意的是，道瓊、標普500、那指均跌破200日移動平均線。

道瓊目前已較2月10日的歷史收盤高(50,188.14點)拉回9.2%，標普500較1月27日的歷史收盤高(6,978.6點)拉回6.8%，那指較2025年10月29日的歷史收盤高(23,958.47點)拉回9.6%，費城半導體指數則較2月24日的歷史收盤高(8,332.34點)拉回7.9%。

小型股指標羅素兩千指數(Russell 2000)則自1月22日的歷史收盤高拉回10%。根據華爾街定義，若指數從前波高回跌10%，便是落入修正領域。

伊朗、以色列前夜互襲，伊朗也對波斯灣地區的能源設施發動新一輪攻擊。華爾街日報引述美國官員報導，五角大廈正向中東增派數千名海軍陸戰隊。同時，CBS News引述多方消息稱，美軍正為向伊朗派遣地面部隊進行「密集準備」。

路透社報導，伊拉克宣布境內所有外國公司經營的油田進入「不可抗力」(force majeure)狀態，消息傳來帶動美股賣壓在午後加劇。油價應聲跳高。布蘭特原油期貨突破每桶113美元、來到盤中最高點，西德州中級(WTI)原油期貨也漲破每桶98美元。

Baird投資策略師Ross Mayfield對CNBC表示，若地面部隊捲入令緊張局勢升溫，那麼油氣價格飛漲的市況恐怕還得延續至少數週；屆時市場將屏息關注該地區能源基礎設施的每一則消息。「坦白說，目前股市跌幅尚未完全反映此類事件的衝擊，後市恐怕還有續跌空間。」

與此同時，通膨疑慮再起，降息已暫非聯準會(Fed)選項，帶動公債殖利率20日續揚，這讓美股走勢進一步受到壓抑。

華爾街最具價值的企業全面走跌。輝達(Nvidia)、特斯拉(Tesla)分別下挫3.28%、3.24%，Google母公司Alphabet、Facebook母公司Meta Platforms以及微軟(Microsoft)也分別下跌2%、2.15%、1.84%。

芝加哥商業交易所(CME) FedWatch工具顯示，聯邦基金期貨投資人20日預測，Fed 2026年底前升息的機率比降息高。

個股變化方面，AI伺服器及儲存技術大廠美超微電腦(Super Micro Computer)爆發重大醜聞。美國司法部(DOJ)控告美超微共同創辦人廖益賢(Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw)、台灣辦公室銷售總經理張瑞滄(Ruei-Tsan “Steven” Chang)，以及第三方中間商孫廷偉(Ting-Wei “Willy” Sun)，涉嫌非法將價值數十億美元、內建輝達(Nvidia Corp.)高階繪圖處理器(GPU)的伺服器轉運至中國。受此利空衝擊，美超微跳空崩跌33.32%、收20.53美元，創2024年11月15日以來收盤新低。

(圖片來源：shutterstock)

＊編者按：本文僅供參考之用，並不構成要約、招攬或邀請、誘使、任何不論種類或形式之申述或訂立任何建議及推薦，讀者務請運用 個人獨立思考能力，自行作出投資決定，如因相關建議招致損失，概與《精實財經媒體》、編者及作者無涉。

《美股》油價揚 標普探今年低、費半自前高跌8%

news.Title: Street Calls of the Week
news.Content:
Investing.com -- Here is your Pro Recap of the top takeaways from Wall Street analysts for the past week.

Qualcomm

What happened? On Monday, Seaport Global downgraded Qualcomm Incorporated (NASDAQ:QCOM) to Sell with a $100 price target.

* TLDR : Internal silicon threatens Qualcomm’s core dominance.

What’s the full story? Qualcomm finds itself in a tightening vice, staring down a year of brutal contraction. Seaport expects mobile volumes to plummet 10%–15% as surging memory costs force a grim choice: jack up handset prices or lobotomize the specs. Either way, the upgrade cycle lengthens, and the consumer walks away. While Android rivals retreat to the low-rent safety of the "value tier"—inviting a margin-killing price war with MediaTek—Apple smells blood. Cupertino’s superior scale secures cheaper chips, allowing it to hold the line on pricing and seize market share while systematically purging Qualcomm from its bill of materials.

The firm sees a deeper existential rot beneath these cyclical headwinds. Qualcomm’s core kingdom is under siege by "internal silicon"—a polite euphemism for customers realizing they can build their own engines. The five largest vendors now design their own application processors; four are actively developing modems. Qualcomm’s diversification efforts look less like a strategic pivot and more like a distracted king losing the palace.

In the market’s cold accounting, a firm that loses its monopoly on its only trick is just a patent collector with an expiring lease.

Target Hospitality

What happened? On Tuesday, Oppenheimer upgraded Target Hospitality Corp (NASDAQ:TH) to Outperform with an $11 price target.

* TLDR : Data-center sprawl drives massive EBITDA growth.

What’s the full story? Oppenheimer is muy toroish on Target Hospitality, a firm once tethered to the fickle, lumpy tides of immigration. The old story—a classic boom-bust cycle for the cynical—is dead. The team watched from the sidelines, waiting to see if the pivot to housing data center drones and rare-earth miners in God-forsaken outposts was a real business or just a hopeful slide deck. It turns out, the frontier pays.

The future arrives early and looks like a bunk bed in a data center sprawl. Recent contract wins are "upsizing" at a pace that suggests the desert is getting crowded. This new segment already claims 55% of 4Q26E gross profit, with a pipeline groaning under 20,000 incremental beds. The team catapults 2027E adjusted EBITDA growth to a staggering +92%. It’s a bold bet on the industrial wasteland; apparently, there is plenty of money to be made in the middle of nowhere.

Story Continues

The Trade Desk

What happened? On Wednesday, Rosenblatt downgraded The Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD) to Neutral with a $25 price target.

* TLDR : Structural decay caps Trade Desk’s multiple.

What’s the full story? Rosenblatt steps aside with a Neutral rating and a $25 target as the advertising peace-treaty dissolves. The firm notes a "surprisingly sharp" tone in reports of a Publicis dispute, trailing similar skirmishes with WPP and Dentsu. It turns out, when the agency sector faces structural decay and decelerating revenue, the polite middleman act ends. The giants are stoping the foot-dragging and entering The Trade Desk’s arena, teeth bared, to protect what remains of their lunch.

The investment bank launched coverage expecting a post-"Liberation Day" calm, but the frontier is getting rowdy. This isn’t just a spat; it’s a civil war for margin. As agencies consolidate and confront TTD, the "structural change" creates a ceiling on TTD’s multiple that no amount of optimism can clear. Until these tensions are neutralized, the firm watches from the porch, waiting to see if the king of programmatic can survive a mutiny from its own best customers

Five Below

What happened? On Thursday, William Blair upgraded Five Below Inc (NASDAQ:FIVE) to Outperform.

* TLDR : Conservative guidance invites massive earnings beats.

What’s the full story? William Blair upgrades Five Below to Outperform, betting the discount king can still "comp the comp" despite a triple-digit stock run. The investment bank shakes off the cautious ghosts of difficult year-over-year comparisons. Management’s 2026 guidance looks like a low hurdle in a high-jump world; while the official line targets a modest 3%–5% full-year comp, the math suggests 7%–10% is the real prize. In a "tricky" consumer environment, the bargain bin is a fortress.

The team sees a "beat-and-raise" cycle fueled by conservative accounting. Even with weather hiccups, the first quarter screams upside. If multiyear trends hold, EPS could hit $9.10 —a 15% leap over the official outlook. Tariffs remain the wild card, but the firm notes management is already braced for a 20% hit; a lighter touch from the administration provides an accidental tailwind. As long as the value proposition holds, Five Below turns macroeconomic dread into a margin-expanding miracle

SolarEdge

What happened? On Friday, Jefferies upgraded SolarEdge Technologies Inc (NASDAQ:SEDG) to Hold with a $49 price target.

* TLDR : Surging gas prices fuel European demand.

What’s the full story? Jefferies upgrades SolarEdge to HOLD as the Middle East descends into the kind of profitable chaos that previously minted money for the inverter trade. It is a familiar, grisly script: geopolitical strife in the Levant mirrors the Russia-Ukraine energy panic that catapulted SEDG’s European revenue from $630M to a $1.9B peak. History doesn’t repeat, but it certainly rhymes with a higher utility bill.

Since the cannons began firing, TTF gas prices have surged 94%. While electricity prices currently sit in a deceptive calm, The analyst expects a panicked demand surge across Europe as consumers scramble for solar lifeboats. Jefferies slaps a $49.00 price target on the volatility, betting that as long as the world remains on fire, the "green" transition remains a convenient hedge for the terrified.

news.Title: Qualcomm Stockholders Back Board, Equity Plan; CEO Amon Highlights AI, Robotics and Data Center Push
news.Content:
Key Points

Stockholders approved all board-recommended items including the election of 11 directors, ratification of PwC as auditor, approval of the amended 2023 long-term incentive plan with a 24 million share increase in reserves, while both stockholder proposals (special meetings and China reporting) were not approved.

CEO Cristiano Amon said Qualcomm is diversifying beyond handsets into personal AI, wearables, PCs, automotive, edge networking and industrial IoT, and has formally entered advanced robotics with Dragonwing IQ 10 series support for AMRs and humanoids.

Qualcomm is accelerating a push into the data center with NPU-based AI inference accelerators and custom SoCs (after completing the Alphawave Semi acquisition), expects data center revenue to become financially material in fiscal 2027, and reiterated strong capital returns including returning ~100% of free cash flow and a $0.03 dividend increase.

Interested in Qualcomm Incorporated? Here are five stocks we like better.

Qualcomm Just Got a Street-Low Price Target—What’s Spooking Analysts?

Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) held its 2026 annual meeting of stockholders on Thursday, with Chair Mark McLaughlin opening the session by introducing board nominees and members of the executive team, including President and CEO Cristiano Amon and CFO/COO Akash Palkhiwala. McLaughlin also recognized two departing directors: Neil Smit, who is retiring after nearly eight years on the board, and Chris Young, who is leaving to focus on his role as CEO of Vertex Incorporated.

Governance items and voting outcomes

General Counsel and Corporate Secretary Ann Chaplin reviewed procedural matters, including confirmation that a quorum was present and that Peter Descovich had been appointed inspector of election. Stockholders considered seven proposals, and management reported preliminary results showing all board-recommended items were approved while the two stockholder proposals were not.

Director elections: Stockholders elected all 11 director nominees: Sylvia Acevedo, Cristiano Amon, Mark Fields, Jeff Henderson, Zico Kolter, Ann Livermore, Mark McLaughlin, Jamie Miller, Marie Myers, Irene Rosenfeld, and Jean-Pascal Tricoire.

Auditor ratification: Stockholders ratified PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as independent public accountants for the fiscal year ending September 27, 2026.

Executive compensation: Stockholders approved the non-binding advisory “say-on-pay” proposal.

Say-on-pay frequency: Stockholders supported holding future say-on-pay votes on an annual basis.

Equity plan: Stockholders approved the amended and restated 2023 long-term incentive plan, including an increase in share reserves by 24 million shares.

Stockholder proposals: Both stockholder proposals were not approved.

Story Continues

→ Planet Labs: The Satellite Stock That Keeps Shooting to the Moon

Qualcomm's Robotics Push Could Be Bigger Than the Market Thinks

Chaplin said final voting results would be reported within four business days in a Form 8-K filing with the SEC and published on the company’s website.

Stockholder proposals focused on special meetings and China risk

In Proposal Six, stockholder proponent John Chevedden asked the company to amend governing documents to allow holders of a combined 10% of outstanding common stock to call a special stockholder meeting. Chevedden argued Qualcomm’s existing provisions—citing a threshold “up to 25%” and a requirement that shares be held for a “full continuous year” to participate—functioned as “poison pills” that effectively prevent stockholders from calling special meetings.

→ Active ETFs Surge Past Passive, and These Are in the Lead

Qualcomm’s Sudden Reversal Signal Could Catch the Bears Offside

Proposal Seven was presented via a prerecorded statement by Todd Russ, Chairman of the Board of Investors for the Oklahoma Tobacco Settlement Endowment Trust. Russ requested reporting on how the company manages “China risk,” describing China as a “massive portion of revenue” and referencing concerns raised in meetings with the company about a lack of tracking for China-specific operating costs, assets, or supply chain specifics. Russ characterized the proposal as “modest, reasonable, and non-political,” and said it sought transparency rather than a decoupling from China.

CEO outlines strategy: diversification, AI, and expansion to new markets

In a business update, Amon said Qualcomm’s strategy is built on anticipating change, investing early in new technologies, and executing with discipline. He described the company as a “connected computing leader” with capabilities spanning high-performance, low-power computing, advanced connectivity, and AI processing. Amon said Qualcomm’s technologies scale from “less than 2 milliwatts” in consumer electronics to “2,000 watts” in next-generation data centers.

→ Trash to Treasure: 3 Waste Removal Stocks to Minimize Volatility

Amon said the company is “successfully expanding beyond handsets” into personal AI and wearables, PCs, automotive, edge networking, and industrial IoT, and is “extending this approach into advanced robotics and the data center.”

Product and market highlights: Snapdragon, automotive, networking, robotics, and data center

In handsets, Amon said Snapdragon platforms “continue to set the pace of mobile innovation,” highlighting the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 as featuring “the fastest mobile CPU ever,” along with enhanced NPU and GPU capabilities. He said the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 and Snapdragon 8 Gen 5 support premium smartphones and are seeing “strong global adoption across OEM flagship designs.”

He also emphasized “personal AI” devices such as smart glasses and other wearables, describing “agentic experiences” and continuous sensing requirements as aligned with Qualcomm’s strengths in performance and power efficiency. Amon said the company is working with “seven of the nine largest cloud companies globally,” and that Snapdragon is the platform of choice with “more than 40 devices in production or development,” with smart glasses likely to become the largest category.

In PCs, Amon pointed to Snapdragon X Series momentum and said Qualcomm expects “approximately 150 Snapdragon-powered laptop designs to be commercialized through 2026.” In automotive, he said Snapdragon Digital Chassis solutions have broad adoption across major OEMs and that the company’s digital cockpit platform is deployed in “75 million cars globally,” including top-selling vehicles from General Motors, Toyota, and Mercedes. He also cited the launch of Snapdragon Ride Pilot, described as Qualcomm’s first full L2+ automated driving solution in its advanced self-driving software stack co-developed with BMW.

In edge networking, Amon cited demand for Wi-Fi 7 gateway platforms and adoption of 5G fixed wireless access solutions, and said Qualcomm is investing in Wi-Fi 8, with consumer devices expected “as early as summer 2026.” He highlighted Xiaomi’s launch of an AI gateway based on the Qualcomm Dragonwing and Pro A7 platform. In industrial and embedded IoT, he said Qualcomm expanded its offerings and cited a range of customer examples using industrial-grade Dragonwing platforms.

Amon also said the company formally announced entry into advanced robotics, describing Dragonwing IQ 10 series support spanning “advanced AMRs to humanoids.” He said robotics underscores the importance of on-device AI processing, low latency sensor fusion, and power efficiency.

In the data center, Amon said Qualcomm is focusing on NPU-based AI inference accelerator cards and servers and custom SoCs, emphasizing inference workloads and an “innovative memory architecture” that provides “more than ten times higher effective memory bandwidth” with lower power consumption. He also said the company completed the acquisition of Alphawave Semi and is “encouraged by early customer traction” with hyperscalers, cloud service providers, and sovereign AI projects.

Q&A: diversification targets, capital returns, and 6G

During the Q&A, Amon reiterated Qualcomm’s target of $22 billion of combined QCT, automotive, and IoT revenues by fiscal 2029. On data center, he said it is “not included” in that $22 billion and that Qualcomm expects the business to start becoming “financially material in fiscal 2027,” calling it a “multi-billion opportunity.”

On capital returns, management said Qualcomm returned approximately “100% of our free cash flow” in fiscal 2025 and is “on track to do that in fiscal 2026,” using a combination of dividends and buybacks. The company also said it announced a $0.03 dividend increase, and reiterated guidance that annual dividend increases are expected to be in the “low single-digit range.”

Addressing 6G, Amon said “6G is definitely coming” and that Qualcomm expects to lead it as it has prior wireless generations. He described 6G as enabling significant uplink speed increases for future AI-driven devices and said the network itself will become “an AI network,” including sensing capabilities and “the abilities to create a digital twin of everything,” which he framed as an opportunity spanning cellular technologies as well as AI compute and data center.

In closing remarks, Amon said Qualcomm is evolving from a company focused on connectivity and mobile into “a connected computing company for the age of intelligence,” and said the company will continue executing its strategy.

About Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM)

Qualcomm Incorporated is a global semiconductor and telecommunications equipment company headquartered in San Diego, California. Founded in 1985, the company is known for its development of wireless technologies and for playing a central role in the evolution of digital cellular standards, including CDMA and subsequent generations of mobile standards. Qualcomm’s business combines the design and sale of semiconductor products with a patent licensing program for wireless technologies and related intellectual property.

The company’s product portfolio includes system-on-chip (SoC) platforms marketed under the Snapdragon brand, cellular modem and RF front-end components, connectivity solutions for Wi‑Fi and Bluetooth, and processors and platforms aimed at automotive, IoT, networking and edge-computing applications.

news.Title: 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks That Are Quietly Outperforming Micron Technology in 2026 With 76% and 82% Gains
news.Content:
Micron Technology has been one of the top performers on the stock market in 2026, with its shares rising an impressive 62% this year, as of this writing. The memory specialist's share price rally is driven by a terrific increase in revenue and earnings, and the good news is that it can sustain its growth.

Even with its strong showing, Lumentum (NASDAQ: LITE) and Western Digital (NASDAQ: WDC) have outperformed Micron this year. While Lumentum is up 90% in 2026, Western Digital has posted an 77% gain.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Importantly, both these tech stocks are likely to soar higher as each should benefit from lucrative artificial intelligence (AI) -fueled growth trends. Let's take a closer look at the catalysts that could send them soaring.

Lumentum caters to a fast-growing AI niche

AI data centers need fast connectivity to move massive datasets quickly. This ensures that AI workloads -- such as training large language models or inference applications -- aren't hindered by slow data transmission.

Lumentum's optical and photonic products help solve this potential bottleneck. The company sells switches, optical products, and lasers focused on enhancing the bandwidth of networks and enabling high-speed data transfers. Not surprisingly, its products are in great demand from hyperscalers.

As noted by Lumentum CEO Michael Hurlston on the company's February earnings call :

We are now recognized as a foundational engine of the AI revolution. Virtually every AI network is powered by Lumentum technology, either through our direct hyperscaler partnerships or as the critical component supplier that enables our network equipment manufacturer customers.

The robust demand for Lumentum's networking products is evident in its remarkable revenue and earnings growth. The company released its fiscal 2026 second-quarter results (for the three months ended Dec. 27, 2025) on Feb. 3. Its revenue in the first six months of the fiscal year jumped by an impressive 62% to $1.2 billion.

Even better, Lumentum's margin profile is improving due to higher factory utilization rates, a more favorable product mix, and higher pricing. These factors explain why Lumentum's non-GAAP earnings jumped by a whopping 367% year over year in the first half of the fiscal year to $2.80 per share.

This red-hot growth isn't going to slow down anytime soon. Lumentum anticipates a stronger year-over-year increase of 85% in revenue in the current quarter, which is well above the growth it has clocked in the first half. The midpoint of its earnings guidance stands at $2.25 per share, a jump of almost 4x over the year-ago period's reading of $0.57 per share.

Story Continues

As the build-out of AI data centers isn't showing any signs of slowing, it is easy to see why analysts anticipate Lumentum's healthy bottom-line growth momentum will continue.

So, don't be surprised to see Lumentum stock rise further as it is poised to deliver remarkable earnings growth in the short and long run.

Western Digital's storage products are sold out due to AI

AI data centers have driven a storage boom, which isn't surprising, since the huge datasets needed for AI training and inference must be stored somewhere. This is the reason why the demand for hard-disk drives (HDDs) has simply taken off.

Tom's Hardware reported in November 2025 that large-capacity HDDs used in data centers have a lead time of two years. Western Digital is a beneficiary of this red-hot demand, which is fueling a major increase in HDD prices. The prices of popular HDDs increased at an average monthly pace of 46% from September 2025 to December 2025.

Not surprisingly, Western Digital's revenue in the first six months of fiscal 2026 (which ended on Jan. 2, 2026) increased by 26% year over year to $5.83 billion. The favorable pricing environment explains why Western Digital's earnings have doubled over the same period to $3.92 per share. What's worth noting is that the company has sold out all of its available HDD capacity for 2026.

CEO Irving Tan pointed out in the company's January earnings call:

As we highlighted, we're pretty much sold out for calendar '26. We have firm POs with our top seven customers. And we've also established LTAs with two of them for calendar year '27 and one of them for calendar year '28.

Western Digital is therefore now entering into long-term agreements with customers for HDD capacity over the next three years. This should ensure continued growth in the company's revenue and earnings. Another important point to note is that the AI-centric storage market's revenue is anticipated to increase at a 25% compound annual growth rate through 2032, according to a third-party estimate.

So, the strong earnings growth that analysts expect from Western Digital should continue beyond the next couple of fiscal years.

This robust earnings growth should ideally be a tailwind for this tech stock, which is why investors can consider buying Western Digital before it skyrockets further.

news.Title: 《美股》收盤速報：美國派兵中東、通膨風險驟增 美股全面潰敗
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】戰爭進入第四周，衝突有擴大跡象，拖累美股周五(3月20日)收挫。美國最新向中東增派軍隊，部署了一艘兩棲攻擊艦，其中包括數千名新增的海軍陸戰隊員和水兵，加劇了人們對戰事擴大、通膨和可能升息的擔憂。華爾街市值最高的幾家公司都下跌，輝達和特斯拉都跌超過3%，Alphabet、Meta和微軟都跌約2%，台積電ADR也跌2.8%。美超微非法向中國輸出AI技術，股價暴跌33%。周五道瓊跌0.96%；標普跌1.51%，來到9月以來最低點；那指跌2.01%，較10月29日創下的歷史最高收盤點，下跌近10%；費半跌2.45%。本周道瓊跌2.11%，標普跌1.85%，那指跌2.07%，費半則升3.01%。

Longbow資產管理執行長Jake Dollarhide說，市場終於開始接受這樣的觀點，即局面延續的時間可能超過最初預期，這就是目前市場拋售的原因，衝突可能不僅會持續幾週，而是會持續幾個月以上。

中東衝突導致油價居高不下，加劇了市場對通膨的擔憂。美國國債價格連續第三個交易日下跌，英國和歐洲的公債也出現更廣泛的拋售。

FedWatch工具顯示，到2026年底，聯準會可能升息，而不是降息。

荷蘭ING集團的全球利率主管Padhraic Garvey說，我們面對的是典型的利率推高環境，這主要是由更高的通膨預期所驅動，而該預期又與油價密切相關。戰爭已經進入第四周，情況顯示這種壓力不會很快消失。

美超微被美國政府指控，3名相關人員非法向中國走私出口價值至少25億美元的AI技術，股價暴跌33%。

被視為商業活動領先指標的聯邦快遞(FedEx)公布了樂觀財測，稱管地緣政治局勢緊張，但全球需求仍保持穩定，隨後股價上漲近1%。

news.Title: Lumentum Touts AI Optics Boom, $2B Quarterly Target, New Fab and 1.6T Transceiver Shipments
news.Content:
Key Points

Lumentum says AI-driven optics demand is outpacing supply—it's “under shipping” the market by roughly 25–30% —and is ramping EML and indium phosphide capacity, including acquiring a Qorvo fab in Greensboro (its fifth indium phosphide fab) to boost UHP production with shipments from that site expected in 2028.

The CFO outlined financial targets of a short-term exit run-rate of $1.25 billion per quarter at about 35% operating margin and a mid-term goal of a $2 billion quarter with 40% non‑GAAP operating margin on an ~18–24 month timeline.

Product and commercial milestones include expected 1.6T cloud transceiver shipments and vertical integration starting this summer, a new multi‑year multi‑billion‑dollar 300×300 optical circuit switch deal, and ongoing development of 400G‑per‑lane and UHP laser solutions for AI scale‑out and scale‑up networking.

Interested in Lumentum Holdings Inc.? Here are five stocks we like better.

More Than Just Brains: The AI Revolution's Nervous System

Lumentum (NASDAQ:LITE) executives used a company event led by investor relations VP Kathryn Ta to outline what they see as a multi-year demand surge for optical components tied to AI infrastructure, while also providing updates on capacity expansion, product roadmaps, and financial targets. The session featured CEO Michael Hurlston, chief technology officer Dr. Wupen Yuen, and CFO Wajid Ali, followed by analyst Q&A.

Leadership framed four “growth drivers” and a capacity shortage

Hurlston said Lumentum’s position rests on “four building blocks,” including deep networking expertise, core laser technology, optical circuit switching built from its telecom heritage, and what he described as industry-leading indium phosphide capacity. He emphasized the company’s intention to evolve its margin profile “towards semiconductor-like margins” as the business remains “component-driven, semiconductor-driven.”

→ Planet Labs: The Satellite Stock That Keeps Shooting to the Moon

These 2 AI Stocks Just Got a Massive S&P 500 Catalyst

He highlighted the company’s manufacturing ramp, stating that since fiscal 2023 Lumentum has increased EML (electro-absorption modulated laser) output by 8x. Looking forward, he said the company plans to raise indium phosphide output by another 50% from the last quarter of calendar 2025 through the last quarter of calendar 2026. Even with these increases, he said demand is outpacing supply and that Lumentum is currently “under shipping the market” by about 25% to 30%, with the gap expected to grow due to demand for ultra-high-power (UHP) lasers for optical scale-out and optical scale-up.

Story Continues

Cloud transceivers: 1.6T shipments expected to start in summer

On cloud transceivers, Hurlston said the business has “turned the corner,” citing improved engineering execution, improving profitability, and expected better margins on upcoming products. He said 1.6T shipments are expected to start “this summer,” and that vertical integration—bringing Lumentum’s own lasers into those modules—should also begin in the summer timeframe to support margin improvement.

→ Amazon’s Prime Day Shift: Why Moving It to June Matters

3 High-Growth Unknowns in Photonics That Are Vital for AI

Yuen later added that Lumentum plans to show a 400G-per-lane optical transceiver demo capable of 3.2T (eight lanes at 400G) and called the company “the largest EML laser supplier in the world” at 100G and 200G. He said Lumentum will demonstrate what he described as the “world’s first” 400G-per-lane differential EML in a pluggable module and argued EMLs are often first-to-market at new speed nodes due to yield and manufacturability advantages versus silicon photonics approaches.

Optical circuit switching: new multi-year, multi-billion-dollar agreement

Hurlston said Lumentum signed a “new multi-year, multi-billion-dollar agreement” with a large optical circuit switch (OCS) customer, following prior disclosures around roughly $400 million of OCS revenue expected in the back half of the calendar year. He said the company remains on track to ship the $400 million of backlog in the second half and described Lumentum as ahead of competitors in high-radix OCS products such as 300-by-300 systems. In Q&A, he confirmed the new commitment relates to a 300-by-300 high-radix switch and said it came from one of the three customers Lumentum is already shipping to.

→ Trash to Treasure: 3 Waste Removal Stocks to Minimize Volatility

Both Hurlston and Yuen emphasized the company’s MEMS-based design, describing it as field-hardened and transparent to wavelength and data rate because it “simply mirrors” light. Yuen said Lumentum’s OCS insertion loss for a 300-by-300 system is less than 1.5 dB and described latency in “tens of nanoseconds,” with power consumption he characterized as far below packet switches.

Scale-out and scale-up optics: UHP lasers, ELS modules, and a new fab

Executives repeatedly linked demand growth to AI networking needs. Yuen said “AI does not scale…without optics,” describing changes in data center traffic patterns such as increased “east-west” machine-generated traffic, more non-blocking network requirements, and the rise of “elephant flows,” which he said supports circuit switching use cases.

Hurlston said Lumentum is already shipping UHP lasers for optical scale-out applications and called the market “huge,” adding that the company expects a first $100 million revenue quarter for scale-out by the end of calendar 2026 and to deliver a “multi-hundred million dollar” commitment in early 2027. He also described an “external light source” (ELS) approach as a more turnkey solution for customers without deep optical engineering resources, saying it could roughly double revenue per system versus lasers alone, albeit with somewhat lower margins while still above company targets.

On scale-up, Hurlston said a “hybrid environment” is expected starting in the second half of 2027, with optics used for longer internal cluster runs (he referenced three meters and up) alongside copper for shorter reaches. He argued scale-up optics would be incremental to the industry because optics “have never shipped inside a cluster” at scale previously. He also said the first instantiation of scale-up could be 3x to 4x larger than initial scale-out CPO in lane count, and that going further “inside the rack” could be 10x larger.

To support anticipated demand, Hurlston announced Lumentum’s “fifth indium phosphide fab,” saying the company closed an agreement the same morning to acquire an existing Qorvo facility in Greensboro, North Carolina. He described it as a staffed and operational brownfield site, with Lumentum planning to move out existing Qorvo products, install its own reactors, and begin shipping from the facility in 2028. In Q&A, Hurlston said Greensboro is expected to focus primarily on UHP output and described it as “four-inch, six-inch compatible,” with a decision still to be made on whether to start on six-inch.

Financial model: $1.25B quarterly run rate target, then $2B, with higher margins

CFO Wajid Ali said the company’s recent investment cycle in fiscal 2023 through fiscal 2025 was driven by the view that indium phosphide capacity would be constrained, and he pointed to growing backlog and long-term orders as providing increased visibility. He also discussed investments in contract manufacturers to enable scale, saying management chose to prioritize operational efficiency and scalability over maximizing margin in every step.

Ali said Lumentum guided to “north of 30%” operating margins for fiscal Q3 ending in March and reiterated a longer-term framework that includes 20% operating margins as a prior target. He then presented updated targets, including:

Short-term: exit at $1.25 billion per quarter with operating margins “around 35% .”

Mid-term: exit at a $2 billion quarter with 40% non-GAAP operating margin .

Ali said the company internally sees a “nine months and nine months” path—roughly nine months to reach the $1.25 billion run rate and another nine months to reach $2 billion—but added a three-month buffer for each step due to supply chain volatility, framing the timeline as 18 to 24 months for the $2 billion and 40% operating margin target.

He also addressed the $2 billion NVIDIA investment, noting 2.9 million additional shares outstanding and saying roughly half the proceeds are expected to go toward strategic capex, with the remainder supporting working capital and potential vertical integration via M&A. Executives said the Greensboro ramp and related capacity were not included in the $2 billion quarterly model presented, as the facility is expected to contribute in calendar 2028.

About Lumentum (NASDAQ:LITE)

Lumentum Holdings Inc, headquartered in San Jose, California, is a leading provider of photonic technologies that enable high-speed optical communication networks and advanced industrial applications. The company designs and manufactures a broad range of lasers, optical modules and subsystems tailored to the evolving requirements of telecommunications carriers, cloud data centers and enterprise networking.

Its core product portfolio includes tunable and fixed-wavelength laser transmitters, coherent optical engines, transceivers for long-haul, metro and data center interconnects, as well as test and measurement instruments.

He emphasized the company’s intention to evolve its margin profile “towards semiconductor-like margins” as the business remains “co

news.Title: 聯電ADR20日下跌0.14美元跌幅1.51%折台股58.31元
SailPoint Q4 Earnings Match Estimates, Revenues Up Y/Y, Shares Fall
news.Content:
SailPoint SAIL reported fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings of 8 cents per share, in line with the Zacks Consensus Estimate. The company reported a loss of $4.29 in the year-ago quarter.

Revenues were $294.6 million, up 22.7% year over year and beat the consensus mark by 0.72%. SailPoint’s shares dropped 1.04% to close at $12.34 on March 19. The company's shares have plunged 37.9% in the past year against the Zacks Computer & Technology sector’s rise of 32.1%.

SAIL’s Q4’26 Top-Line Details

As of Jan. 31, 2026, annualized recurring revenues (ARR) grew 28% year over year to $1.12 billion.

Segment-wise, SaaS revenues accounted for 57.6% of total revenues in the fiscal fourth quarter, increasing approximately 37% year over year to $169.6 million. Maintenance and support revenues represented 12.6% of total revenues, which decreased 4.2% year over year to $37.1 million.

SailPoint, Inc. Price, Consensus and EPS Surprise

SailPoint, Inc. price-consensus-eps-surprise-chart | SailPoint, Inc. Quote

Term subscription revenues contributed 22.4% of total revenues, which rose 19.4% to $66 million. Other subscription services comprised 2.7% of total revenues, which increased 27.3% year over year to $8.1 million. Total subscription revenues, comprising the four sub-segments, accounted for 95.3% of total revenues, which increased 25.1% year over year to $280.8 million. The remaining segment, Services and other, represented 4.7% of total revenues in the reported quarter. The figure decreased 11.9% to $13.9 million.

SailPoint’s Operating Highlights

The non-GAAP gross margin contracted 40 basis points (bps) year over year to 78.5%.

Sales and marketing expense, on a non-GAAP basis and as a percentage of revenues, decreased 100 bps from the year-ago quarter’s level to 35.4%.

Research and development expense, on a non-GAAP basis and as a percentage of revenues, decreased 70 bps from the year-ago quarter’s level to 15.7%.

General and administrative expense, as a percentage of revenues, decreased from the year-ago quarter’s level of 7.1% to 6.8%.

SailPoint reported a non-GAAP operating margin of 20.6%, up from an operating income of 19% reported in the year-ago quarter.

SailPoint’s Balance Sheet Remains Strong

As of Jan. 31, 2026, cash and cash equivalents were $358 million compared with $298 million as of Oct. 31, 2025.

In the reported quarter, the company generated a cash flow from operations of $64 million compared with $54 million in the previous quarter.

SAIL generated free cash flow of $57 million compared with $49 million in the previous quarter.

Story Continues

SAIL Offers Q1 and FY27 Guidance

For first-quarter fiscal 2027, SailPoint expects revenues between $273 million and $277 million, indicating year-over-year growth of 18% to 20%.

The company expects the adjusted operating income to be in the range of $30-$31 million. Adjusted earnings are expected to be between 4 cents and 5 cents for the first quarter of fiscal 2027.

For fiscal 2027, revenues are forecasted to be between $1.260 billion and $1.270 billion, indicating year-over-year growth of 18% to 19%.

The company expects the adjusted operating income to be in the range of $231.5-$236.5 million.

Adjusted earnings are expected to be between 30 cents and 34 cents for fiscal 2027.

SailPoint’s Zacks Rank & Stocks to Consider

Currently, SAIL carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

Applied Materials AMAT, Advanced Energy Industries AEIS and Arrow Electronics ARW are some better-ranked stocks that investors can consider in the broader Zacks Computer and Technology sector.

Applied Materials, Advanced Energy Industries and Arrow Electronics each sport a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank stocks here.

Long-term earnings growth rate for Applied Materials, Advanced Energy Industries and Arrow Electronics are currently pegged at 20.2%,19.3% and 15.2%, respectively.

news.Title: Why Is Analog Devices (ADI) Down 10.1% Since Last Earnings Report?
news.Content:
It has been about a month since the last earnings report for Analog Devices (ADI). Shares have lost about 10.1% in that time frame, underperforming the S&P 500.

But investors have to be wondering, will the recent negative trend continue leading up to its next earnings release, or is Analog Devices due for a breakout? Before we dive into how investors and analysts have reacted as of late, let's take a quick look at the most recent earnings report in order to get a better handle on the important drivers.

Analog Devices Q1 Earnings Beat Estimates, Revenues Rise Y/Y

Analog Devices reported first-quarter fiscal 2026 non-GAAP earnings of $2.46 per share, which beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 6.5%. This company reported earnings of $1.63 per share in the year-ago period.

Analog Devices’ first-quarter fiscal 2026 revenues of $3.16 billion surpassed the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 1.3%. The top line increased 30% from the year-ago quarter’s revenues of $2.42 billion.

Analog Devices’ Q1 Details

Industrial: Revenues from this segment were $1.49 billion, representing 47% of total revenues and reflecting 38% year-over-year growth.

Automotive: Revenues reached $794.4 million (or 25% of total revenue), up 8% year over year.

Communications: Revenues came in at $476.8 million, accounting for 15% of total revenues and rising 63% year over year.

Consumer: The segment generated $399.8 million (or 13% of revenues), marking a 27% increase compared with the same quarter last year.

The adjusted gross margin expanded 240 basis points to 71.2%, while the adjusted operating margin was 45.5%, up 500 basis points year over year.

ADI’s Balance Sheet and Cash Flows

As of Jan. 31, 2026, cash and cash equivalents were approximately $2.91 billion, up from $2.5 billion as of Nov. 1, 2025. The company also held $1.14 billion in short-term investments during the first quarter.

Long-term debt was $7.24 billion compared with $8.15 billion at the end of the previous quarter.

Analog Devices generated $1.37 billion in operating cash flow and $1.26 billion in free cash flow during the first quarter of fiscal 2026.

In the fiscal first quarter, the company returned $1 billion to shareholders, comprising $484 million in dividends and $516 million in share repurchases.

ADI Initiates Q2 FY26 Guidance

For the second quarter of fiscal 2026, management expects revenues of $3.5 billion (+/- $100 million). The company projects a reported operating margin of approximately 36.4% (+/-150 bps) and an adjusted operating margin of about 47.5% (+/-100 bps). Reported earnings are anticipated to be $2.19 (+/-$0.15) per share, while adjusted earnings are expected to be $2.88 (+/-$0.15) per share.

Story Continues

How Have Estimates Been Moving Since Then?

In the past month, investors have witnessed a upward trend in estimates review.

The consensus estimate has shifted 18.34% due to these changes.

VGM Scores

At this time, Analog Devices has a nice Growth Score of B, a grade with the same score on the momentum front. However, the stock was allocated a score of D on the value side, putting it in the bottom 40% for this investment strategy.

Overall, the stock has an aggregate VGM Score of C. If you aren't focused on one strategy, this score is the one you should be interested in.

Outlook

Estimates have been trending upward for the stock, and the magnitude of these revisions looks promising. It comes with little surprise Analog Devices has a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy). We expect an above average return from the stock in the next few months.

news.Title: Lumentum vs. Amphenol: Which AI Connectivity Stock is the Better Buy?
news.Content:
Lumentum LITE and Amphenol APH have emerged as key suppliers to the AI data center ecosystem. Both are positioned at critical points in the infrastructure stack that enable large-scale AI workloads. Surging hyperscaler capital expenditure has been a shared tailwind, lifting demand for high-speed connectivity solutions across the board.

Lumentum operates through optical components that move data across longer distances within and between data centers. Amphenol provides the electronic and fiber interconnects that handle power delivery and shorter-range transmission. As data center architectures evolve, the focus shifts to which connectivity layer offers greater scalability and long-term economic potential. Let us delve deep to determine which one is a better investment now.

The Case for LITE

Lumentum occupies a foundational role in the AI data center supply chain, manufacturing the optical and photonic components that keep high-speed networks running efficiently. Its product suite covers Electro-absorption Modulated Laser chips (EML), cloud transceivers, optical circuit switches (OCS) and co-packaged optics (CPO), collectively enabling the data movement that large-scale AI workloads depend on. Demand for these components has moved from cyclical to structural as hyperscalers embed LITE's technologies across their core infrastructure roadmaps.

The transceiver business is emerging as a near-term catalyst. The ramp in 800G products is well underway, while the move to 1.6T is arriving faster than the industry had anticipated. Higher-margin 200-gigabit-per-lane EMLs are capturing a growing share of this transition, positioning Lumentum to expand both its top line and margins as the product mix continues to upgrade. OCS is adding to this momentum, with the order backlog crossing $400 million across a diversified hyperscaler customer base.

CPO is gaining traction, with commercial orders already in place for the first half of 2027. Optical scale-up is expected to displace copper connectivity within AI clusters and represents a significant untapped addressable market from late 2027 onward.

Fiscal second-quarter revenues of $665.5 million grew 65.5% year over year, while the fiscal third-quarter guidance of $780 million to $830 million points to a business where each product cycle is adding a fresh layer of revenue. The Zacks Consensus Estimate pegs LITE's fiscal 2026 EPS at $7.7, up by 7 cents over the past 30 days and indicating year-over-year growth of 273.79%.

Lumentum Holdings Inc. Price and Consensus

Lumentum Holdings Inc. price-consensus-chart | Lumentum Holdings Inc. Quote

Story Continues

The Case for APH

Amphenol is a diversified interconnect solutions provider supplying the electronic and fiber optic components that enable power delivery and high-speed data transmission across AI data centers, communications networks and industrial systems. Its portfolio spans copper interconnects, fiber optic cables and sensor solutions, serving a broad range of end markets that insulate the business from over-dependence on any single vertical.

The IT datacom segment has emerged as Amphenol's near-term catalyst, fueled by sustained hyperscaler investment in AI infrastructure. The acquisition of CommScope's connectivity business expands APH's fiber optic capabilities, allowing it to address the full interconnect spectrum within next-generation data centers. This breadth of offering, spanning copper, fiber and power delivery, is increasingly relevant as data center architects navigate complex technology trade-offs across every new system generation. While CommScope is expected to contribute approximately $4.1 billion in 2026 revenues, its high-teens operating margin profile is expected to weigh modestly on near-term profitability as it scales within the Amphenol fold.

However, Amphenol's broad end market exposure, while a structural strength, also tempers the pace of AI-driven growth. Segments such as mobile devices and automotive face near-term cyclical pressure, diluting the impact of the strong IT datacom momentum on overall earnings growth.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate pegs APH's 2026 EPS at $4.32, up 8 cents over the past 30 days and reflecting year-over-year growth of 29.34%.

Amphenol Corporation Price and Consensus

Amphenol Corporation price-consensus-chart | Amphenol Corporation Quote

Price Performance and Valuation of LITE and APH

Over the trailing six months, LITE shares have surged 370% against APH's modest 5.8% return. Lumentum's outperformance reflects a business where accelerating revenue momentum, expanding backlogs and deepening hyperscaler relationships are translating directly into market recognition. Amphenol's modest appreciation reflects that while its AI exposure is real, the market recognizes that it remains one growth driver within a much larger and more diversified business.

LITE vs. APH Price Performance

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

LITE trades at a price-to-sales ratio of 12.95x compared to APH's 5.02x. Lumentum's premium reflects the market's recognition of its structural hyperscaler relationships and a product portfolio where each cycle adds a compounding revenue layer. Amphenol's lower multiple is consistent with its more diversified earnings profile.

LITE vs. APH Valuation

Conclusion

Lumentum's structural hyperscaler relationships, multi-product growth roadmap and accelerating revenue momentum position it as a pure-play beneficiary of the AI optical connectivity buildout, with the bulk of its growth catalysts still ahead. Amphenol's diversified business model and broad end market presence make it a resilient holding for investors who value stability and consistent execution across market cycles.

With LITE sporting a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) against APH's Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), Lumentum emerges as the more compelling pick at this juncture. You can see the complete list of today's Zacks #1 Rank stocks here.

news.Title: QCOM Hikes Dividend on Solid Cash Flow: Should You Stay Invested?
news.Content:
Qualcomm Incorporated QCOM recently announced a 3.4% year-over-year hike in its quarterly dividend payout to 92 cents per share or $3.68 on an annualized basis. A steady dividend payout is part of the long-term strategy of Qualcomm to provide attractive risk-adjusted returns to its stockholders. In addition, healthy dividend increases at periodic intervals have been one of its strengths.

The company also approved a $20 billion share repurchase program in addition to its existing stock buyback program announced in November 2024, under which $2.1 billion worth of shares are available for repurchase.

The current hike reflects the inherent financial strength of the company and the strong cash flow generated from continued focus on high-margin businesses and healthy execution of operating plans. Qualcomm generated $4.96 billion of net cash from operating activities in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 compared with $4.59 billion a year ago. At quarter-end, the company had $7.2 billion in cash and cash equivalents.

With a dividend yield of approximately 2.7%, Qualcomm appears to be financially strong, with healthy cash generation and a strong ability to fund dividends and share buybacks.

Some Tech Firms With Recent Dividend Hikes

Analog Devices, Inc. ADI, has increased its quarterly dividend by 11% to $1.10 per share. This is the 22nd consecutive year of dividend hike by the semiconductor firm. Analog Devices has returned more than $32 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases in the past 22 years.

Broadcom Inc. AVGO increased its quarterly dividend by 10% to 65 cents per share for fiscal 2026. This represents the 15th consecutive increase in annual dividends since fiscal 2011.

QCOM’s Price Performance, Valuation and Estimates

Qualcomm shares have lost 16.2% over the past year against the industry’s growth of 59.9%.

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Going by the price/earnings ratio, the company's shares currently trade at 11.6 forward earnings, lower than 27.26 for the industry.

Earnings estimates for fiscal 2026 have decreased 6% to $11.28 per share over the past 60 days, while the same for fiscal 2027 are down 5.8% to $11.61.

Qualcomm currently carries a Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell).

You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

news.Content:
For Immediate Release

Chicago, IL – March 20, 2026 – Stocks in this week’s article are Flowserve FLS, Analog Devices ADI, Broadcom AVGO, NVIDIA NVDA and TIM S.A. TIMB.

5 Dividend Growth Stocks to Buy amid Rising Inflation Risk

All three major U.S. stock indices closed on a weak note yesterday, falling more than 1%, reflecting investor concerns about persistent inflation. With the Federal Reserve keeping rates unchanged amid rising Middle East tensions and surging oil prices, investor patience appears to be fading.

Meanwhile, the February Producer Price Index ("PPI") disappointed those hoping for a smooth economic recovery, showing that wholesale price increases are harder to control than expected.

Against this backdrop, the appeal of high-beta growth stocks may be fading, prompting investors to turn toward steady dividend-growth stocks. These companies have a proven track record of increasing payouts, reflecting the balance sheet resilience and cash flow durability needed to navigate a period in which the traditional growth narrative is being re-evaluated.

Stocks with a strong history of year-over-year dividend growth can help build a resilient portfolio with greater potential for capital appreciation compared to simple dividend-paying or high-yield stocks.

We have selected five dividend growth stocks — Flowserve , Analog Devices , Broadcom , NVIDIA and TIM S.A. — that could be solid choices for your portfolio.

Why Is Dividend Growth Better?

Stocks with a strong history of dividend growth belong to mature companies, which are less susceptible to large swings in the market and thus act as a hedge against economic or political uncertainty as well as stock market volatility. At the same time, these offer downside protection with their consistent increase in payouts.

These stocks possess strong fundamentals, making them attractive long-term dividend-growth investments. Their strengths include sustainable business models, a long track record of profitability, rising cash flows, solid liquidity, strong balance sheets and attractive valuation characteristics. A consistent history of dividend growth underscores the potential for continued growth ahead.

Although these stocks do not necessarily have the highest yields, they have outperformed the broader stock market or any other dividend-paying stock for an extended period.

Texas-based Flowserve develops and manufactures precision-engineered flow control equipment, such as pumps, valves and seals, for critical service applications that require high reliability. news.Title: Wall Street Bulls Look Optimistic About Analog Devices (ADI): Should You Buy?
news.Content:
Investors often turn to recommendations made by Wall Street analysts before making a Buy, Sell, or Hold decision about a stock. While media reports about rating changes by these brokerage-firm employed (or sell-side) analysts often affect a stock's price, do they really matter?

Before we discuss the reliability of brokerage recommendations and how to use them to your advantage, let's see what these Wall Street heavyweights think about Analog Devices (ADI).

Analog Devices currently has an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.44, on a scale of 1 to 5 (Strong Buy to Strong Sell), calculated based on the actual recommendations (Buy, Hold, Sell, etc.) made by 32 brokerage firms. An ABR of 1.44 approximates between Strong Buy and Buy.

Of the 32 recommendations that derive the current ABR, 23 are Strong Buy and four are Buy. Strong Buy and Buy respectively account for 71.9% and 12.5% of all recommendations.

Brokerage Recommendation Trends for ADI

Check price target & stock forecast for Analog Devices here>>>

The ABR suggests buying Analog Devices, but making an investment decision solely on the basis of this information might not be a good idea. According to several studies, brokerage recommendations have little to no success guiding investors to choose stocks with the most potential for price appreciation.

Do you wonder why? As a result of the vested interest of brokerage firms in a stock they cover, their analysts tend to rate it with a strong positive bias. According to our research, brokerage firms assign five "Strong Buy" recommendations for every "Strong Sell" recommendation.

This means that the interests of these institutions are not always aligned with those of retail investors, giving little insight into the direction of a stock's future price movement. It would therefore be best to use this information to validate your own analysis or a tool that has proven to be highly effective at predicting stock price movements.

With an impressive externally audited track record, our proprietary stock rating tool, the Zacks Rank, which classifies stocks into five groups, ranging from Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) to Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell), is a reliable indicator of a stock's near-term price performance. So, validating the Zacks Rank with ABR could go a long way in making a profitable investment decision.

ABR Should Not Be Confused With Zacks Rank

In spite of the fact that Zacks Rank and ABR both appear on a scale from 1 to 5, they are two completely different measures.

Story Continues

The ABR is calculated solely based on brokerage recommendations and is typically displayed with decimals (example: 1.28). In contrast, the Zacks Rank is a quantitative model allowing investors to harness the power of earnings estimate revisions. It is displayed in whole numbers -- 1 to 5.

Analysts employed by brokerage firms have been and continue to be overly optimistic with their recommendations. Since the ratings issued by these analysts are more favorable than their research would support because of the vested interest of their employers, they mislead investors far more often than they guide.

In contrast, the Zacks Rank is driven by earnings estimate revisions. And near-term stock price movements are strongly correlated with trends in earnings estimate revisions, according to empirical research.

In addition, the different Zacks Rank grades are applied proportionately to all stocks for which brokerage analysts provide current-year earnings estimates. In other words, this tool always maintains a balance among its five ranks.

There is also a key difference between the ABR and Zacks Rank when it comes to freshness. When you look at the ABR, it may not be up-to-date. Nonetheless, since brokerage analysts constantly revise their earnings estimates to reflect changing business trends, and their actions get reflected in the Zacks Rank quickly enough, it is always timely in predicting future stock prices.

Is ADI Worth Investing In?

In terms of earnings estimate revisions for Analog Devices, the Zacks Consensus Estimate for the current year has increased 12.6% over the past month to $11.22.

Analysts' growing optimism over the company's earnings prospects, as indicated by strong agreement among them in revising EPS estimates higher, could be a legitimate reason for the stock to soar in the near term.

The size of the recent change in the consensus estimate, along with three other factors related to earnings estimates, has resulted in a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) for Analog Devices. You can see the complete list of today's Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) stocks here >>>>

Therefore, the Buy-equivalent ABR for Analog Devices may serve as a useful guide for investors.

news.Title: 《陸股》滬指跌1.24%、失守4000點；恆指日K連二黑
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-20 17:23:17 新聞中心 發佈

中國滬深兩市三大指數今(20)日開盤漲跌互見，滬指開低後偏弱震盪，午前的一波急漲未能延續至午後。盤面上，太陽能、鋰電池、CPO概念股爆發，電力、消費電子、半導體板塊走強；金融科技、算力租賃、AI應用題材走弱，油氣、軍工板塊跌幅靠前。整體來看，兩市個股呈普跌態勢，下跌個股超過4,700檔，滬指4000點終告失守，尾盤跌幅擴大，終場收跌1.24%，日K連二黑。

上證指數今日收於3957.05點，下跌49.50點或1.24%；深證成指收於13866.20點，下跌35.37點或0.25%；創業板指收於3352.10點，上漲43.00點或1.30%；科創50收於1318.31點，下跌20.72點或1.55%；中證A500收於5659.05點，下跌29.58點或0.52%。

以週線來看，上證指數本週跌3.38%、深證成指跌2.90%、創業板指漲1.26%、科創50跌4.03%、中證A500跌3.18%。

成交量方面，滬市今成交9,649億元(人民幣,下同)，深市成交1兆3,220億元，兩市成交額2兆2,869億元，較前一交易日量增約8%。

從盤面上來看，太陽能設備、電池、電力等板塊概念漲幅居前；通信服務、軟體發展、廚衛電器等板塊概念跌幅居前。

港股方面，受到中東局勢等影響，香港恆生指數今日開盤下跌0.64%，呈現開低走低行情。盤面上，恆生科技指數跌2.48%，大型科技股領跌，小米(1810.HK)跌約8.6%，阿里巴巴(9988.HK)跌約6.3%，美團(3690.HK)、京東(9618.HK)、百度(9888.HK)跌近2%，騰訊(0700.HK)亦跌近1%。恆指終場收於25277.32點，下跌223.26點或0.88%，日K連二黑；以週線來看，本週跌0.74%。

《陸股》滬指跌0.85%、日K連四黑；恆指日K翻黑
